What You’ll Do Create campaign concepts and key visuals from scratch, translating business or marketing briefs into strong creative directions. Design premium digital assets for eCommerce, demand generation, and campaign channels, including hero visuals, banners, promotional modules, product-focused graphics, and campaign extensions. Build photorealistic product compositions that show Lenovo products in use, in lifestyle environments, or in product-centric scenes that feel realistic and visually elevated. Ensure high-quality execution across visual details such as lighting, shadows, reflections, highlights, color accuracy, materials, perspective, and product scale. Develop creative concepts that communicate urgency, energy, technology, innovation, and dynamism across different segments and audiences. Adapt campaign ideas into multiple formats while maintaining consistency, impact, and brand alignment. Present creative concepts in English, explaining the visual direction, rationale, and narrative behind the work. Interpret stakeholder feedback clearly and iterate designs while maintaining the strength of the original concept. Maintain organized source files, clean layer structures, naming conventions, and production-ready exports. Use AI-assisted tools and automation when appropriate to improve workflow efficiency and elevate the quality of deliverables. Required Qualifications 3–5 years of experience in digital design, campaign creative, visual design, or marketing design for digital channels. Preferred educational background in Graphic Design, Visual Communication, Digital Design , or a related field. Strong portfolio demonstrating campaign visuals, digital assets, product compositions, and high-quality visual execution. Strong visual design fundamentals: composition, hierarchy, typography, color, storytelling, and attention to detail. Strong experience with photorealistic image composition and retouching , including realistic lighting, shadows, reflections, perspective, and product proportions. Ability to create concepts from scratch and translate them into polished digital campaign assets. Advanced English communication skills with the ability to present ideas clearly, build a creative narrative, and interpret stakeholder feedback effectively. Adobe Creative Cloud Proficiency Required Especially : Photoshop . Mandatory, strong working knowledge required Illustrator . Mandatory, comfortable creating, editing, and exporting vector assets Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with multiple deliverables, deadlines, and feedback cycles. Ability to follow brand guidelines while still bringing fresh, creative, and elevated visual solutions. Nice to Have Experience creating campaign systems, from a main key visual to multiple format adaptations and cutdowns. Experience with video editing or motion design using tools such as Premiere Pro, After Effects , or similar. Familiarity with Figma and/or Adobe XD for collaboration, reviewing layouts, or working with digital design files. Experience using AI tools to improve creative quality and production efficiency, such as Adobe Firefly, generative fill, image enhancement, background generation, or similar tools. Experience in designing for technology, eCommerce, performance marketing, or product-led campaigns. Additional

requirements: ***Please Note: This is a fully remote role; however, international travel is expected to collaborate with global R&D labs, business units, and pilot customers, and may reach 30 to 50% depending on project needs and incubation phase.*** About Lenovo CTO Org Lenovo CTO Org is the cutting-edge R&D and technology incubation organization of Lenovo, dedicated to advancing information technology, smart devices, and services. Our mission is to turn breakthrough innovations into real businesses, powering Lenovo's most advanced products and services and shaping the future of AI, hybrid computing, edge technologies, and beyond. About the Role This is a leadership role at the heart of Lenovo CTO Org's Tech Incubation team, accountable for the end-to-end delivery of solutions across a portfolio of concurrent incubation projects. You will build, lead, and scale a multidisciplinary delivery organization as the portfolio evolves. You will establish and scale a new Solution Delivery organization, creating the processes, operating model, and team required to support a growing incubation portfolio. This is not a single-program management role. You will operate as the central orchestrator across multiple incubation projects — dynamically prioritizing resources, resolving cross-functional bottlenecks, and ensuring alignment between engineering, product, business development, and customer stakeholders. Success requires the ability to context-switch across projects, reallocate talent based on shifting priorities, and maintain delivery momentum in a dynamic environment. Our incubation portfolio spans AI-driven products across smart manufacturing, full-stack AI agent solutions, robotics, sports technology, and other emerging domains. Each project moves at speed through the 0→1 journey — from concept validation and pilot deployment to repeatable commercial engagements. Success is measured not only by delivering successful pilots, but by helping transform incubation projects into repeatable, scalable commercial solutions ready for transition into Lenovo business units.Your role is to ensure every solution is delivered with excellence, on time, and with the quality and scalability required to transition successfully to Lenovo's business units. You bring a customer-first, results-driven mindset to everything you do. This is a US-based role operating within a global environment; international travel is expected to collaborate with R&D labs, pilot customers, and business units, and may range from approximately 30–50% depending on project needs and incubation phase. Key Responsibilities: Portfolio-Wide Solution Delivery Ownership Own the full solution delivery lifecycle across all active incubation projects — from initial architecture and resource planning through pilot deployment, customer validation, and handover to business units. Prioritize across a portfolio of projects rather than managing only one fixed program. Continuously assess project priority, risk, and strategic value to allocate delivery resources where they matter most. Define and enforce delivery standards, methodologies, and quality gates appropriate for early-stage, fast-moving environments without introducing unnecessary overhead. Establish delivery standards, deployment playbooks, and operating processes for the newly formed Solution Delivery organization. Establish clear delivery metrics and dashboards that provide real-time visibility into project health, risks, and resource utilization across the portfolio. Serve as the ultimate escalation point for delivery blockers, cross-functional conflicts, and critical customer-facing issues. Cross-Project Orchestration & Resource Optimization Dynamically prioritize and allocate delivery resources across incubation projects based on strategic priority, customer commitments, and business milestones. Manage interdependencies between concurrent projects, ensuring knowledge, reusable components, and best practices are shared effectively. Partner with Product Managers to align delivery scope with roadmap timelines and incubation stage gates. Decompose complex delivery objectives into actionable workstreams; coordinate resources across teams and geographies to drive execution toward goals. Customer & Stakeholder Alignment Own customer onboarding, adoption, satisfaction, and long-term success for deployed incubation solutions, ensuring pilot engagements deliver measurable business value and create pathways for expansion. Engage directly with strategic pilot customers and solution partners on delivery matters, ensuring commitments are met and customer satisfaction remains high. Act as the primary delivery interface to senior internal stakeholders, including CTO leadership, business unit heads, and R&D lab directors. Ensure seamless handover of successfully incubated solutions to business unit engineering and operations teams, including documentation, training, and transition planning. Help turn incubation projects into repeatable commercial solutions by capturing delivery insights and feeding them back to the solution R&D team. Technical & Operational Excellence Provide architectural and technical oversight across software, hardware, and systems integration workstreams, ensuring solution coherence and feasibility. Champion operational best practices — including risk management, change control, configuration management, and field support processes — adapted for incubation speed. Identify and mitigate delivery risks early; drive root-cause analysis and corrective actions when issues arise. Proactively review and distill solution delivery experience into actionable feedback and optimization recommendations for both software and hardware components, and relay them to the solution R&D team in a timely manner. Team Leadership & Development Build, mentor, and scale a high-performing delivery organization. Build and lead a multidisciplinary team spanning Technical Program Management, Customer Success, Solution Architectu

Coach and grow team members, fostering a culture of ownership, velocity, accountability, and continuous improvement within the delivery team. Resolve resource conflicts, mediate cross-functional disagreements, and create an environment where high-caliber talent thrives. What We Look For: Customer-first orientation: You naturally measure success by customer outcomes. Results-driven execution: You drive teams toward clear goals, hold yourself and others accountable, and deliver measurable outcomes under ambiguity and time pressure. Builder mentality: You thrive in 0→1 dynamic environments and know how to create structure from chaos without over-engineering process. Portfolio thinking: You are comfortable constantly re-prioritizing across multiple workstreams that optimize for overall portfolio success rather than any single project. Technical breadth across software architecture, embedded systems, IoT, cloud/edge infrastructure, and AI/ML solutions — sufficient to engage credibly with architects and engineers during design reviews and technical decision-making. Track record of defining and enforcing delivery standards, methodologies, and quality gates appropriate for early-stage, fast-moving environments without introducing unnecessary overhead. Demonstrated experience in repeatable, scalable commercial solutions. Excellent task decomposition skills — ability to break complex delivery objectives into clear, executable plans and milestones. Strong resource planning and coordination skills — able to forecast capacity needs, optimize team utilization, and reallocate talent dynamically across competing priorities. Strong customer-facing and executive communication skills — able to present confidently to senior leadership, navigate complex stakeholder landscapes, and represent delivery at the executive level. Conflict management — skilled at resolving disagreements, mediating between competing interests, and maintaining productive relationships under pressure. Team coaching and development — demonstrated ability to coach, mentor, and grow talent, building bench strength and leadership pipelines within the organization. Candidate is responsible for creating innovations on Motorola products, which help consumers worldwide enrich their digital lives. These are in the space of cool innovative software-hardware pairings. This role is responsible for preparing, cleaning, and analyzing datasets for AI and machine learning models, developing AI applications leveraging LLMs, chatbots, agent-based workflows, and automation technologies, and integrating AI models into production environments through scalable APIs and deployment pipelines. requirements: Summary - We are looking for Services and Solutions Training Manager for Asia Pacific region. Learning and upskilling are a key area of focus for SSG and Lenovo. As we are moving to a more services and solution driven business, sellers will need to change their approach to a consultative sales methodology. The offers are much more customizable, so sellers need to learn about the different solution building blocks and how those can deliver business outcomes to our customers. Sellers need to address new personas in their account, learn to customize their messaging and must work with more stakeholders internally, requiring usage of new process and tools. We also need to train our partner account sales reps to sell to partners and train sellers of these partners. What You will Do: Develop and implement quarterly AP training plans by sales persona, aligned with organizational needs and global strategy. Act as the single point of contact (SPOC) for AP sales management, working closely with local enablement, global training teams, and sales leaders. Set learning objectives, select methodologies, manage content and trainers, and oversee logistics, budget, and KPIs for learning initiatives. Ensure sellers and partners are equipped to sell services and solutions through consultative approaches, addressing new personas and using updated tools. Measure training effectiveness, track consumption, and continuously improve programs based on feedback and performance data. Train partner account managers and partner sellers to effectively position and sell Lenovo’s service offerings. Participate in shaping the global training strategy and innovate with new approaches, including AI-supported learning experiences. Position Requirements: Ability to design and deliver sales-focused training programs tailored to business needs and continuously improve learning experiences. Skilled in various techniques including e-learning, workshops, role plays, and instructional design. Strong understanding of how adults learn to ensure training is engaging and effective. Experience in the IT sector, with familiarity in high-tech sales or services environments. Excellent presentation skills and the ability to lead, motivate, and support trainers and trainees. Capable of managing multiple learning programs, schedules, and operational tasks efficiently. Comfortable working in fast-paced environments with flexibility, dependability, and a creative mindset. Bachelor's degree with 10+ years in training, curriculum design, and working with management and decision-makers. 5+ years using different training methodologies, incl ownership for F2F trainings (incl AI is a plus.) Executive Director, Global ESG & Regulatory Compliance Reporting to: SVP, Chief Legal & Corporate Responsibility Officer Why Work at Lenovo We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers. Lenovo is a US$83 billion global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its leadership in devices, infrastructure, and solutions to drive innovation across the technology landscape. Our continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. Description and Requirements Lenovo is seeking a strategic, business-solutions-driven leader to serve as Executive Director, Global ESG & Regulatory Compliance. This role reports to the SVP, Chief Legal & Corporate Responsibility Officer and is responsible for leading Lenovo’s global ESG reporting, sustainability regulatory compliance, and product environmental compliance programs. This role plays a critical part in enabling business growth and market access by ensuring compliance with evolving global regulatory requirements while supporting customer and investor expectations. In addition, the Executive Director oversees enterprise ISO management systems (ISO 9001, 14001, 50001) and aligns ESG and compliance programs with customer sustainability requirements, ecolabel expectations, and commercial needs. Key responsibilities include leading global ESG disclosures (including HKEX and CSRD readiness), defining enterprise ESG strategy and commitments (including net-zero and 2030 ESG KPIs), and managing external ESG ratings and surveys (e.g., CDP, EcoVadis, MSCI). The role oversees product and environmental regulatory compliance programs and governance structures, including the Product Compliance Review Board and associated tools and processes, and leads the preparation and delivery of ESG updates and materials to executive leadership and the Board of Directors. The role also owns the sys tems, tools, and digital infrastructure that enable ESG reporting and regulatory compliance, including advancing automation and AI-enabled capabilities. The Executive Director will serve as a trusted advisor to senior leadership, engaging regularly with executive teams on ESG performance, regulatory developments, and risk management. The role also supports investor engagement, customer ESG requirements, and external interactions with regulators and industry groups to anticipate and influence evolving stand ards. This position leads a global, multi-disciplinary team spanning ESG reporting, regulatory compliance, and ISO management systems, and is responsible for building scalable processes and capabilities to manage increasing regulatory complexity. Basic Qualifications - 15+ years’ e xperience in ESG, sustainability regulation, regulatory compliance, or related field - Strong knowledge of global ESG frameworks and regulations, including HKEX, CSRD, and related regimes - Demonstrated ability to translate regulatory requirements into scalable business processes and controls - Experience leading complex, global compliance or reporting programs - Proven ability to manage large, distributed, and specialized teams - Strong cross-functional leadership and executive stakeholder engagement skills Preferred Qualifications - Experience managing product and environmental regulatory compliance programs - Experience with ESG reporting, data, and compliance tools and platforms - Familiarity with ISO management systems and certification processes - Experience supporting investor, customer, and ESG ratings/survey requirements - Experience engaging with senior leadership and contributing to executive-level governance discussions - Strong strategic thinking, problem-solving, and risk management skills Desired Skills - Strategic agility and ability to operate in a fast-moving, global environment - Strong executive presence and ability to influence senior stakeholders - Ability to anticipate and proactively manage regulatory and reputational risks - Collaborative leadership style with strong team development capabilities - Clear and effective communication skills across technical and executive audience s We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. based in Chicago, Illinois. requirements: Lenovo's Infrastructure Solutions Group is innovating the future-defined data center, today. From the simplest to the most complex IT environments for all types of workloads, our servers, storage, networking and solutions help maximize the potential of data centers. What You’ll Do As a Senior Account Executive for ISG , you’ll be responsible for delivering net new account wins and expanding existing customer footprint across the Public Sector segment for ISG Education. You will drive not only revenue but profit as well to position our company for continued growth and success. You will champion the innovative power of our products to make large enterprise companies more productive, collaborative, and transformative. You will understand Lenovo’s portfolio of award-winning products and develop strategies to help new and existing Public Sector customers find and implement the best solutions. Within each account you will have responsibility for selling solutions from our entire data center portfolio, including HCI, IOT/Edge devices, IaaS, Software, Data Management/Storage systems, Professional Services, Networking and Servers. This is a client facing, retention & development and acquisition sales role requiring deep industry experience, the ability to identify, cultivate, and close net new business, and expertise working complex solution deals at the C level. We are looking for a seasoned seller with a successful and proven track record to take their career, and the territory, to the next level. The position is work from home and the successful candidate will possess extensive Public Sector data center solution technology sales experience in the territory geography. Key Responsibilities: Independently identify, develop, and close new opportunities within the territory for Education. Achieve revenue and profitability objectives for Lenovo while driving growth across multiple product sets Independently develop, implement, and execute an effective sales strategy to achieve sales goals Develop C level relationships and serve as a trusted consultant to customers Understand and adapt to Lenovo’s ongoing product and services developments Lead a cross functional team within the company to keep deals moving through the funnel Ensure that our customers receive world class sales and customer service Effectively and consistently use MS Dynamics and other reporting tools to track key sales metrics and consistently meet those metrics while delivering Operational Excellence. Basic qualifications: BA/BS degree or equivalent professional work experience. 5+ years of successful Enterprise technology sales in public sector is required. Preferred qualifications: Demonstrated track record of top performance with multi-million-dollar quota. Executive relationship within account set within target market Proven experience dealing with C-level executives Proven ability to develop strategies to penetrate and sell to large companies. Ability to travel to any or all customer sites when appropriate. Deep knowledge of customer set in designated territory “In CA, CO, CT, DC, HI IL, MD, MA, MN, NV, NJ, NY, OR, RI, WA the base salary range budgeted for this position is $130,000 – $150,000 (annually/hourly). Individuals may also be considered for bonuses and/or commissions on a 60/40 Plan. Lenovo’s various

requirements: We are looking for a Finance DT Associate Specialist to join the Finance Digital Transformation team. This role will support Lenovo’s AI-native transformation by contributing to finance AI application prototyping, workflow automation, process re-imagination, and cross-functional solution delivery. The role is designed for early-career talent with strong learning agility, hands-on problem-solving capability, technical curiosity, and interest in finance process transformation. The candidate will work closely with Finance, DT, IT, data, AI, and product teams to help translate finance business problems into digital solutions, AI-enabled prototypes, and future-state process designs. Key Responsibilities Support the design, development, testing, and iteration of AI-driven finance applications, workflow automation tools, and digital solution prototypes. Assist in API integration, data processing, basic application development, testing, troubleshooting, and documentation. Analyze finance processes, identify pain points, document current-state and future-state workflows, and support automation opportunity assessment. Translate finance user needs into structured business requirements, user stories, process maps, and solution design inputs. Learn and apply AI-native technologies such as LLM, RAG, agentic workflow, Python, FastAPI, APIs, LangChain / LangGraph, MCP, CI/CD, and GitHub Actions where relevant. Collaborate with Finance, DT, IT, data, AI, product, and change teams to support end-to-end delivery from problem framing to prototype, deployment, adoption, and benefit tracking. Contribute to project documentation, knowledge sharing, reusable templates, and continuous improvement within the Finance DT team. Requirements Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Information Systems, Data Science, Finance, Accounting, Business Analytics, Industrial Engineering, Management Science, or a related field. Early-career candidates are welcome; internship, campus project, graduation project, or personal project experience in application development, automation, AI, data analytics, or process improvement is preferred. Basic knowledge of at least one programming language, preferably Python. Basic understanding of APIs, databases, application development, data processing, automation, or system integration. Strong interest in Finance Digital Transformation, AI/ML, LLM, RAG, workflow automation, and enterprise process optimization. Ability to understand finance business scenarios, analyze process pain points, and communicate ideas clearly through documents, diagrams, tables, or presentations. Strong learning agility, logical thinking, hands-on attitude, ownership, and willingness to work in a cross-functional environment. Good communication skills in both Chinese and English. Preferred Qualifications Exposure to Python, SQL, FastAPI, Git, Power Platform, Power BI, RPA, workflow automation, cloud tools, or DevOps practices. Exposure to GenAI, LLM, RAG, chatbot, agent workflow, LangChain, LangGraph, or similar technologies. Basic understanding of finance processes such as accounting, reporting, costing, budgeting, forecasting, service finance, tax, treasury, or contract accounting. Familiarity with SAP, Oracle, ServiceNow, process mining tools, ERP processes, or finance reporting platforms is a plus. Experience in business analysis, user research, process mapping, requirement documentation, workshop support, dashboard creation, or automation solution design is a plus. This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship now or in the future. Candidates must have authorization to work in the United States without the need for sponsorship, including CPT or OPT. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Assist in API integration, data processing, basic application development, testing, troubleshooting, and documentation. Analyze finance processes, identify pain points, document current-state and future-state workflows, and support automation opportunity assessment. Translate finance user needs into structured business

requirements: About the Role We are looking for a Firmware Validation Engineer to develop and maintain a comprehensive firmware validation framework for platform firmware components, including BMC, UEFI, and SMM . This role focuses on designing validation strategies, developing automated test solutions, integrating test infrastructure with Jenkins, and driving continuous quality improvements through close collaboration with firmware development teams. Key Responsibilities Review BMC, UEFI, and SMM firmware design specifications and architecture documents to develop comprehensive validation plans and test cases. Design, develop, and maintain automated test scripts and validation frameworks for BMC, UEFI, and SMM firmware components. Develop and maintain Jenkins-based test pipelines to support both automated and on-demand firmware validation execution. Integrate validation tools and test scripts developed by different testing teams into a unified Jenkins pipeline infrastructure. Manage, maintain, and upgrade Jenkins environments to ensure the stability, scalability, and reliability of the firmware testing platform. Analyze test results, identify failures, and work closely with firmware developers to drive issue resolution and root-cause analysis. Conduct regular reviews of validation coverage, test effectiveness, and execution efficiency with firmware development teams to continuously improve product quality. Requirements Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. Experience in firmware validation, system validation, or software test automation. Proficiency in scripting or programming languages such as Python, Shell Script, PowerShell, or similar. Hands-on experience with Jenkins pipeline development, CI/CD infrastructure, and automation frameworks. Familiarity with Linux and Windows operating environments. Experience reviewing technical specifications and translating requirements into effective test cases. Additional
responsibilities: Review BMC, UEFI, and SMM firmware design specifications and architecture documents to develop comprehensive validation plans and test cases. Design, develop, and maintain automated test scripts and validation frameworks for BMC, UEFI, and SMM firmware components. Develop and maintain Jenkins-based test pipelines to support both automated and on-demand firmware validation execution. Integrate validation tools and test scripts developed by different testing teams into a unified Jenkins pipeline infrastructure. Manage, maintain, and upgrade Jenkins environments to ensure the stability, scalability, and reliability of the firmware testing platform. Analyze test results, identify failures, and work closely with firmware developers to drive issue resolution and root-cause analysis. Conduct regular reviews of validation coverage, test effectiveness, and execution efficiency with firmware development teams to continuously improve product quality.

requirements: This role is responsible for managing and optimizing Gross-to-Net processes, ensuring accurate accounting, forecasting, reporting, and control of commercial investments, rebates, incentives, pricing programs, and channel claims. This role partners closely with Finance, Sales, Operations, Business Partners, and Regional Teams to drive financial accuracy, compliance, and operational efficiency across Lenovo's commercial programs. Key Responsibilities Financial Management & Controls Manage Gross-to-Net activities including rebates, channel incentives, promotions, market development funds (MDF), pricing adjustments, and other commercial programs. Validate and approve rebate calculations, claims, accruals, and settlements. Ensure compliance with Lenovo financial policies, accounting standards, and audit requirements. Support month-end, quarter-end, and year-end closing activities related to GTN transactions. Business Partnership Act as the primary GTN finance partner for Sales, Channel Operations, Category Management, and Commercial Finance teams. Provide financial guidance regarding channel programs, incentives, and profitability. Collaborate with distributors, resellers, and internal stakeholders to resolve rebate disputes and operational discrepancies. Analysis & Reporting Conduct financial analysis on channel investments, rebate spending, profitability, and revenue impact. Prepare recurring and ad hoc reports for senior management. Analyze trends, identify risks and opportunities, and provide recommendations to improve Gross-to-Net performance. Develop dashboards and reporting tools to enhance visibility and decision-making. Forecasting & Planning Support GTN forecasting and budgeting activities. Monitor actual spending versus forecast and explain variances. Partner with regional and country finance teams to ensure accurate financial projections and accruals. Process Improvement Lead initiatives to automate and streamline GTN processes. Identify process gaps and implement solutions that improve efficiency, controls, and reporting accuracy. Participate in cross-functional projects related to systems enhancements and financial transformation. Required Qualifications Bachelor's Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Economics, or related field. 4+ years of experience in Finance, Commercial Finance, Revenue Management, Pricing, FP&A, Accounting, or Gross-to-Net functions. Strong financial analysis and problem-solving skills. Advanced Microsoft Excel skills. Experience working with ERP systems such as SAP. Advanced English proficiency (written and verbal). Strong stakeholder management and communication skills. Preferred Qualifications Experience in technology, consumer electronics, manufacturing, or multinational environments. Knowledge of revenue recognition, rebates, channel incentives, and commercial finance processes. Experience with Power BI, SQL, or other data analytics tools. We are equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as veteran and basis of disability or any other federal, state, or local protect class. If you belong to a priority group, such as people with disabilities, we encourage you to share this information if you wish. requirements: Business Development Manager (BDM) com experiência no mercado de tecnologia com o grande objetivo de expandir a presença da Lenovo no mercado de monitores no Brasil. O profissional será responsável por impulsionar o crescimento do negócio, identificar novas oportunidades e fortalecer o relacionamento com parceiros e clientes. Principais Atividades e Responsabilidades: 1. Estratégia de Vendas e Crescimento: Planejar e executar a estratégia de vendas para o portfólio de monitores, visando o aumento da receita e da participação de mercado. Identificar e desenvolver novos canais de vendas e parcerias estratégicas (revendas, varejistas, distribuidores, etc.). Analisar o mercado e a concorrência para identificar tendências, oportunidades e ameaças, e ajustar a estratégia de forma proativa. 2. Gestão de Parceiros e Relacionamento: Cultivar e fortalecer o relacionamento com parceiros-chave (distribuidores e principais parceiros), garantindo o alinhamento de metas e a execução de planos de negócio. Capacitar as equipes de vendas dos parceiros sobre os diferenciais e as novas tecnologias dos monitores Lenovo. Negociar acordos comerciais e programas de incentivo que impulsionem as vendas e a rentabilidade. 3. Apresentações e Interações com Clientes: Conduzir apresentações de produto para clientes diretos (empresas) e indiretos (através dos parceiros), destacando os benefícios e o valor dos monitores Lenovo. Representar a marca em feiras, eventos e reuniões de negócios, atuando como o porta-voz do nosso negócio de monitores. Apoiar a equipe de vendas em negociações complexas, fornecendo expertise técnica e comercial para fechar grandes negócios. 4. Gestão de Portfólio e Produtos: Atuar como o especialista do portfólio de monitores, fornecendo feedback do mercado para as equipes de produto e marketing. Definir o mix de produtos ideal para o mercado local, considerando as necessidades dos clientes e a competitividade dos preços. Lançar e promover novos produtos, garantindo que as equipes de vendas e os parceiros estejam preparados para comercializá-los com sucesso. 5. Análise de Dados e Reporte: Monitorar e analisar o desempenho de vendas (sell-in e sell-out) e os indicadores-chave de performance (KPIs). requirements: As an HR Business Partner based in Bratislava, you will be part of the HR team supporting the International Sales Organization (ISO) Finance function. requirements: Join Lenovo, a global technology leader driving innovation across AI, data center solutions, smart devices, and digital transformation. As the world's #1 provider of supercomputing systems, we help leading organizations solve complex challenges through cutting-edge HPC (High Performance Computing) and AI technologies. We're looking for an HPC System Administrator to join our Data Center Operations team in Munich, Germany. In this customer-facing role, you'll support and optimize advanced HPC and AI environments, working with Linux systems, servers, storage, networking, and data center infrastructure while ensuring secure, reliable, and high-performing platforms for leading enterprises and research organizations. What You Will Do: Act as the primary technical contact (SPOC) for customers, providing expert support across HPC (High Performance Computing) and AI platforms while building strong long-term customer relationships. Monitor and maintain critical data center infrastructure, including servers, storage, networking, operating systems, cluster management software, power, cooling, and environmental systems to ensure reliability, availability, and security. Troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, operating system, firmware, network, and infrastructure issues, coordinating with vendors and internal teams to quickly minimize downtime and service disruptions. Manage daily system administration activities, including user access management, software deployment, OS upgrades, firmware updates, security patching, defect resolution, and ongoing platform maintenance. Take ownership of incident and problem management by responding to system alerts, monitoring performance and system health, identifying root causes, opening vendor support tickets, and tracking issues through resolution. Support the installation, configuration, testing, upgrade, and optimization of HPC and AI environments, helping customers continuously improve their research computing capabilities and platform performance. Work closely with customer technology, infrastructure, security, governance, and vendor teams to deliver projects, implement controlled changes, maintain documentation, ensure compliance, and support audits and service reviews. Provide technical guidance, knowledge transfer, and training to customers and researchers, contribute to documentation and best practices, participate in projects and customer meetings, and help maximize the value of HPC resources through outstanding customer support and analytical problem-solving skills. What We Look For: Strong Linux administration knowledge, including SUSE, RHEL, and CentOS environments. Hands-on expertise with Confluent and Slurm in enterprise or HPC environments. Background in system administration, monitoring, troubleshooting, and technical support of complex infrastructure. Ability to independently perform operating system installations, software deployments, firmware updates, and system upgrades. Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to diagnose, investigate, and resolve complex technical issues. Experience supporting mission-critical systems, ensuring reliability, performance, and operational stability. Fluent English communication skills, both written and spoken, with the ability to work directly with international customers. Customer-focused mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills, capable of providing high-quality technical support and guidance. What Lenovo Can Offer You: Employee Share Purchase Plan Employee Assistance Program, e.g., for health, legal & financial consultancy Pension Plan Meal Allowance / Lunch Vouchers Internal E-learning Development Platform Available for Employees Specialized Development Trainings (based on nomination process) Employees Groups (LGBT+, WILL, etc.) Opportunity to Join/Create Employees Groups (inclusivity, well-being, sports, volunteering, charity, etc.) Job Rad (Bike Leasing) Mobile phone + 3 Sim Cards for Mobile Working Link DE: Lenovo Central (sharepoint.com) Hybrid Working Model * Lenovo’s inclusive culture is shaped by strong voices and real stories — discover our Women@Lenovo here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZwcbxS0KS-w Please apply via our online tool with your CV (including cover letter and references). The HR Partner will support the Lenovo SSG Global Geo Service Delivery organization, and its leaders in developing and implementing strategic and tactical HR initiatives. The ideal candidate will have a heightened focus on customer centricity as they support their internal candidates in supporting Lenovo's end-users. requirements: 联想目前正在招聘一位经验丰富的HRIS Analyst加入我们的团队，负责主导HR系统解决方案的设计、部署与优化等工作。该岗位将重点聚焦于用友(Yonyou) 薪资系统，提供稳定且可扩展的系统解决方案，确保薪资运营的高效性、准确性及跨区域合规性。 该角色需与跨职能团队紧密合作，推动端到端的解决方案交付，并持续推进系统优化与能力提升。 Lenovo is currently seeking an experienced HRIS analyst to join our team. requirements: About Our Team Lenovo is building Quantum, a next-generation hybrid AI platform that spans Windows, Android, and cloud. As Head of Core Intelligence, you will lead the engineering teams responsible for the “AI brain” of the system — including agentic orchestration, reasoning, fused memory, RAG retrieval, perception models, recommendation systems, and model optimization. You will own the intelligence substrate that powers every AI experience Lenovo ships across millions of devices. This is a hands-on engineering leadership role, combining deep technical understanding with the ability to operate across multiple layers of the AI stack. We recently introduced Qira, our new cross‑device Personal AI that works seamlessly across Lenovo and Motorola products. More information can be found here: https://news.lenovo.com/pressroom/press-releases/lenovo-unveils-lenovo-and-motorola-qira/
responsibilities: • Build and lead the Core Intelligence organization, owning agentic systems, orchestration, routing, tool frameworks, RAG retrieval, memory, knowledge graph, and perception signals. • Architect the intelligence layer for hybrid AI: on-device inference, cloud fallback, multi-model routing, policy enforcement, and context grounding. • Drive the development of perception-based triggers , wake-word detection logic, screenshot and audio understanding, OCR/ASR pipelines. • Own the recommendation and next-action engines, working closely with Experience Engineering to deliver contextual, proactive AI. • Lead the optimization of small and medium models (SLMs): quantization, distillation, pruning, and device-specific tuning. • Partner closely with Platform Engineering, Cloud Platform, SRE & Delivery, and Experience teams to ensure Core Intelligence is performant, reliable, and tightly integrated. • Define evaluation frameworks and quality gates for intelligence features such as retrieval accuracy, hallucination avoidance, model grounding, and agent reliability. • Set the technical direction for the intelligence roadmap, balancing innovation, prototyping, and production reliability. • Mentor senior ICs across perception, agentic systems, RAG, memory, and model optimization. • Collaborate with research, product, and design teams to bring new agent capabilities and multimodal experiences to life. Basic

requirements: HR Specialist – Solutions & Services Group (SSG) The HR Specialist will play a key role in supporting SSG's business transformation initiatives by partnering closely with the HR Leader and broader HR team. This position provides support across talent acquisition, employee development, workforce planning, organizational effectiveness, HR analytics, and employee engagement. The ideal candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, and passionate about delivering a positive employee experience while contributing to business success. Key Responsibilities Talent Acquisition & Workforce Planning • Support recruitment activities, including candidate coordination, interview scheduling, and onboarding of new hires. • Monitor and track headcount, requisitions, and hiring progress to support workforce planning efforts. • Assist with the Lenovo OHRP process and related talent planning activities. Learning & Organizational Development • Coordinate and support employee training and development programs. • Assist with organizational development initiatives designed to improve business effectiveness and team performance. • Support change management activities and help ensure smooth implementation of organizational changes. Employee Engagement & Communications • Help execute employee engagement initiatives that foster an inclusive, collaborative, and high-performing culture. • Coordinate employee events, recognition programs, and team-building activities. • Serve as a point of contact for employee questions and HR-related inquiries. • Assist in developing and distributing HR communications to employees and leaders. HR Operations & Analytics • Provide administrative support for HR programs, policies, and procedures. • Generate HR reports and dashboards to support data-driven decision-making. • Maintain and analyze HR metrics related to hiring, retention, engagement, and workforce trends. • Ensure the accuracy and integrity of HR data and records. Basic Qualifications • Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field. • 0–3 years of experience in Human Resources, Talent Acquisition, or a related area. Preferred Qualifications • Foundational knowledge of HR principles, practices, and employment processes. • Strong interpersonal, communication, and relationship-building skills. • Demonstrated ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion. • Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications and AI-enabled productivity tools. • Strong analytical, organizational, and time-management skills. • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, evolving environment. • Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and committed to delivering high-quality work. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: 岗位职责 1 、 IT 运维服务项目全流程管理 负责企业级 IT 基础设施、云资源、系统运维、驻场外包、技术支持类服务项目的整体落地，涵盖需求对接、方案落地、进度管控、风险把控、资源协调、验收交付、客户关系维护，保障项目按期、保质、平稳交付。 2 、项目团队与交付质量管理 统筹运维交付团队、外包技术人员的工作排期、任务分配、过程督导，建立标准化运维交付流程，持续优化服务 SLA 、交付质量、客户满意度，输出周报、月报、复盘报告。 3 、客户沟通与需求管理 深度对接甲方客户，独立完成需求拆解、问题答疑、变更管控、争议协调，提前识别运 维项目 风险（人员变动、系统故障、需求蔓延、延期风险），制定应急预案并落地闭环。 4 、 AI 产品 / AI 应用落地项目推进（核心能力） 具备 AI 领域 FDE 前端开发思维与落地经验，可独立理解 AI 大模型应用、 AI Agent 、智能运维平台、 AI 可视化后台的前端架构逻辑，配合研发团队推进 AI 运维系统、 AI 办公工具、智能监控平台的项目落地、需求评审、版本迭代与上线交付。 5 、项目成本、进度、文档与回款管理 负责项目成本管控、预算把控、过程资料归档、项目结项、对账回款，完善项目知识库、运维标准化文档，沉淀可复用的项目交付流程。 岗位要求 1 、本科及以上学历， 2-5 年 IT 服务运维、系统集成、技术服务类项目管理经验，完整经历过至少 2 个 以上中大型运维服务全周期项目交付，熟悉 ITIL 、 SLA 运维服务体系。 2 、核心要求：具备 AI 领域 FDE 前端开发项目背景 拥有 AI 项目前端落地实战经验， 了解前后端开发的主流技术架构 ， 了解 AI 应用后台、智能运维平台、 AI 对话系统、数据可视化管理平台的开发逻辑，能够看懂前端技术需求、高效对接研发团队、把控 AI 前端模块交付质量与进度。 3 、技术型项目管理底色：熟悉服务器运维、云服务、企业日常 IT 运 维工作 内容，可精准评估技术工作量、识别技术风险，无缝衔接研发、测试、运维、客户多方协同。 4 、逻辑清晰、擅长需求拆解与项目统筹，风险预判和问题解决能力强，抗压性高，可独立全权负责项目全生命周期交付与客户对接。 5 、具备优秀的书面及口头汇报能力，熟练使用 Project 、 Office 套件、飞书 / 钉钉协作工具，持有 PMP 、 ITIL 证书优先。 6 、有 AI 智能运维、 AI Agent 平台落地、企业数字化 AI 改造项目经验者优先。 Additional

requirements: Role: The IT Product Analysis Specialist is responsible for bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions by gathering and analyzing requirements, translating them into clear technical specifications, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality products. The role involves working closely with business stakeholders, developers, architects, and QA teams to design scalable solutions, support system integrations, validate product functionality through testing, and drive continuous process improvements. The specialist ensures that technology solutions align with business objectives while enhancing operational efficiency and delivering an excellent user experience. Job Description Requirement Analysis & Documentation: Gather, analyze, and document detailed business and technical requirements. Translate business requirements into technical specifications and user stories. Ensure alignment between business goals and technical solutions. Technical Solution Design: Collaborate with development and architecture teams to design scalable and efficient technical solutions. Identify technical constraints, risks, and opportunities during solution design. Stakeholder Collaboration: Serve as a liaison between business users, development teams, and project managers. Effectively communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders. Data Analysis & Integration: Analyze and interpret data to provide actionable insights. Support integration of systems, APIs, and data flows across platforms. Testing & Quality Assurance: Support QA processes, including reviewing test plans and validating technical requirements. Perform user acceptance testing (UAT) and ensure the delivered product meets business expectations. Process Improvement: Evaluate existing processes and identify areas for technical and operational improvement. Recommend enhancements and automation opportunities. Key Technical Competencies Ability to convert business requirements into technical solutions. Strong SQL and data analysis capabilities. Experience analyzing APIs and system integrations. Ability to create process flows and technical documentation. Experience working in Agile product teams. Strong understanding of UAT, defect management, and quality assurance. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Experience: 6+ years of relevant experience Education: Bachelor’s degree, preferably specialization in Artificial Intelligence. Additional

requirements: The IT Site Operations Engineer is responsible for ensuring stability, continuity, and continuous improvement of IT operations supporting manufacturing environments at the site/GEO level. This role evolves beyond traditional infrastructure support by integrating AI‑enabled operations (AgenticOps), automation, and business alignment, acting as a key enabler of resilient and intelligent IT services. This role represents the evolution from traditional infrastructure support to a next-generation IT operations model, where engineers are empowered by AI, operate within an AgenticOps framework, and actively contribute to business value through technology. Responsibilities IT Operations & Manufacturing Support Ensure high availability of critical manufacturing applications, systems, and infrastructure. Provide operational support aligned with defined SLA targets. Manage and resolve incidents, service requests, and outages with a focus on minimizing production impact. Execute and maintain runbooks, SOPs, and operational standards. AgenticOps & AI Enabled Operations Leverage AI tools and agents (AI Buddy / AgenticOps) to improve troubleshooting, RCA, and operational efficiency. Participate in the adoption of automation and AI driven processes for repetitive, high volume support tasks. Ensure human in the loop governance, maintaining full control over AI assisted actions in critical environments. Contribute to continuous improvement of knowledge bases used by AI agents. Infrastructure & Application Ownership Oversee the health and performance of IT infrastructure, networks, and site applications. Support system maintenance, upgrades, and lifecycle management. Collaborate with global teams to ensure standardization and alignment across sites (MX, BR, HU, US). Business Analysis & Demand Alignment Act as a Business Analyst partner to manufacturing and operational stakeholders. Understand business processes and translate needs into IT requirements and solutions. Identify opportunities for process optimization, automation, and AI adoption. Support initiatives and projects that enhance site productivity and efficiency. Service Experience & Stakeholder Engagement Provide high quality IT support to business end users, ensuring a strong service experience. Act as the IT ambassador for the local site, bridging global IT capabilities with local business needs. Communicate effectively with users, leadership, and cross functional teams. Continuous Improvement & Compliance Drive root cause analysis (RCA) and reduction of recurring incidents. Ensure alignment with security, compliance, and audit requirements. Contribute to operational excellence through standardization, documentation, and best practices. Education & Experience Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Engineering, Computer Science, or related field. 3–7+ years of experience in IT operations, infrastructure support, or site support roles. Experience in manufacturing IT environments is highly preferred. Exposure to or experience with AI tools, automation, or digital transformation initiatives is a strong plus. Skills Technical Strong knowledge of IT infrastructure and operations (networks, servers, endpoints, and site-critical systems). Experience supporting manufacturing or production environments (high availability, low tolerance for downtime). Solid understanding of incidents, problems, and change management processes (ITIL-based). Familiarity with monitoring tools, log analysis, and troubleshooting methodologies. Basic to intermediate knowledge of automation and scripting (e.g., PowerShell, Python, or similar). AI & Digital Operations (AgenticOps Readiness) Understanding of AI‑enabled operations concepts (AI agents, automation, AgenticOps). Ability to leverage AI tools (e.g., copilots / AI assistants) to improve troubleshooting, RCA, and productivity. Strong analytical thinking for identifying repetitive processes suitable for automation. Knowledge of data quality and knowledge management practices to support AI-driven operations. Awareness of AI governance, security, and human‑in‑the‑loop principles. Business & Analytical Skills Ability to act as a Business Analyst partner, understanding manufacturing processes and business needs. Strong skills in problem analysis, root cause identification, and solution definition. Capability to translate business requirements into IT solutions or improvements. Experience identifying process optimization and automation opportunities. Soft Skills & Leadership Attributes Strong customer service mindset with focus on end-user experience. Excellent communication skills (technical and non-technical audiences, bilingual preferred). Proactive and ownership-driven mindset in incident resolution and operational improvements. Ability to work in cross-functional and global teams (MX, BR, HU, US). Strong collaboration and stakeholder engagement capabilities. Virtualization (VMware/Hyper-V), and storage solutions Cyber-security basic level. intermediate is a plus. High motivation, customer focus, and ability to manage multiple priorities. requirements: This position is with an Infrastructure Management team. The chosen candidate will have primary responsibilities across both Windows and Linux systems and applications, with overall functionality of the environment being a shared responsibility across all members of the infrastructure team. The environment is not static, and duties assigned will change as the environment grows or evolves; all team members are expected to be flexible in both skills and schedule. The infrastructure team manages, maintains, and supports an isolated environment which facilitates delivery of several Appliance Management and Systems Management Services to customers. This position requires significant hands-on experience with Windows Server platforms, Active Directory, systems management, and security configurations, as well as strong working knowledge of Linux-based systems in production environments. The candidate will be responsible for building, configuring, optimizing, patching, maintaining, and securing both Windows and Linux systems. This includes applying security fixes and remediation plans, documenting procedures, diagramming systems, responding to alerts and incidents through the ticketing system, and maintaining proper configuration and operational records. On the Windows side, the role includes administration and support of technologies such as Active Directory, Microsoft SQL Server, Exchange, and SharePoint. On the Linux side, the role includes administration of enterprise Linux distributions (such as Red Hat, Rocky, or Ubuntu), managing system services, performance tuning, patching, log analysis, storage and filesystem management, and supporting applications hosted on Linux platforms. In addition, the candidate will be required to learn and support applications running on both Windows and Linux systems in order to maintain operational stability. Current applications include SolarWinds Orion, Password Manager Pro, AD Manager, and AD Audit, as well as Linux-hosted services supporting monitoring, automation, or infrastructure tooling. Critical Skills/Qualifications: Advanced Windows Server administration skills with certification and 5+ years of experience, or 10+ years of equivalent relevant work experience. Strong Linux system administration experience, including command-line troubleshooting, service management, patching, and security hardening in production environments. Experience supporting Microsoft SQL (including clustered environments), Exchange email systems, or SharePoint. Solid troubleshooting, documentation, and operational support skills across mixed OS environments. Language Skills – fluent in spoken and written English. Bonus: Experience administering mixed Windows and Linux enterprise environments. Experience with email systems, monitoring platforms, or infrastructure management tools. Experience with security remediation, vulnerability management, and system hardening. Familiarity with automation or scripting (PowerShell, Bash, or similar). Additional

requirements: As part of Lenovo’s growth strategy, we are focusing in the DACH region on customers in the mid-market segment (approx. 200–1,000 employees) and continuing to expand our team. To support this, we are looking for a communicative, sales-driven professional who will support our customers throughout the entire sales cycle, with a strong interest in data center and solutions topics (servers, storage, infrastructure, and solution approaches), ideally complemented by initial experience in this area. In this inside sales role, you are the main point of contact for a defined customer portfolio and/or sales territory and take ownership of opportunity management from prospecting through to closing. You work closely with field sales, product specialists, and technical experts, and you build long-term relationships up to C-level. Lenovo supports you with structured onboarding, product training, and sales coaching. Develop existing accounts with the goal of profitably increasing revenue and margin and sustainably strengthening customer satisfaction. Create, calculate, and follow up on quotes, including pricing, through to project completion. Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders at different levels. Very good MS Office skills. Very good German and good English, both written and spoken. Resilience and enjoyment of working in a dynamic, demanding environment. What we offer Comprehensive onboarding and continuous development (including product training and sales coaching). requirements: Inside Sales Representative (Partner Focused) ■募集の背景 レノボ・ジャパンはスマートフォンからクラウドまで、幅広い製品を提供しています。法人向け事業においては、お客様のＩＴＣ環境の構築、 DX （デジタルトランスフォーメーション）をご支援することで、お客様の成功と豊かな社会の実現を目指しています。現在は VUCA と呼ばれる状況ではありますが、私たちは法人向け事業の更なる拡大のため営業組織を強化します。 ■組織について レノボ・ジャパン コマーシャル事業本部 広域営業事業部 中小企業から大企業まで、幅広いお客様を担当しています。チームワークを重んじ、メンバー同士でノウハウを共有、全員で与えられたミッションをクリアすべく日々業務に取り組んでいます。ハイブリッドワークを取り入れ、柔軟な働き方も可能です。 Manager （上司） : Japan Commercial SMB MM Inside Sales Manager Number of Peers （同僚の人数） : 15 ■職務内容 インサイドセールスとして電話、メール、 Web 面談等を効果的に活用してお客様にハードウェア、ソフトウェア、サービスなどを提案し、成約に結び付けていきます。外勤営業との連携は不可欠ですが、外勤営業のサポート役ではなく、お客様のフロントに立って担当セールスチームのハブとなる重要な仕事です。常に最先端の IT 技術に触れることができるので、営業力だけでなく、 IT の知識も自然と身に付きます。 ・お客様ニーズを分析・理解し、意思決定プロセスを把握することで、お客様にとって魅力的な提案をおこなう ・外勤営業、パートナーセールス、製品別市場開発マネージャー、ビジネスパートナーの担当者など社内外関係者と密に連携し、ビジネスチャンスを成功に導く ・個人の営業目標を達成することにより、会社の目標や目的を達成させる ・セールスパイプラインの的確な状況判断により、営業活動および収益達成を正確に予測する ・見込み客への提案からクロージング、製品・サービス導入後のフォロー、次回購入に向けたお客様との関係構築まで包括的にアカウントマネジメントをおこなう ・社内マネジメント層と連携し、お客様トップマネジメントとのリレーションシップ構築を実現する ■ Key Interaction with: ・外勤営業 ・パートナーセールス（ビジネスパートナーを担当する営業） ・ビジネス・ディベロップメント・マネージャー（製品別市場開発マネージャー） ・製品マネージャー ・カスタマーサポート ・オペレーション ・マーケティング ・ファイナンス ・需給管理 ■キャリアパス ・外勤営業 ・インサイドセールスマネージャー ・マーケティング ・製品担当 など 私たちの期待する候補者は、日々アップデートされる専門知識を身につけ、お客様の成功に貢献する情熱をもったプロフェッショナルです。このポジションには業績に応じた競争力のある報酬パッケージ、包括的な福利厚生、専門的な成長と開発の機会を提供します。私たちと一緒にビジネス拡大の旅に出ませんか。興味がある方は、履歴書とカバーレターを人事チームに提出してください。あなたを歓迎できる日を楽しみにしています。 必須となるご経験 3 年以上の PC または IT 業界での営業経験 社会人経験 10 年くらいまで 求められるスキル等 1. コミュニケーションスキル : 営業担当者は製品情報を効果的に伝え、潜在的なお客様を説得する必要があります。電話がメインとなるので優れた口頭および書面によるコミュニケーションスキルが重要です。 2. 交渉スキル : 相互に有益な合意に達するためにお客様・ビジネスパートナーと交渉することがよくあります。高い交渉力は必須のスキルです。 3. 関係構築スキル : お客様との関係を構築し維持することは、インサイドセールスとして不可欠です。営業担当者は、お客様との信頼関係を築き、良好な関係を維持して、リピート ビジネスを促進する必要があります。新しい見込み客を継続的に探し、潜在的なお客様をフォローアップし、成約につなげる意欲が求められます。 4. 製品知識 : 営業担当者は、機能、利点、競争上の優位性など、製品やサービスについて深く理解する必要があります。この知識により、お客様の期待を超える提案が可能となります。 5. 適応性 : 営業担当者は順応性があり、さまざまなお客様や状況に応じてアプローチをすばやく調整できる必要があります。 6. リスニング スキル : 効果的なリスニングスキルは、お客様のニーズを理解し、機会を特定し、懸念に対処するために重要です。営業担当者は、お客様からのフィードバックに積極的に耳を傾け、適切に対応できる必要があります。 7. チームワーク : 営業担当者はお客様に最新・最適な製品サービスを提供するため、チームの一員として活動し他の部門と協力します。チームの目標を達成することがゴールです。 8. 時間管理スキル : お客様の期待に応えるためには、スピーディーな対応・活動が必須です。効果的かつ効率的に時間の優先順位を付けることが重要です。 9. 自発的 : チームの成功のため、自発的・積極的なアクションが求められます。業務範囲外の活動への積極参加は称賛の対象となります。 Additional

We are seeking a passionate and detail-oriented Integration Associate Engineer to join our dynamic technology team. As a key member, you will bridge system connectivity, product development, and AI innovation, supporting seamless data flow between software systems while contributing to product iteration and intelligent solution exploration. This role is ideal for graduates with a strong technical foundation, hands-on SQL experience, and a keen interest in middleware, ETL tools and AI technologies. You will work on cutting-edge projects, gain exposure to industry-leading technologies, and grow into a versatile engineer with expertise in integration, product collaboration, and intelligent innovation. Responsibilities: Assist in designing integration solutions, including defining interface specifications and data flow logic to ensure smooth interoperability between modules/systems. Develop and debug integration interfaces based on requirements, participate in solution design and development and ensure interface functionality, performance, and stability. Communicate closely with other teams to resolve integration issues and drive project progress. Leverage self-developed platforms or ETL tools (if experienced) to optimize data integration workflows and enhance system efficiency. Collaborate with front-end developers, product managers, and other stakeholders to address issues and requirement changes during system development. Continuously learn and research new technologies. Explore the application of AI technologies (e.g., data analysis automation, intelligent workflow optimization) in integration scenarios to improve efficiency. Follow the team's development standards and processes to ensure code readability and maintainability. Requirements: Graduate with a graduation date between September 2024 and August 2026, majoring in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science or related fields (domestic or international). Proficient in Java object-oriented programming, multithreading, networking, and familiar with technologies such as microservices, message queues, and MySQL. Solid foundational knowledge, familiarity with common data structures and algorithms, and a deep understanding of computer networks, operating systems, etc. Additional

Strong commercial acumen , with the ability to craft and present compelling proposals. Collaborative mindset , able to thrive in a team-driven and fast-paced environment. Based in or able to commute to Farnborough , with flexibility for hybrid working (3 days in-office). experience: · 具有2年以上知名PE/VC机构、产业投资部门或3年以上头部投行相关岗位工作经验，且在半导体、AI、机器人、先进制造等领域有直接投资或深度行业研究经历
requirements: 岗位职责： 1. 项目挖掘与筛选 ：围绕公司投资战略，重点聚焦半导体、人工智能、机器人/具身智能、先进制造等硬科技领域，主动开拓项目渠道，筛选优质早期及成长期投资标的，建立并维护项目来源网络。 2. 尽职调查与投资分析 ：主导或参与项目的业务、财务、法律及技术尽调，组织内外部资源完成深度调研；独立撰写投资建议书、立项报告、投资决策报告等核心文件，对项目进行估值分析和风险判断。 3. 交易执行与谈判 ：参与投资交易结构设计、关键条款谈判及协议签署，协调各方推动项目顺利完成交割。 4. 投后管理与赋能 ：负责对被投企业的日常投后管理工作，跟踪企业经营动态，定期撰写投后报告；依托联想集团产业生态，为被投企业提供技术、市场、供应链等增值赋能支持。 5. 行业研究与战略支持 ：持续跟踪所负责细分行业的动态趋势，定期输出行业研究报告、竞争格局分析及投资策略建议，为公司投资决策提供数据与逻辑支撑。 6. 其他任务 ：完成上级交办的其他相关工作。 岗位要求： 1.教育背景：硕士及以上学历，理工科专业背景（计算机、人工智能、电气工程、自动化、数学、物理等优先）；同时具备金融、经济或管理类复合学历者优先。 2.工作经验： · 具有2年以上知名PE/VC机构、产业投资部门或3年以上头部投行相关岗位工作经验，且在半导体、AI、机器人、先进制造等领域有直接投资或深度行业研究经历； · 有上述领域研发、产品经理或技术管理经验者优先考虑（可适当放宽投资年限）。 3.专业能力： · 对所负责行业有深入理解，熟悉技术演进路径、产业链格局及商业模式演变，具备独立判断项目技术壁垒和市场前景的能力； · 具备扎实的财务建模、估值分析和风险控制能力，能独立完成项目尽调和投资报告。 4.综合素质： · 热爱科技创新，对新兴技术趋势和商业新模式保持高度敏感与好奇心； · 拥有较强的学习能力、逻辑思维和沟通协调能力，善于与创业团队、行业专家及内部团队高效协作； · 具备良好的职业操守和抗压能力，能够适应快节奏的工作环境； · 具备使用英语作为工作语言的能力。

requirements: Job Responsibilities: Design and develop platform business functions based on Product Requirement Documents (PRD). Implement scalable microservices using Java and Spring ecosystems . Ensure high-performance, reliable backend services that meet business requirements. Design efficient business models and data structures based on platform requirements. Collaborate with database teams to optimize PostgreSQL schema design. Implement best practices for data integrity and system scalability. Diagnose and resolve platform issues including service exceptions and interface failures. Ensure platform stability and reliability through proactive maintenance. Analyze and optimize platform performance through code improvements and database tuning. Configure and optimize middleware components including RabbitMQ and Redis. . Familiarity with REST/ gRPC integration patterns for AI services and GPU-accelerated environments. Exposure to NVIDIA AI ecosystem technologies (NIMs, Triton Inference Server, Metropolis, DeepStream ) is a plus. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree or above in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related fields. Strong proficiency in Java programming including multithreading, collections, and JVM optimization. Extensive experience with Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, and microservices architecture. Expertise in middleware technologies: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, Redis, Docker, and Kubernetes. Experience with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices and designing real-time event-driven architectures and streaming data pipelines. Familiarity with handling telemetry, sensor, IoT, or operational event data at scale. Experience integrating AI/ML services, inference APIs, or LLM-based workflows into enterprise applications Experience integrating enterprise platforms, third-party APIs, and operational systems into unified backend services. Experience working with Kafka, MQTT, or real-time messaging/event platforms , video metadata pipelines, computer vision systems, or AI-generated event data is a plus. requirements: Key Responsibilities 1. Regional Portfolio Strategy & Roadmap Define and continuously refine the LATAM portfolio strategy for Visual Products, aligned with business goals, customer needs, and market dynamics. Build and manage a portfolio roadmap (3–12 months), balancing growth opportunities, profitability, and operational feasibility across countries and channels. Drive portfolio governance and prioritization: what to scale, what to simplify, and where to invest to win. 2. Market Intelligence & Category Insights Lead competitive benchmarking, trend analysis, and market segmentation to identify opportunities, threats, and whitespace across LATAM. Consolidate Voice of Customer (VOC) insights from key accounts and channels, converting findings into portfolio requirements and positioning recommendations. 3. Go‑to‑Market (GTM) Leadership & Commercial Enablement Partner with Sales and Marketing to define and execute GTM strategy for priority segments and countries (launch plans, messaging, channel plays, and enablement assets). Ensure sales readiness across geos: value proposition, competitive battlecards, portfolio storytelling, and product training for internal teams and partners. Support complex and strategic opportunities by providing portfolio guidance and product expertise (while Sales retains quota ownership). 4. Pricing, Profitability & Portfolio Mix Management Own regional recommendations on pricing corridors, positioning, and portfolio mix , in close collaboration with Finance and Sales, to protect margin and increase competitiveness. Propose programs and actions to improve revenue quality and efficiency (e.g., portfolio rationalization, attach opportunities, promotion guardrails). 5. Launch & Lifecycle Management Lead new product introductions (NPI) and portfolio refreshes across LATAM, ensuring operational and commercial readiness. Manage lifecycle actions across the region, including end‑of‑life planning, substitution strategies, and portfolio simplification to improve execution and reduce complexity. 6. Performance Management & Executive Reporting Monitor and analyze portfolio KPIs such as sell‑in/sell‑out , market share (where available), ASP, margin, inventory health, and channel performance. Deliver regular business reviews to leadership with clear performance insights, risks, and corrective action plans. 7. Supply & Demand Alignment (Regional) Collaborate with Supply Chain to provide demand signals and portfolio priorities to improve forecast accuracy, allocation decisions, and product availability across LATAM. Proactively identify supply risks and coordinate mitigation actions with key stakeholders. Additional

requirements: We are looking for a strong individual contributor who thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment and can navigate ambiguity with confidence. As the hiring manager and broader 4P team are based outside Egypt, success in this role requires a self-motivated professional who can work independently, build strong stakeholder relationships, drive execution, and consistently deliver business and financial results. In this role, you will: Act as a co-P&L owner for the Consumer business across the LENA region. Develop and execute product strategies across PCs, Tablets, Visuals, and Consumer Accessories. Drive business performance and support delivery of financial targets. Serve as the product champion for the region, ensuring sales teams clearly understand product positioning and strategy. Influence stakeholders and sales teams to drive execution and business results. Work closely with headquarters and regional teams to align on strategic and financial objectives. Proactively identify opportunities, risks, and solutions to support business growth. Utilize Microsoft tools, particularly Excel and PowerPoint, for business analysis, reporting, and decision-making. requirements: At Lenovo, we're looking for an inspiring sales leader who is passionate about developing people, building strong customer relationships, and driving business growth. As our Sales Manager – Large Enterprise , you'll lead a team of Client Managers responsible for some of Lenovo's most strategic enterprise customers across the UK and Ireland. This is an exciting opportunity to shape sales strategy, foster a high-performing and collaborative team culture, and help customers unlock value through Lenovo's innovative portfolio of devices, infrastructure, services, and solutions. You'll work closely with cross-functional teams and senior stakeholders to deliver exceptional customer experiences while achieving ambitious business goals. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, enjoy coaching others to succeed, and are motivated by delivering meaningful business impact, we'd love to hear from you. What You'll Do Lead, coach and develop a team of Large Enterprise Client Managers, creating an inclusive, high-performing culture focused on collaboration, accountability and continuous growth. Drive sales performance by delivering against revenue, profitability, pipeline and customer satisfaction objectives. Establish a disciplined sales operating rhythm through regular pipeline reviews, opportunity coaching, forecast management and account planning. Support your team in identifying new business opportunities, expanding existing customer relationships and increasing share of wallet across Lenovo's portfolio. Build trusted relationships with senior customer stakeholders, including executive decision-makers, to strengthen Lenovo's strategic position. Collaborate with technical, services, marketing, partner and commercial teams to deliver integrated customer solutions and win complex enterprise opportunities. Use data and insights to monitor performance, identify trends, mitigate risks and improve forecasting accuracy and sales execution. Champion operational excellence by embedding best practices across sales processes, opportunity qualification and customer engagement. Inspire innovation, continuous improvement and a customer-first mindset across the team. What We're Looking For Proven experience leading successful enterprise technology sales teams. Strong track record of delivering revenue growth through coaching, strategic account management and sales execution. Experience managing complex enterprise sales cycles involving multiple stakeholders and long-term customer relationships. Excellent knowledge of pipeline management, forecasting, opportunity qualification and account planning methodologies. Commercially minded with the ability to identify growth opportunities and drive cross-sell and upsell strategies. Strong leadership, coaching and people development skills with the ability to motivate diverse teams. Confidence engaging with senior business leaders, including C-suite stakeholders. Analytical mindset with the ability to use data to inform decisions, improve performance and drive business outcomes. Excellent communication, collaboration and influencing skills, with a passion for delivering outstanding customer experiences. What Lenovo Can Offer You Holiday purchase scheme Private medical insurance Income protection Attractive pension scheme Positive work-life balance Learning and development opportunities Life insurance Employee discounts on Lenovo and Motorola products Lifestyle discounts Cycle to Work scheme MyGymDiscounts Mortgage advice and support Employee referral bonus Free onsite parking We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: 岗位职责/Job Responsibilities： 1. 产品规划与落地 · 负责公司核心 AI 产品的整体规划与路线图，制定长期愿景与年度关键目标。 · 基于业务诉求、用户需求及模型能力（LLM、RAG、Agent、多模态等），定义产品策略并推动落地。 · 推动产品从 0→1 / 1→N 的规模化建设，对用户增长、体验指标和业务价值负责。 · 定期向管理层进行结构化汇报（策略、版本节奏、风险、进展与数据成果）。 2. 项目管理与研发节奏推进 · 对开发过程进行持续跟踪与节奏控制，包括里程碑状态、交付风险、资源匹配度和上线准备情况。。 · 制定清晰的里程碑与资源安排，识别并管理关键风险，确保复杂 AI 项目按期高质量交付。 · 高效协同算法、研发、数据、设计、运营等跨团队，推动共识与决策落地。 3. 产品 + 运营一体化负责制 · 建立智能体验指标体系（如准确率、召回、生成质量、满意度等），推动基于数据的持续优化。 · 联合运营制定增长策略、用户教育、内容/知识管理方案，提升使用深度和价值实现。 · 通过实验、数据分析与用户洞察驱动产品迭代。 4. 团队管理与外部沟通 · 指导产品团队进行目标拆解、方法论建设与输出质量管理。 · 面向管理层、客户、合作伙伴等场景进行产品宣讲、价值阐述与外部分享。 · 推动组织内 AI 产品方法论、流程规范与最佳实践的沉淀。 岗位要求/Job Requirements： 1. 专业经验 • 7年以上产品经验，3 年以上 AI 产品经验。 • 有规模化 AI 产品经验。 2. 核心能力 • 深刻理解大模型能力（问答、生成、智能体、RAG、多模态等）及其产品化路径。 • 具备成熟的项目管理能力，能驱动复杂多人、多团队项目稳定推进。 • 具备强向上管理能力——能对策略、节奏、风险、资源做出清晰结构化汇报。 • 具备数据驱动能力，能基于指标体系、用户行为与模型表现进行决策。 3. 沟通与协作 • 逻辑清晰、沟通顺畅，擅长跨部门协调与复杂决策推动。 • 英语流利 Additional
