requirements: The Learning & OD Coordinator supports the execution and operational delivery of Learning & Development and Organization Development activities. This role applies established L&D and HR processes to support pre‑hire and post‑hire learning programs, maintains accurate learning data, produces training‑related reports, and assists with program implementation. The role operates within defined policies and procedures and escalates more complex issues as needed. Learning & OD Practice Perform L&D activities supporting pre‑hire and post‑hire processes. Support the implementation of L&D and HR programs, courses, and learning initiatives. Track, analyze, and report data related to L&D programs, courses, and projects. Maintain learning records and documentation in learning systems. L&D Operations Complete basic and repetitive operational tasks related to L&D. Work under guidance of experienced team members to learn L&D operational policies, programs, and processes. Assist in resolving issues raised by managers, employees, HR partners, vendors, or team members. Produce training‑related reports and basic analysis. Support the implementation and maintenance of L&D programs as required. Stakeholder Interaction Primarily interfaces with internal L&D, HR, and business team members and managers. May interact with Finance or other internal teams to complete tasks or exchange information. Problem Solving & Decision Making Uses established policies, procedures, and processes to resolve issues or answer questions. Escalates complex issues to more experienced specialists. Basic Qualifications Associate or Bachelor’s degree (or relevant coursework) in Human Resources, Learning & Development, Organizational Development, Adult Education, Business, Psychology, or a related field. Early experience (internship, rotation, project work, or 0–2 years role) supporting: Training coordination or delivery HR or people programs Data tracking, reporting, or operations Ability to work 3:2 Hybrid Schedule in Morrisville, NC (3 days in office and 2 flex). Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. for any employer. We are unable to sponsor work visas for this position. Preferred Qualifications Coursework, certification, or demonstrated interest in Learning & Development, Talent Development, or Organizational Effectiveness. Exposure to or foundational knowledge of adult learning principles , instructional design, or facilitation (formal or on‑the‑job). Comfort using learning systems, HR systems, or basic data/reporting tools (e.g., LMS, Excel, dashboards). Strong attention to detail and ability to follow defined processes while learning broader L&D practices. Clear written and verbal communication skills and willingness to engage with internal stakeholders. Curiosity, learning agility, and interest in building a career in corporate Learning & Development. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

At Lenovo, we believe in smarter technology for all. As a Local Account Manager within our Global Accounts organization, you will play a key role in supporting some of the world's largest multinational customers by driving business growth across their French operations. Acting as the local face of Lenovo, you will build trusted customer relationships, execute global account strategies at a local level, and identify new business opportunities across our Intelligent Devices, Infrastructure Solutions, and Services portfolios. This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced enterprise sales professional who enjoys working in a dynamic, international environment and thrives on delivering value to strategic customers. What You Will Do Manage and grow a portfolio of global enterprise accounts in France, acting as the primary local contact for customers whose headquarters are located outside of France. Build strong relationships with key stakeholders, from operational teams to senior executives, and become a trusted business partner. Collaborate closely with Global and Regional Account Managers to execute account strategies, identify growth opportunities, and drive business across Lenovo's full portfolio, including Intelligent Devices Group (IDG), Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), and Solutions & Services Group (SSG). Develop customer proposals, negotiate commercial opportunities, and support the complete sales cycle from opportunity creation through order fulfillment and post-sales support. Partner with internal teams including Sales Support, Product Specialists, Supply Chain, Pricing, and Business Partners to deliver an outstanding customer experience. Manage demand planning, sales forecasting, CRM updates, and pipeline reporting to ensure accurate business planning and execution. Support customers in resolving commercial and operational challenges while proactively identifying opportunities to strengthen long-term partnerships. Stay informed about customer business priorities, governance structures, and decision-making processes to tailor solutions that create measurable business value. Contribute to Lenovo's continued growth by driving profitable sales and delivering an exceptional customer experience. What We're Looking For Proven experience in enterprise B2B sales, account management, or global account management within the IT industry. Experience selling Data Center, Infrastructure, Enterprise Solutions, or related technology solutions is strongly preferred. Strong understanding of enterprise IT environments, solution selling, and complex sales cycles. Demonstrated ability to build relationships with senior decision-makers, including C-level executives. Experience managing strategic customer accounts and working with multinational organizations. Strong commercial mindset with excellent negotiation, forecasting, and opportunity management skills. Highly collaborative, proactive, and self-driven, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Excellent communication and presentation skills in both French and English. Bachelor's degree in Business, Information Technology, or a related field is an advantage but not essential. What Lenovo Can Offer You Medical Insurance Pension / Retirement Plan Employee Referral Bonus Lenovo and Motorola Product Discounts Internal E-learning Development Platform available for all employees Employee Assistance Program, including health, legal, and financial consultancy 75% reimbursement of public transportation costs (Paris area – Rueil-Malmaison) Profit Share Scheme We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Role Overview As MEA Desktop 4P Manager for the Commercial business, you will lead Lenovo’s desktop portfolio strategy across the Middle East and Africa. The role is responsible for defining and executing the right product, pricing, promotion, and channel plans to drive revenue growth, improve profitability, strengthen market share, and reinforce Lenovo’s competitive position across countries and territories. Key Responsibilities 4P Strategy and Business Planning: Define and execute the MEA Commercial Desktop 4P strategy across product portfolio, pricing, channel placement, and promotional planning. Revenue and Profitability Management: Manage product line-up, pricing, promotions, rebates, and sales programs to support revenue growth, margin improvement, and profitable mix expansion. Market and Competitive Analysis: Conduct regular market, customer, and competitor analysis to identify growth opportunities, sharpen product positioning, and differentiate Lenovo’s desktop offerings. Forecasting and Supply Alignment: Lead desktop demand forecasting with country and territory sales teams, ensuring alignment with sales plans, supply availability, inventory health, and aged inventory targets. Sales and Channel Execution: Partner with country and territory sales teams to support weekly sales-out priorities, stock reviews, campaign execution, channel pricing, and promotional activities. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with regional and global product teams, marketing, alliances, operations, and sales stakeholders to implement commercial desktop strategies and support business growth initiatives. Performance Management: Track performance against revenue, profitability, product mix, inventory, and market share objectives, and recommend corrective actions where required to stay on plan. Customer and Product Positioning: Support sales and customer discussions by understanding business needs, recommending suitable desktop solutions, and positioning Lenovo offerings effectively against alternatives. Position Requirements Minimum of 5 years of experience in product management, 4P, business management, commercial channel management, or a related role. Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree in Business Administration, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field preferred. Strong understanding of the commercial PC business, desktop portfolio management, and channel dynamics across the Middle East and Africa. Proven ability to manage pricing, promotions, rebates, product mix, forecasting, and business performance against revenue and profitability targets. Strong analytical skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to translate data into clear business insights and actions. Good understanding of financial metrics, P&L drivers, inventory KPIs, margin management, and business planning processes. Effective communicator and influencer, able to present strategies, recommendations, and product positioning clearly to sales teams, leadership, and business partners. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: The Channel Tools & Platforms Analyst is responsible for overseeing and optimizing the ecosystem of tools and platforms that support partner, distribution, and channel account management. This role ensures that systems are efficient, integrated, and aligned with business objectives to drive productivity, partner engagement, and operational excellence. Key Responsibilities Platform Ownership & Optimization Manage and continuously improve channel-related platforms, including, partner portals, incentive management tools, communication and marketing tools and contribute to reporting dashboards, ensuring they meet evolving business needs. Business Alignment & Requirements Management Work closely with GEO business leaders and stakeholders to understand requirements, identify gaps, and translate them into actionable system enhancements that improve user experience and efficiency. System Integration & Operations Oversee integrations and ongoing improvment of tools in collaboration with DTIT and LXG teams, external vendors, and internal stakeholders, ensuring seamless functionality and minimal disruption. User Enablement & Training Develop and deliver comprehensive training programs, user documentation, and support materials to drive adoption and maximize effective use of platforms across the organization. Performance Monitoring & Continuous Improvement Track platform performance through analytics, user feedback, and usage insights to identify improvement opportunities and proactively resolve issues. requirements: We are looking for an enthusiastic and curious Intern to join our Marketing Sponsorships & Events team. This role is a chance to learn how global sponsorships and events come to life, and to gain hands-on experience working in a fast-moving international environment. You will support the Sponsorships & Events Manager in the daily activities of the team, from brainstorming ideas to helping with event preparation, reporting, and coordination with different departments. If you are curious about how brand sponsorships and events come to life, and want to gain hands-on experience, this internship opportunity will give you a front-row seat! What You Will Do: Support the team in preparing marketing events and sponsorship activations. Help coordinate small operational tasks: creating presentations, following up with teams, preparing reports. Join brainstorming sessions and share your ideas on how Motorola can connect with its target audiences and be on top of trends. Learn how to work with different departments (communications, sales, digital, etc.) and external partners. Get exposure to exciting projects like flagship product launches to large-scale fan activations. What We’re Looking For: Currently enrolled in university in Spain and able to sign an internship agreement (6–12 months). Excellent level of English (spoken and written). Curious, motivated, and eager to learn about marketing, events, and sponsorships. Organized and ready to help in a fast-paced team environment. Interest in sports, music, or urban culture is a big plus! What You’ll Get: The chance to learn from an experienced marketing team. Exposure to global sports partnerships (Euroleague, FIFA) and high-impact marketing campaigns. A friendly, international team where your ideas are welcomed. Real experience working inside a Fortune Global 500 company. The opportunity to make an impact while developing your career in marketing. About Motorola & Lenovo Motorola, part of Lenovo, is a global leader in technology and innovation. With partnerships across sports, fashion, and culture, we connect with people in ways that go beyond devices. From Euroleague Basketball to FIFA, our brand is present in some of the world’s most exciting moments. We look forward to discussing this opportunity with you soon! Please send CV in English. Our team is expanding its in-house design capabilities and is seeking an experienced Senior Mechanical Engineer with a strong background in server design. As part of Lenovo’s Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), you will contribute to the development of next-generation server products that help solve some of the world’s most complex computing challenges. In this role, you will collaborate with engineering teams across Lenovo’s global design centers to develop common building-block designs and deliver high-quality, innovative server solutions. You will be involved throughout the full product lifecycle—from concept development and design validation to market release and field sustainability. The fast-paced server industry demands solutions that are faster, smaller, and more energy efficient. As technologies continue to evolve, this role offers the opportunity to tackle complex engineering challenges while helping shape the future of Lenovo server products. Responsibilities: Design and develop next-generation server platforms, including: Printed circuit board (PCB) layouts Structural sheet metal enclosures Plastic components and assemblies Partner with system architects and cross-functional engineering teams to define and finalize product concepts. Create detailed mechanical designs and engineering documentation using industry-standard CAD tools. Develop, prototype, validate, and optimize mechanical designs to meet performance, quality, cost, and manufacturability requirements. Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve design issues throughout the product development lifecycle. Collaborate closely with thermal, power, system, manufacturing, and other engineering teams to ensure successful product delivery. Contribute to common building-block designs used across Lenovo's server portfolio. Perform engineering change management activities and maintain design documentation. Develop and manage bills of materials (BOMs), cost estimates, and tolerance analyses. Support product introduction, qualification testing, and field sustainment activities. Provide regular project updates and report weekly design status to management. Ensure designs comply with industry standards, regulatory requirements, and product quality objectives. Basic Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering Minimum of 10 years of mechanical design experience in server, storage, telecommunications, or related hardware product development. Minimum of 10 years of experience using PTC Creo for 3D mechanical design. Minimum of 10 years of experience using PTC Windchill for product data and CAD file management. Strong experience with Sheet metal design, Plastic part design, Mechanical assemblies and integration Experience managing engineering changes and product lifecycle documentation. Preferred Qualifications: Direct experience designing enterprise server platforms and rack-mounted systems. Hands-on experience assembling, debugging, and testing server hardware. Experience using basic mechanical fabrication and assembly tools. Familiarity with manufacturing processes and design for manufacturability (DFM). Knowledge of thermal and power system integration considerations in server products. Experience supporting products through launch, sustainment, and field issue resolution. requirements: Key Responsibilities Lead the technical development, validation, and project management of memory solutions for Consumer Notebook products. Act as the primary technical interface between memory suppliers and Lenovo cross-functional teams, driving alignment on memory specifications, sourcing strategies, qualification plans, and issue resolution throughout the product lifecycle. Review and direct suppliers' memory design activities, qualification plans, and validation status across component, AVL, and system levels; evaluate, approve, or challenge qualification results as required. Perform memory pre-qualification testing, design reviews, risk assessments, and critical validation activities to ensure product quality, reliability, and performance targets are achieved. Drive root cause analysis, defect investigation, and problem resolution efforts. Manage suppliers to deliver effective corrective actions and review/approve remediation plans. Support urgent qualification activities to mitigate memory supply constraints, evaluate lower-cost memory alternatives, and introduce competitive memory solutions to enhance product cost competitiveness and supply flexibility. Partner with engineering, procurement, quality, and product teams to develop and execute memory sourcing strategies that balance performance, cost, quality, and supply continuity. Provide deep technical expertise in emerging memory technologies, driving innovation and pioneering technology adoption for next-generation notebook platforms. Monitor industry trends, supplier roadmaps, and technology advancements to identify opportunities for memory innovation, performance optimization, and competitive differentiation. Preferred Candidate Profile Strong expertise in DRAM, LPDDR, memory architecture, signal integrity, validation, and qualification methodologies. Proven experience working with memory suppliers and leading cross-functional technical programs from concept through mass production. Strong problem-solving, project management, and stakeholder management skills with the ability to drive complex technical issues to closure. Demonstrated ability to evaluate emerging memory technologies and translate innovation into commercial product advantages. Strong verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with global teams, suppliers, and stakeholders across multiple regions. Additional

The team is closely working with Asia Pacific HQ in India. • The products are delivered through mobile network operators (MNO) and retailers. • Total team members are around 25 members. ■Job Objective • This role would be responsible for end-to-end marketing for the Motorola brand across phone and non-phone business in JAPAN. ■Job Description • End to End Marketing Campaign Planning for JAPAN’s product and brand campaigns. • Marketing communication planning, development and localization. • Drive paid media through - Media planning, execution and optimization for all product lines on all relevant channels. • Develop and execute corporate communication and product PR to drive earned media consistently. • Lead influencer campaigns to build consideration and purchase intent for the brand and products. • Lead end to end retail marketing. • Plan and execute events for new launches and/or tentpole moments for the brand. • Lead marketing discussions and plans with MNOs and Retail partners to maximise co-marketing initiatives and drive higher SOV through partner channels and investments. • Think innovatively to drive higher share of voice (SOV) with lesser share of expense (SOE) • Maintaining our social media presence across all digital channels, measure and report performance of all digital marketing campaigns, and assess against goals. • CRM and own media management (website, social platforms etc.) • Develop and monitor campaign budgets. • Management and coordination with all relevant internal (Finance, SCM, eCom, Product - Japan and global) and external stakeholders (Media agency, creative agency, PR agency, ecommerce partner) • Drive and deliver on brand and marketing metrics (search, awareness, consideration, Preference) that enable and result in driving the business metrics for the region (Profitable and rapid revenue growth) • Drive, Track, Monitor and report consumer insights through consumer research, consumer reviews/comments (Social and eCommerce) and Sentiment (Social) • Collaborate with agencies and other vendor partners to ensure brand deliverables. ■Requirement Must to have • Experience leading integrated marketing communications, including campaign planning, media strategy and localization. • Strong revenue-driven marketing expertise with a focus on sell-out and mid- to lower-funnel marketing. • Experience working with channel partners and retailers to develop and execute Go-to-Market (GTM) and co-marketing activities. • Strong understanding of marketing and business metrics, with a proven track record of driving revenue growth and marketing ROI. • Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to collaborate across cross-functional, regional and global teams. • Strong business-level proficiency in both Japanese and English. • Experience leading and developing high-performing marketing teams. Better to Have • Smartphone, telecom, MNO/MVNO or consumer electronics industry experience. • Experience with retail/trade marketing and carrier business. • PR, influencer marketing, CRM and owned media management. • Consumer insights, eCommerce and social listening. • AI-enabled marketing and marketing technology. • Understanding of P&L and business KPIs. • Curiosity for emerging technologies and consumer trends. About the Role Lenovo's North American category organization operates under the Intelligent Device Group (IDG). Within IDG, the 4P framework (Product, Price, Place, Promotion) is managed by dedicated 4P Managers. These teams control product offerings, align pricing, manage inventory, and drive promotions. The NA Category Director position will own the North America Tablet P&L, driving strategies and teams to maximize revenue, profit and category growth. This position has a hybrid schedule based out of Morrisville, NC with 3 onsite and 2 remote days per week. Key Responsibilities Business & Financial Leadership Lead end‑to‑end product lifecycle management from requirements definition and demand planning through launch, enablement, and product end‑of‑life. Deliver Business Management System (BMS) to improve forecast accuracy, business predictability, and execution excellence. Deliver regular updates to senior leadership. Portfolio & Go‑to‑Market Strategy Drive the overall NA Tablet portfolio strategy, including commercial and consumer products. Lead planning and execution for Relationship, SMB, and Consumer campaigns across U.S. and Canada. Align campaign strategy and execution with Lenovo Worldwide Business Unit (WW BU) teams. Ensure timely product announcements supported by robust Sales Enablement and training. Cross‑Functional Collaboration Partner closely with Sales, Marketing, Finance, Operations, Supply Chain, and Worldwide Product Marketing to achieve financial and operational objectives. Translate North America product and market needs into WW product and marketing requirements. Collaborate with supply chain and demand planning to manage parts risk, optimize inventory health, and minimize aged inventory exposure. Ensure competitive pricing strategies aligned with market dynamics. Sales & Partner Enablement Work with Corporate, Global, Public Sector, SMB, Retail and Web sales teams to develop and execute sales strategies that drive Tablet growth. Educate and enable Lenovo sellers and channel partners on the Tablet portfolio. Partner with retail sales, channel teams to support deal execution and close opportunities, particularly within Consumer, SMB and Web segments. People Leadership Lead, mentor, and develop a team of Tablet Product Managers and Business Managers. Foster strong collaboration, accountability, and performance through effective communication, coaching, and talent development. Basic Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Sales, or a related field required. 15+ years of proven success growing and scaling commercial electronic product categories and expanding routes to market. 7+ years of people management experience in a matrixed global organization. Preferred Qualifications Deep understanding of the Tablet market, including customer needs, competitive positioning, and technology trends. Strong ability to analyze competitive landscape and develop differentiated sales and marketing tactics. Demonstrated experience managing complex, cross‑functional initiatives in a fast‑paced environment. Experience with Retail and Telco business models. Knowledge of demand gen and marketing strategies to drive brand awareness. Knowledge of pricing strategies, inventory management, and supply chain logistics. Strong sense of ownership, transparency and accountability. Strong sense of team success, including recognition. Strong written and spoken presence. Cultural Competency working across Chinese, American, Desi, European, and South American cultural contexts, ensuring messages are communicated with clarity and tact. MBA degree in Business, Marketing, Sales, or a related field strongly preferred. This position has a hybrid schedule based out of Morrisville, NC with 3 onsite and 2 remote days per week. requirements: The North America (NA) Alliances team develops and manages strategic partnerships that drive mutual growth and business outcomes for Lenovo and its alliance partners. We are seeking a highly skilled and results-oriented Alliances Budget Planner to join the team and serve as a critical enabler of operational excellence, funding optimization, and strategic growth. This position plays a key role in maximizing end-to-end partner investments, increasing funding utilization, and providing the data-driven insights needed to strengthen partner funding strategic allocations. This role serves as the central point of coordination for partner investment planning and funding utilization across the NA Alliances organization and will be the one-stop-shop source of NA alliances funding through a centralized site. Responsibilities: Serve as the primary point of contact for Alliance funding planning, optimization, and governance across North America. Lead annual and quarterly budget planning in partnership with Alliance Leads, Marketing Operations and other areas that depend on alliances programs. Provide guidance to optimize partner investments, ensuring funds are allocated to the highest-priority business opportunities and fully utilized. Develop and maintain funding tracking through dashboards, reporting, and insights to drive visibility, accountability, and informed decision-making. Act as the “Partners Chessboard Master,” balancing investments across programs, initiatives, and stakeholders to maximize business impact. Maintain expertise in partner funding models, programs, and investment vehicles, identifying opportunities to support Lenovo growth objectives. Drive continuous improvement in planning, execution, and funding management processes to increase operational efficiency and investment effectiveness. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Marketing, Operations, or a related field. 8+ years of professional experience in technology, partner ecosystems, business operations, finance, marketing, or related fields. 5+ years of experience managing partner funding, program investments, budgets, or financial planning processes. Strong budget management and financial planning experience. Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to influence senior leaders and executive stakeholders. Demonstrated ability to operate effectively in a complex, matrixed organization. Self-motivated, highly organized, and capable of driving results with minimal supervision. Strong attention to detail and commitment to operational excellence. Proven ability to analyze data and translate insights into actionable recommendations. Working knowledge of Lenovo products, services, partner ecosystems, or similar technology industry environments. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Job Summary: As the OEM Lab Manager, you will engage with OEM customers, partners, sales, and Lenovo product teams to evaluate, understand, and drive technical requirements for complex OEM solution offerings. Your role will involve leveraging Lenovo’s existing product portfolio and customizing new offerings for unique OEM solutions. You will develop technical relationships with key alliance partners, suppliers, and integrators to ensure Lenovo meets market needs. Additionally, you will identify gaps in our portfolio, provide input to product development teams, and engage in specific testing and certifications for the OEM business. Building strong relationships with manufacturing teams will be crucial to ensure timely project progression through various manufacturing processes. You will act as Lenovo's technical arbiter with customers, external partners, and internal teams. Responsibilities: Lead and empower a lab engineering team to conduct customer-driven Proof of Concepts, customer test scenarios, and provide pre-sales customer support. Collaborate with manufacturing teams to create processes supporting customization efforts for OEM customers. Work with process control systems (e.g., Windchill, Factsheets, PCRs, Jira). Manage process execution and release methodologies within the manufacturing environment. Collaborate with local test engineers (TE), quality engineers (QE), manufacturing engineers (ME), ITC, and special bids teams. Define test automation processes and solutions for the lab engineering team. Define process flow for team duties and assign tasks. Customize and support of various Linux distributions. Architect new ideas for revenue generation through innovative engineering services & solutions. Leverage technologies including Windows OS, Windows IoT, Linux distros, shell scripting, Java, Python, device drivers, BIOS customizations, and Linux kernel. Summarizing and conveying ideas and issues to upper management. Collaborate effectively on a WW team with members located in different parts of the world – across various time zones. Basic Qualifications: Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Electronic Engineering, or a related engineering discipline. 5+ years of experience in the PC or related industry. Past experience managing a technical lab team. Experience working with third-party suppliers. Ability to work in person at the OEM Engineering Lab in Morrisville, NC. Preferred Qualifications: Proven capacity to navigate and resolve undefined issues. Ability to create repeatable, efficient processes across teams. Vision and drive to grow and empower the team. Understanding of custom services and manufacturing processes. Strong collaboration and negotiation skills. Excellent communication and reporting skills. Experience with various retail solutions and technologies. This position will be working onsite in our OEM Lab that is based at our North America Headquarters in Raleigh, NC. Candidates that are already local to Raleigh, NC are preferred. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Who You’ll Work With: We manufacture one of the world’s most extensive portfolios of connected products, including: PCs (ThinkPad, Yoga, Lenovo Legion), tablets, smartphones and workstations; as well as augmented and virtual reality (ThinkReality), smart home office solutions, software and services. In this role you will be supporting our Intelligent Device Group (IDG). The IDG at Lenovo houses our PC and Smart Devices. We combine investment in high-growth segments (workstations, gaming, visuals, thin and light devices) with new innovation in the smart device categories and continue to explore new opportunities across AR/VR, artificial intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), security and as-a-service solutions. What You'll Do: This retention and development and/or acquisition focused PC Specialist will work with K12 field partners and end-user customers within the US to drive opportunities and education around Lenovo's portfolio offerings in K12 segments. PC Specialists will support configuration discussions and provide solutions as well as proactive follow-up, product information and overall account support via the telephone. This role will also work within the segments initiatives including weekly forecast and opportunity management, outbound calls and weekly program follow ups with the partners. Responsibilities include: Achieve profit and revenue objectives Develop and cultivate client & partner relationships Lead and assist in implementing the sales strategy within your account, driving revenue growth across the Lenovo’s IDG portfolio. Ensure that products, services and solutions recommended to clients will fulfill their needs Manage all aspects of complex IDG sales and drive to closure Utilize MS Dynamics to effectively manage sales activity & sales pipeline across multiple quarters. Stay informed and adaptable to Lenovo’s ongoing product and service developments Serves as a trusted advisor within IDG for customers, partners, and internal teams. Drive to achieve company growth projections in a dynamic and competitive environment Excellent interpersonal, communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills Has the ability to operate efficiently and achieve outcomes within typical business pressures and accelerated customer timelines. Acquisition-minded and driven for prospecting through digital and traditional outreach methods such as cold calling, email campaigns, Vidyard, ZoomInfo, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator. Basic Qualifications: BS or BA degree or equivalent professional work experience At least 2 years of inside sales or field (outside) sales experience PC Industry/IT Hardware/Software sales experience Open to occasional business travel Preferred Qualifications: Prior experience in a quota-driven environment. Excellent communication & collaboration skills Proven success in building relationships at all levels of business and IT contacts within assigned accounts We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: We are looking for a PCG Testing Engineer to join our team on a full-time basis. In this role, you will execute detailed product testing, document defects, support test environment setup, and provide usability feedback. Your Key Responsibilities: Execute detailed testing of product functionality, performance, compatibility, and user experience based on test cases. Accurately document and report defects found in hardware, system software (e.g., Windows), drivers, and pre-installed applications, and assist in issue reproduction. Support the setup of PC testing environments, including OS deployment, driver installation, peripheral connections, and test software configuration. Write selected test cases and provide usability and design feedback from an end-user perspective. Communicate actively with the team, respond quickly, and complete assigned testing tasks. Position Requirements: * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Electronic Information, or related fields. * Understanding of basic software testing concepts and strong interest in computer hardware. * Minimum 2 years of testing experience; experience in mobile devices, smart hardware, or consumer electronics is preferred. * Familiar with Windows OS usage, troubleshooting, and system reinstallation. * Knowledge of computer hardware components (e.g., CPU, memory, hard drive) and basic hardware identification skills. * Basic knowledge of any programming language (e.g., Python, PowerShell) and ability to write simple scripts. What Lenovo Can Offer You: An international team with a high focus on Gender Diversity. Employee Assistance Program for psychological, legal, and financial consultancy. A company committed to sustainability through CO2 Offset, Asset Recovery Services, and Lenovo Certified Refurbished portfolio. Access to training for personal development via our internal e-learning platform. Mentorship program to support your career growth. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Position Overview Lenovo is seeking an experienced PR & Communications Manager to lead strategic communications, media relations, executive thought leadership, and content development for our Solutions & Services business in Hong Kong. This role will be instrumental in positioning Lenovo as a leader in AI, digital transformation, and technology solutions by driving compelling storytelling, executive visibility, and customer-centric communications. The successful candidate will be a strategic communicator with excellent writing skills, strong relationships across Hong Kong's Tier 1 business and technology media, and the ability to influence senior stakeholders. Fluency in both English and Cantonese is essential. Key Responsibilities Strategic Communications Develop and execute integrated communications strategies that support Lenovo's AI and Solutions & Services business priorities. Create compelling narratives that strengthen Lenovo's reputation as a trusted technology solutions provider. Partner with regional and global communications, marketing, and business teams to ensure consistent messaging across all external communications. Media Relations Build and maintain strong relationships with Tier 1 business, technology, and trade media in Hong Kong. Develop and execute proactive media relations strategies that generate high-impact earned coverage for Lenovo's Solutions & Services business. Write, edit, and distribute press releases, media alerts, executive statements, and other press materials that communicate Lenovo's business milestones, product announcements, partnerships, customer successes, and thought leadership initiatives. Manage media inquiries, interviews, press briefings, and executive engagements. Collaborate with external PR agencies to develop compelling media narratives, story angles, and integrated communications campaigns. Monitor media coverage, identify emerging opportunities, and provide insights and recommendations to strengthen Lenovo's brand presence. Thought Leadership & Content Develop thought leadership campaigns that position Lenovo as an AI and solutions leader. Create compelling content including executive bylines, articles, customer stories, opinion pieces, and media pitches. Work with internal subject matter experts and agency partners to translate complex technology topics into engaging business-focused content. Support paid and earned amplification strategies to maximize content reach among target audiences. Executive Communications Elevate the profile of Lenovo's Hong Kong-based Solutions & Services leaders through strategic communications, media engagement, speaking opportunities, and thought leadership. Develop executive messaging, presentation materials, media briefing documents, and keynote content. Manage executive LinkedIn content strategies that build credibility and industry influence. Events & Speaking Opportunities Identify and secure speaking opportunities at leading industry conferences, business forums, and technology events. Build relationships with event organizers and industry associations to increase Lenovo's executive visibility. Support keynote presentations, panel discussions, and executive media appearances. Customer Storytelling Showcase customer success stories that demonstrate measurable business outcomes enabled by Lenovo's Solutions & Services portfolio. Collaborate with sales, marketing, and customer teams to identify compelling customer references and transform them into external communications assets. Ensure customer-centric messaging is consistently reflected across media, executive communications, and thought leadership content. Qualifications Required Experience: Bachelor's degree in Communications, Public Relations, Journalism, Marketing, or a related discipline. 7–10 years of experience in corporate communications, public relations, or executive communications. Experience in the technology, enterprise IT, consulting, or B2B sectors is highly preferred. Demonstrated success developing executive thought leadership and media engagement strategies. Language Requirements: Fluent written and spoken English. Fluent written and spoken Cantonese. Mandarin proficiency is an advantage. Skills & Competencies Established relationships with Hong Kong Tier 1 business and technology media. Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills. Strong executive presence and experience working with senior business leaders. Excellent project management and stakeholder management skills. Experience managing PR agencies and cross-functional communications programs. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, global matrix organization. Strong understanding of digital communications, LinkedIn thought leadership, and integrated media strategies. Additional

Job Responsibilities ： 1) Lead and review internal control and Enterprise Risk Management mechanisms, help the business department build up robust internal control environment and processes. 2) Take ownership of GTN review on end-to-end forecast, accrual, program, payment, and reconciliation. 3) Perform risk assessment and prepare annual work plan; Involve in program design and take charge of all programs review including annual and flexible progams. 4) Lead team to carry out process review upon GTN reconciliation to assess compliance, control effectiveness and reationality of existing policies / processes and provide recommendations to business owners; 5) Lead team to conduct monitoring testing upon GTN key controls, supervise BU and Functions to determine if internal policies are followed and key controls are effectively implemented. 6) Work closely with business teams by focusing on high-risk / emerging business areas and driving control improvement, support business teams to build / transform control processes where appropriate; 7) For findings identified by internal or external auditors, discuss with business owners and help them work out proper remediation plans and follow up the action items to completion. Job Requirements: 1) Above 7 years’ working experience with internal control / audit / compliance areas; experience with both Big 4 and MNC is a plus; 2) Familiar with internal control framework, financial reporting and enterprise risk management, holder of CIA/CISA/Public Accountant (e.g. CICPA, ACCA, AICPA…) is a plus; 3) Advanced data analysis and documentation skills; 4) Strong capability to convey messages through presentations; 5) Excellent communication, leadership and problem-solving skills; 6) Honest, uphold high professional ethical bar; 7) Customer oriented, strategic thinking, strong sense to link corporate strategy with tactical actions to be deployed by function teams; 8) Self-motivated and be able to motivate team; 9) Strong business acumen and risk awareness; 10) Ability to work under pressure as well as handle multiple tasks, stay organized and deal with ambiguity; 11) Logical thinker, proactive and self-starter. 12) Fluent in English, could use it as working language. Additional

requirements: Lenovo is currently looking for a Client Manager to own and grow a portfolio of strategic and key accounts as a trusted advisor. Build senior customer relationships, set account strategy, and drive sustainable revenue and profit growth across the full One Lenovo portfolio (ISG, SSG, IDG). Key responsibilities: Develop and execute account plans across complex, multi-stakeholder customer ecosystems. Build and own senior and C-level relationships, positioning Lenovo as a strategic partner. Lead the sales strategy for your accounts and drive revenue across the full Lenovo portfolio. Own financial performance and forecast governance across your account base. Work with cross-functional teams to move complex deals through the funnel, including RFP response. requirements: Job Responsibilities: 1. CapEx Planning & Budget Management a. Collect, consolidate, and validate CapEx budget submissions across BUs. b. Standardize and structure data (templates, categorization, initiatives) to ensure consistency and transparency. c. Maintain accurate and complete CapEx data in financial systems. d. Ensure alignment with strategic priorities, funding constraints, and ROI expectations. 2. Forecast, Actual Tracking & Data Management a. Track CapEx performance across budget, forecast, and actuals with strong data discipline. b. Lead rolling forecast updates based on project progress and latest business outlook. c. Ensure data integrity and consistency across planning and reporting cycles. 3. Variance Analysis & Financial Insights a. Analyze execution variances and identify key drivers (timing, cost overrun, scope change). b. Translate variance analysis into clear financial insights and actionable recommendations. c. Support management decision-making with forward-looking risk and opportunity views. 4. Budget Discipline & Process Governance a. Enforce approval processes and prevent out-of-STAMP or bypass requests. b. Ensure full compliance with DOA policies and governance requirements. c. Promote disciplined investment practices and strengthen control over CapEx execution. 5. Reporting & Cross-functional Communication a. Prepare regular CapEx reporting (budget, forecast, actuals, variance insights). b. Provide clear, concise insights to CFO and BU Finance teams to support decision-making. c. Drive data reconciliation across Finance, BUs, and Accounting for accurate external reporting. d. Act as a key interface to align cross-functional stakeholders on CapEx status and priorities. 6. Incubation Expense Management a. Manage incubation expenses across budgeting, forecasting, and actual tracking. b. Prepare regular reporting to ensure transparency on spending and variance. Job Requirements: 1. Bachelor’s degree or above in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related fields. 2. 2–3 years of experience in FP&A, Controlling, Financial Analysis, or Finance Operations. 3. Proficient in Excel and PowerPoint; experience with SAP, Anaplan, or Power BI is preferred. 4. Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills. 5. Detail-oriented, self-motivated, and capable of managing multiple priorities. Additional

requirements: Position Description The General Procurement organization is responsible for sourcing, purchasing, and managing spend for all non-production goods or services used internally by Lenovo. Major operating expense categories: RD, marketing, and workforce. The General Procurement team is responsible for leading the following activities: - Working with business unit/budget owner (our internal Lenovo stakeholders), on appropriate spending strategy, including industry best practices, category/market trend analysis, and supplier capabilities; - Appropriately selecting Supplier sources based on driving competitive sourcing activities; - Forming contracts with selected Suppliers to help ensure both deliverables and risk avoidance; - Managing Supplier performance via stakeholder feedback and performance-based metrics; - Ensuring appropriate business controls are in place to protect Lenovo. Job Responsibilities： As a sourcing professional, this candidate will take the lead in multi-category indirect procurement management, responsible for developing and executing category management strategies, optimizing end-to-end sourcing processes for non-production goods and services across various categories. He/she should have extensive experience in indirect procurement covering multiple categories, proactively engage internal stakeholders to provide strategic sourcing solutions, drive high-impact procurement initiatives, and act as the core commercial lead for cross-category spend optimization and supplier ecosystem management. The candidate will be responsible for six (6) primary business metrics: - Manage and optimize Lenovo’s cross-category spend with external suppliers, generating significant Procurement Value-Add through strategic sourcing and category management; - Drive Customer Satisfaction (Serviceability) for internal executives and operations teams via scientific supplier selection, category strategy alignment, and proactive engagement; - Ensure robust Business Controls throughout sourcing, contracting, spending, and supplier management processes; - Develop and promote best practices, lead transformation initiatives to achieve continuous improvement in category procurement efficiency and effectiveness; - Initiate and optimize procure-to-pay processes for non-strategic purchases, enhancing overall procurement operational efficiency; - Formulate and implement category management strategies to optimize category structure, reduce supply chain risks, and improve long-term supply stability. Job Requirements： The selected candidate will exhibit the following abilities: - Analytics -- Proficient in competitive bid analysis, cost analysis, fair-value analysis, and scenario modeling, with the ability to translate data into category strategy insights; - Category Management -- Capable of developing and executing category management strategies, analyzing category lifecycle, and optimizing category procurement mix across multiple indirect categories; - Problem solving -- Ability to understand core objectives, identify key obstacles, conduct in-depth root cause analysis, mitigate cross-category supply risks, and develop feasible alternative solutions; - Negotiation -- Expertise in conducting fact-based, high-level negotiations with suppliers, driving favorable commercial terms for cross-category collaborations; - Team Collaboration -- Ability to communicate and collaborate effectively, drive cross-functional and cross-regional work successfully in a matrix team environment; - Independence -- Strong self-management ability to complete complex tasks and strategic initiatives with minimal supervision; - Fact-seeking -- Proactive in conducting in-depth research on goods/services across multiple categories to support strategy development and negotiation advantages; - Project Management -- Excellent ability to prioritize strategic and operational activities, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and maximize overall procurement value. - Indirect procurement experience covering multiple categories/ RD demand is plus; - Proven track record in category management strategy development and execution is highly desirable; - 2-3 years’ professional experience in spend management and strategic sourcing is required; - Bachelors Degree at least; - Fluent in oral and written English Additional

He/she should have extensive experience in indirect procurement covering multiple categories, proactively engage internal stakeholders to provide strategic sourcing solutions, drive high-impact procurement initiatives, and act as the core commercial lead for cross-category spend optimization and supplier ecosystem management. As a Software Product Manager (SPM) on the AIW team, you will define, drive, and deliver software products that leverage on-device AI and wearable innovation . You will own the end-to-end product lifecycle, partnering closely with engineering, design, data science, and business stakeholders to transform AI-driven concepts into impactful customer experiences across Motorola devices. Key Responsibilities Product Strategy & Vision Define product vision, strategy, and roadmap for AI-powered wearable and mobile experiences Translate emerging AI capabilities (e.g., on-device AI, contextual intelligence) into differentiated user value Identify market opportunities and drive competitive positioning aligned to Motorola’s software philosophy Execution & Delivery Own end-to-end product lifecycle from ideation through launch and post-launch optimization Partner with engineering teams to translate requirements into deliverable features and releases Define product requirements, user stories, success metrics, and delivery timelines Drive prioritization decisions balancing user impact, technical feasibility, and business goals Cross-Functional Leadership Collaborate with: Software engineering (Android, AI frameworks) UX/design Data science/AI teams Marketing and business stakeholders Lead cross-functional alignment and decision-making across global teams Ensure strong inter-group coordination and execution discipline AI & Innovation Focus Drive adoption of on-device AI technologies to enable real-time, efficient user experiences Partner with engineering to integrate AI models, frameworks, and features into Motorola devices Identify opportunities to differentiate through AI in wearables, smartphones, and emerging form factors Customer & Market Insights Gather and synthesize customer insights, usage data, and feedback Conduct competitive analysis and benchmark Motorola experiences against industry leaders Continuously improve product performance and user satisfaction Performance & Optimization Define and track KPIs (adoption, engagement, retention, feature success) Drive iterative improvements post-launch through data and experimentation Ensure continuous improvement of software experiences and timely updates Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field 5+ years of experience Experience in software product management , preferably in mobile, wearables, or AI-driven products Strong understanding of Android platform and software development lifecycle Experience working with engineering teams on technical product delivery Proven ability to translate business needs into technical product requirements Preferred Qualifications Experience with AI/ML products or on-device AI technologies requirements: Lenovo is looking for a trusted Technical Consultant to customers and sales teams across the full sales cycle - from early customer discussions to final solution design. Translate complex customer requirements into the right infrastructure architecture, correctly sized and commercially viable, focused on the customer’s business outcome rather than technology alone. Key responsibilities: Own the technical solutioning for complex infrastructure opportunities across the full sales cycle (discovery → sizing → architecture → proposal → deal support). Translate customer requirements into scalable architectures covering compute, storage, networking and AI / HPC where relevant. Conduct product demonstrations, proofs of concept (POCs), and technical presentations. Design and size solutions based on workload, performance and commercial constraints. Act as trusted advisor to customers, supporting technical discussions at expert and CIO level. Partner closely with Sales, Product and Services teams to build winning and margin-accretive solutions. Drive technical differentiation versus competition in strategic deals. Enable partners and internal teams on solution design, sizing tools and best practices. Support key bids and large opportunities, including RFP responses and technical presentations. Contribute to a shared sales target through solution wins, supporting pipeline progression and forecast accuracy. What you bring: Strong experience in infrastructure (servers, storage, networking), ideally with exposure to HPC / AI / high-performance workloads. Proven track record in pre-sales, solution consulting, or technical advisory roles. Solid capability in solution sizing and architecture design (workloads, performance drivers, TCO considerations). Ability to simplify complex technical topics for different audiences. Strong customer-facing experience and confidence in senior-level discussions. Structured working style, able to manage multiple complex opportunities in parallel. Fluent English required; local-market language required per country. What we offer: A dynamic, international environment within a global technology leader. Strong career development across Europe and globally. Access to continuous learning platforms. Flexible working models (country dependent). Competitive compensation and performance-based incentives. We are looking forward to discussing this career opportunity with you! requirements: Position Overview Lead the architecture design, technology innovation, and development of next-generation power products, including GaN chargers, desktop charging solutions, power banks, docking power solutions, and emerging energy products. Drive power technology roadmaps, common platform development, and technical collaboration with ODM/OEM partners to deliver industry-leading solutions for global customers. Job Responsibilities Define system architecture and key technology strategies for power products ranging from 65W to 400W+, covering power conversion, fast charging protocols, power allocation, thermal management, EMC/EMI, safety, and reliability. Lead development of chargers, power banks, docking power solutions, and custom enterprise power products, ensuring delivery targets for quality, cost, performance, and schedule. Build and evolve reusable power platforms to improve technology reuse, development efficiency, and portfolio competitiveness. Drive technical engagement with ODM/OEM partners, evaluate technical solutions, manage risks, and resolve complex design and manufacturing challenges. Monitor industry trends, explore emerging technologies, and generate innovations and patents that strengthen Lenovo's competitive advantage. Job Qualifications Bachelor's degree or above in Power Electronics, Electrical Engineering, Electronics, Automation, or related fields. 10+ years of experience in power electronics product development, with proven expertise in chargers, adapters, fast charging technologies, and high-power power systems. Strong knowledge of LLC, ACF, QR Flyback, Active Clamp Flyback, Synchronous Rectification, USB PD3.1 EPR, PPS, and major charging protocols. Solid understanding of thermal design, EMC/EMI, safety compliance, reliability engineering, DFM/DFA, and mass production processes. Experience with GaN power architectures, high-power products (140W+), platform development, and global ODM/OEM collaboration is highly preferred. Demonstrated technical leadership, system-level thinking, innovation capability, and strong execution skills. Patent contributions in power electronics is must. Additional

education: 本科及以上学历，市场营销、电力工程、自动化、机器人相关专业优先
requirements: 一、岗位职责 自主开拓：依托自身自有能源电力客户资源，独立负责机器人巡检产品（主要为轮足机器狗等）在电网、发电行业的客户对接、需求挖掘、方案输出、商务谈判及招投标全流程，实现个人销售业绩目标、回款目标，打造标杆项目案例。 团队协同：牵头协同区域销售团队，制定区域市场拓展策略，分享客户资源、行业经验及成单技巧，指导区域销售开展客户开发、项目跟进工作，协助解决区域拓展中的难点问题，推动区域整体销售目标达成。 客户深耕：维护自身核心客户关系，同时联动区域销售，搭建覆盖国网、南网（省/市/县级）、大型能源集团（国能、华能、大唐等）的全域客户体系，挖掘客户二次合作及转介绍机会，提升客户粘性与忠诚度。 市场引领：调研能源电力行业巡检领域政策导向、市场动态、竞品情况及技术趋势，结合公司产品优势，输出市场分析报告及拓展建议，参与公司市场策略、产品迭代及解决方案优化的研讨。 渠道搭建：协同区域销售拓展行业内渠道商、集成商资源，建立战略合作关系，整合内外部资源，提升产品在能源电力领域的市场覆盖度与渗透率。 二 、 岗位要求 （一）核心硬性要求 学历：本科及以上学历，市场营销、电力工程、自动化、机器人相关专业优先 行业资历： 8 年及以上能源电力行业（电网、发电）机器人巡检业务拓展/销售经验 ，具备资深业务拓展能力，熟悉行业发展规律、采购流程、预算体系及决策链条。 成单能力：具备 独立成单经验 ，有机器人巡检产品（机器狗、巡检机器人等）在能源电力场景的成功成单案例，有百万级及以上项目操盘经验者优先。 客户资源： 拥有稳定、优质的自有能源电力客户资源 ，可直接对接国网、南网、大型能源集团核心决策层，能快速启动业务、达成业绩突破。 协同能力：具备团队协同与指导能力，有带领区域销售团队、协同拓展市场的经验，能有效整合资源、赋能团队，推动区域整体业绩提升。 （二）能力素质要求 商务能力：具备卓越的商务谈判、沟通表达及资源整合能力，善于捕捉客户需求，能独立应对复杂商务场景，高效推进项目落地。 市场敏感度：能精准把握能源电力行业政策机遇与市场需求变化，具备较强的市场分析、预判及开拓能力，能主动挖掘潜在客户及合作机会。 管理协同：具备良好的团队管理、指导及协同能力，能快速联动区域销售、技术、交付等部门，高效解决项目推进及团队拓展中的各类问题。 抗压与执行：目标感极强，工作积极主动，能承受高强度业绩压力，具备优秀的时间管理能力、执行力及攻坚能力，可适应高频行业出差。 职业素养：诚信正直，具备强烈的责任心、敬业精神及行业深耕意识，认同公司产品理念，愿意与公司、团队共同成长，打造行业核心竞争力。 Additional

requirements: Sobre a posição Estamos em busca de uma pessoa dinâmica, organizada e com excelente habilidade de comunicação para atuar na interface entre times internos e parceiros externos. Essa pessoa terá um papel fundamental no acompanhamento de iniciativas relacionadas a serviços financeiros digitais, garantindo alinhamento entre áreas, acompanhamento de indicadores e suporte à execução de projetos e campanhas. Buscamos alguém com perfil colaborativo, que goste de se relacionar com diferentes stakeholders, tenha facilidade para transitar entre áreas de negócio e tecnologia e consiga acompanhar múltiplas frentes simultaneamente. Principais responsabilidades Atuar como ponto de contato entre parceiros externos e equipes internas. Apoiar o relacionamento e a comunicação com parceiros estratégicos. Coordenar e acompanhar demandas envolvendo diferentes áreas da empresa, como Vendas, Produto, Operações, Tecnologia e Negócios. Garantir o alinhamento entre stakeholders internos e externos durante a execução de projetos e iniciativas. Acompanhar cronogramas, entregas e pendências, contribuindo para o andamento das atividades. Monitorar indicadores operacionais e elaborar relatórios de acompanhamento. Apoiar a execução de campanhas e iniciativas comerciais em conjunto com os times envolvidos. Identificar oportunidades de melhoria em processos e fluxos operacionais. Contribuir para a organização e documentação de processos, atividades e projetos. Requisitos Ensino superior completo ou em andamento em Administração, Engenharia, Economia, Sistemas de Informação, Tecnologia ou áreas correlatas. Boa capacidade de comunicação verbal e escrita. Facilidade para se relacionar com diferentes públicos e áreas da empresa. Perfil organizado, proativo e com forte senso de responsabilidade. Capacidade de acompanhar múltiplas demandas simultaneamente. Boa capacidade analítica e atenção aos detalhes. Inglês avançado. Diferenciais Experiência prévia em operações, atendimento corporativo, customer success, parcerias, vendas consultivas ou gestão de projetos. Vivência em empresas de tecnologia, fintechs, bancos digitais ou meios de pagamento. Familiaridade com indicadores de negócio e elaboração de relatórios. Interesse por produtos financeiros digitais e soluções de crédito. Perfil que buscamos Pessoa comunicativa e com boa desenvoltura para interação com parceiros e diferentes áreas. Capacidade de construir relacionamentos e atuar de forma colaborativa. Perfil "hands-on", com disposição para aprender e assumir novas responsabilidades. Flexibilidade para atuar em um ambiente dinâmico e em constante evolução. Interesse em entender o negócio de forma ampla e contribuir para o sucesso das iniciativas da área #MBG Additional

About our Team The Manufacturing Test Strategy and Automation team is responsible for delivering test platforms and visualization solutions to support the manufacturing of Lenovo Servers, Storage, and Rack products. As our product portfolio evolves and grows into new strategic areas, we have an opening for a Software Engineer to develop and maintain test platform content and associated tools and utilities that enable automation and monitoring of the manufacturing test process. Our Test Platform is a framework that enables manufacturing tests to be executed in our factories and provides the means to execute tests on a variety of systems, configure the test to be run, provide run control and error handling, data and floor visualization, as well as interfacing to shop floor control and warranty databases. New features are constantly required as product architecture and content changes, as well as to provide process optimizations. The Test Platform team also develops tools for process visualization and for extracting and presenting process and quality data as required by our internal teams and customers. Lenovo has manufacturing facilities in The United States, China, Hungary, Mexico and Brazil and the expectation is that travel to these sites would be required as part of this role. Responsibilities: Platform Test Engineering is responsible for designing, implementing, testing, and maintaining processes and code for our test platform and visualization tools. You will be working with Local and global teams to identify, quantify and solve problems and improve overall procedures through automation and internal tool development. Interface with multiple fulfillment sites to enable and manage solution delivery without impacting production. Design, develop, and maintain code to provide platform and tool solutions Using Python 3 for code development Vue for web frameworks (JavaScript) MSQL for database queries Use data from IT systems and shop floor control to develop real time visualization tools On-Site support and review of ongoing challenges Data Driven Engineering – create tools and processes to support manufacturing and testing based on actual site performance Interface with multiple fulfillment sites to enable and manage solution delivery without impacting production. Basic Requirements: 3-5+ years of programming experience Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Computer Engineering, or a related engineering degree program Proficient in Python3.x Vue for web frameworks MSQL for database queries Preferred Requirements: You will be successful in this position if you are detail oriented, methodical, and organized, and like to work through complex problems A master’s degree in computer science, Computer Engineering, or a similar field would be helpful Experience with developing manufacturing tools, working with manufacturing systems, and test processes Experience with complex process development and deployment Self-motivated team player Able to apply engineering concepts Effective communicator Additional

experience: 10–15+ years in Digital Workplace, end user computing, or managed services
requirements: As a Principal Digital Transformation Consultant (FDE Track), you will lead strategic workplace modernization for enterprise customers while also acting as a forward‑deployed technical leader during early deployment and MVP phases. You combine executive‑level advisory, solution architecture, and hands‑on technical orchestration to accelerate time‑to‑value across Lenovo DWS, DaaS, and Lenovo xIQ. You will embed with lighthouse customers to run discovery, shape roadmaps, configure early experiences, prototype automation or telemetry patterns, and ensure customer value is realized rapidly. You will bridge business strategy, product thinking, and pragmatic engineering execution, guiding programs from blueprint → MVP → pilot → scale → steady state. Key Responsibilities: 1) Strategy, Value Advisory & Executive Leadership - Lead C‑suite and VP‑level strategy workshops to align vision, value drivers, AI adoption priorities, and operating model shifts. - Build board‑ready business cases and P&L models quantifying TCO, OpEx/CapEx mix, cost‑to‑serve improvements, productivity lift, and experience impact. - Translate AI/automation, UEM, and collaboration programs into investment theses and risk narratives for executives. 2) Forward‑Deployed Engagement & Early Value Realization (FDE) - Embed with customers during early engagement to drive hands‑on discovery, process validation, and rapid prototyping. - Build or orchestrate lightweight integrations, automations, telemetry mappings, and xIQ insights needed to achieve the first measurable outcomes. - Configure early deployment patterns across provisioning, xIQ dashboards, automation rules, or experience telemetry readiness. - Own the first working version of the solution in partnership with SAs, delivery, and product. - Ensure early value milestones (e.g., MTTR reduction, automation coverage, Autopilot/UEM readiness) are achieved quickly. 3) GTM Leadership & Customer Journey Excellence - Drive end‑to‑end customer journeys from assessment → blueprint → MVP → scale → steady state. - Partner with Sales, Offering, and Marketing to craft value narratives and pursuit strategies. - Mentor pursuit teams and raise win‑rates through improved storytelling, qualification, and solution strength. 4) AI‑Enabled Operating Model & HITL Governance - Create hybrid AI + human operating models with clearly defined HITL touchpoints, safety constraints, and governance. - Define escalation paths, decision boundaries, and quality guardrails for predictive and agentic automation patterns. - Prioritize AI and automation use cases, guiding rollout into proactive support and self‑heal scenarios using Lenovo xIQ. 5) Deep Process Discovery & FDE‑Driven Product Goal Creation - Run hands‑on discovery using workshops, telemetry, analytics, and frontline shadowing. - Translate discovery insights into product goals, user stories, and measurable outcomes. - Shape MVPs, automation packs, and roadmap increments with DWS, xIQ, and Offering teams. - Validate hypotheses through customer‑embedded prototypes and pilots. 6) Solution Architecture & Hands‑On Orchestration - Convert business outcomes into end‑to‑end architecture across DWS, DaaS, UEM/Autopilot, collaboration, security, and modern support. - Act as forward‑deployed orchestrator during MVP: configure telemetry, align identity/security, tune automation rules, validate xIQ dashboards. - Partner with architects and delivery to ensure feasibility, scalability, and optimization. 7) Program Governance, Change Leadership & Value Tracking - Establish program governance with milestones, KPIs, risks, and dependencies. - Lead stakeholder communications, executive updates, and operational readiness. - Track value realization throughout MVP → pilot → scale. 8) Product Management Mindset & Reusable IP Creation - Operate with product‑owner discipline for AI/ML platforms and automation at scale. - Manage backlog, MVP scope, pilot feedback loops, and release cadence. - Convert customer patterns into reusable IP—frameworks, automation packs, telemetry templates. Required Qualifications: • Experience: 10–15+ years in Digital Workplace, end user computing, or managed services; 5+ years leading complex, multi tower transformation programs with executive visibility. Preferred Qualifications: • AI & Automation: Practical knowledge of Predictive AI, Generative AI, and Agentic patterns; proven ability to frame use cases, risks, and controls for executive decision making. • Financial Acumen: Strong command of business case development, P&L impact modeling, TCO/ROI, and cloud economics; ability to map IT investment to business value. • Workshops & Co Creation: Exceptional facilitation skills (discovery, value, design, governance) with a record of co creating roadmaps and gaining stakeholder alignment. • Solution Depth: Hands on familiarity with modern deployment, UEM, collaboration, identity/security, DEX/experience analytics (Lenovo xIQ or equivalent), and service desk modernization. • Product Mindset: Experience owning the lifecycle of a complex, data intensive enterprise product (AI/ML platform or large scale automation preferred). • Industry Exposure: Direct experience in healthcare, retail, and/or telco, with ability to adapt patterns to domain outcomes. • Communication: Outstanding executive storytelling, negotiation, and written/verbal communication across business and technical audiences. • Leadership: Mentors senior consultants/architects; influences cross functional teams; collaborates across Sales, Offering, Delivery, and Partners. We follow a friendly hybrid model with three days a week in the office—great for collaboration and connection! We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Prioritize AI and automation use cases, guiding rollout into proactive support and self‑heal scenarios using Lenovo xIQ. 5) Deep Process Discovery & FDE‑Driven Product Goal Creation - Run hands‑on discovery using workshops, telemetry, analytics, and frontline shadowing. - Translate discovery insights into product goals, user stories, and measurable outcomes. - Shape MVPs, automation packs, and roadmap increments with DWS, xIQ, and Offering teams. - Validate hypotheses through customer‑embedded prototypes and pilots. 6) Solution Architecture & Hands‑On Orchestration - Convert business outcomes into end‑to‑end architecture across DWS, DaaS, UEM/Autopilot, collaboration, security, and modern support. - Act as forward‑deployed orchestrator during MVP: configure telemetry, align identity/security, tune automation rules, validate xIQ dashboards. - Partner with architects and delivery to ensure feasibility, scalability, and optimization. 7) Program Governance, Change Leadership & Value Tracking - Establish program governance with milestones, KPIs, risks, and dependencies. - Lead stakeholder communications, executive updates, and operational readiness. - Track value realization throughout MVP → pilot → scale. 8) Product Management Mindset & Reusable IP Creation - Operate with product‑owner discipline for AI/ML platforms and automation at scale. - Manage backlog, MVP scope, pilot feedback loops, and release cadence. - Convert customer patterns into reusable IP—frameworks, automation packs, telemetry templates. requirements: Procurement Strategy Project Manager This role is ideal for professionals who thrive in highly complex, cross-functional environments and enjoy leading strategic initiatives across engineering, product development, supply chain, and procurement functions. You will serve as a key program leader, influencing product decisions from concept through mass production while ensuring cost competitiveness, supply readiness, and business success. What You'll Do Lead Strategic Cross-Functional Programs Act as the Procurement Program Lead within new product development teams, partnering closely with Engineering, Product Management, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Sales, Marketing, and Procurement stakeholders. Drive alignment across diverse teams to ensure products are delivered on schedule, meet business objectives, and achieve operational readiness. Lead discussions, facilitate decision-making, and influence stakeholders without direct authority in a highly matrixed global environment. Own Product Commercial & Supply Strategy Shape sourcing and supply strategies throughout the product lifecycle, from concept development to production ramp. Provide strategic guidance on: Product cost structure Supplier ecosystem strategy Multi-sourcing approaches Supply continuity planning Commercial risk mitigation Influence product design and sourcing decisions that improve competitiveness, flexibility, and long-term scalability. Drive New Product Introduction (NPI) Partner with product and engineering teams during the entire NPI lifecycle. Analyze the impact of design changes, market conditions, and supply constraints on program execution. Ensure procurement and supply chain readiness while supporting rapid product evolution in fast-moving AI and data center environments. Manage Complex Stakeholder Ecosystems Coordinate priorities across multiple teams with competing objectives. Resolve conflicts, drive trade-off decisions, and build consensus among technical and business stakeholders. Act as a trusted advisor who brings together diverse perspectives to achieve the best outcome for the program. Anticipate Risk & Enable Execution Excellence Identify potential supply, supplier, commercial, and execution risks early in the development cycle. Work cross-functionally to develop mitigation plans that protect project milestones and launch commitments. Maintain agility and execution discipline in a rapidly changing technology landscape. What Makes You Successful We're looking for someone who combines strong program management capability with strategic business thinking. Core Competencies Cross-functional Program Management Stakeholder Management & Influence Executive Communication Conflict Resolution & Negotiation Preferred Experience You may come from backgrounds such as: Technical Program Management Product Development Program Management Engineering Program Management NPI Program Management Supply Chain Program Management Strategic Sourcing Program Management Procurement Program Management Additional Requirements Comfortable influencing stakeholders without formal authority. Experience leading initiatives involving multiple global teams. Strong English communication skills with the confidence to lead discussions in international settings. Comfortable working across global time zones and collaborating with teams worldwide. Able to thrive in fast-paced environments where priorities and requirements continuously evolve. Why This Role is Different This is not a traditional buyer or commodity procurement role. You will function as a strategic program leader, helping shape next-generation AI platforms by connecting technology, business strategy, supplier ecosystems, and global execution teams. You'll work at the intersection of innovation, product development, and commercial strategy—driving decisions that directly impact Lenovo's AI and data center growth journey. Additional
