requirements: Business Transformation Project Manager – Mexico At Lenovo, the Business Transformation Project Manager is responsible for leading and executing strategic business transformation initiatives across Mexico, ensuring end-to-end delivery with measurable impact on business performance, operational excellence, and organizational effectiveness. This role works closely with cross-functional local and global teams—including Sales, Product Management, Engineering, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Human Resources, and other business functions—to translate the business strategy for Mexico into actionable projects. The position drives structural change, process optimization, and the adoption of new ways of working in a fast-paced, highly competitive environment. The Business Transformation Project Manager serves as a key change enabler and the central point of coordination between local and global stakeholders, ensuring strategic alignment, disciplined execution, and the delivery of sustainable business value for the Mexico organization. Key Responsibilities Lead business transformation initiatives in Mexico from definition through implementation, execution, and post-implementation follow-up, ensuring end-to-end project delivery. Translate Mexico business priorities into actionable projects with measurable business impact. Define project scope, objectives, timelines, resource requirements, KPIs, and deliverables aligned with both local and global business goals. Coordinate cross-functional local and global teams, fostering collaboration, accountability, and execution excellence. Manage key stakeholders across local, regional, and global leadership, ensuring alignment, visibility, and timely decision-making. Identify opportunities for business transformation, including process optimization, organizational design, go-to-market improvements, and operational efficiency, and convert them into actionable initiatives. Proactively identify project risks, dependencies, and roadblocks, developing mitigation plans to ensure successful execution. Monitor project progress, ensuring milestones, KPIs, timelines, and business objectives are achieved. Prepare and deliver executive-level presentations and reports focused on business outcomes and value creation rather than activity tracking. Support organizational change management by facilitating communication, stakeholder engagement, and adoption across all levels of the organization. Ensure compliance with project management methodologies, governance standards, and best practices. Drive continuous improvement, operational excellence, and process simplification within the context of the Mexican market. Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Finance, or a related field. A Master's degree (MBA or equivalent) is preferred. 5+ years of experience in project management, business transformation, management consulting, strategy, or similar roles within multinational organizations. Proven experience leading business transformation or organizational change initiatives in local markets, preferably in Mexico or Latin America. Experience working in highly collaborative, cross-functional, and global environments. Strong knowledge of project management methodologies, including Agile, Scrum, Waterfall, or hybrid approaches. Demonstrated ability to manage multiple strategic initiatives simultaneously in fast-paced, ambiguous environments. Excellent communication, presentation, stakeholder management, and influencing skills, with the ability to engage senior executives. Advanced English proficiency (written and spoken). Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate data into strategic insights and business recommendations. Highly organized, results-oriented, and capable of driving execution with minimal supervision. We are equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as veteran and basis of disability or any other federal, state, or local protect class. responsibilities: • Provide day-to-day administrative support to the Chief of Staff and the HR Director for AI Ecosystem — calendaring, inbox triage, expense reporting, travel coordination, and document preparation. • Own end-to-end logistics for executive meetings, leadership offsites, and AIES team events — including space booking, AV setup, catering, and on-site coordination. • Manage device shipping logistics for executive demos and events — coordinating with IT, supply chain, and vendors to ensure equipment arrives on time and in working order. • Order, track, and manage inventory of Lenovo brand and marketing materials (giveaways, signage, swag) for internal and external events. • Project manage end-to-end logistics for major AIES events (CES / Tech World, MWC, IFA, and other flagship moments) — owning timelines, vendor contracts, shipping, and on-site execution. • Coordinate cross-functionally with Marketing, IT, Facilities, Travel, and external vendors to keep event workstreams on schedule. • Maintain master event calendars, run-of-show documents, vendor lists, and post-event recaps for the CoS Office. • Process purchase orders, vendor onboarding, invoices, and event budgets in partnership with Finance and Procurement. Position Description: This position is for a QA Automation Engineer in Lenovo's Imaging Technology Center (ITC), which manages large amounts of worldwide data for reporting, decision-making, and forecasting. The role involves designing, developing, and maintaining automated testing frameworks and solutions to ensure the quality and reliability of our applications. The ideal candidate must possess strong analytical skills and be highly self-driven to independently solve problems and drive automation initiatives. Position Requirements: Basic Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in computer science or engineering years of experience working in C# years of experience with Selenium for Web and Windows/Desktop automation years of experience in API automation using tools like RestSharp, Restfit etc.. Strong experience with BDD framework Cucumber / SpecFlow / Reqnroll for behavior-driven development (BDD). Proven experience designing and developing robust, high-quality test automation frameworks for the team. Skilled in leveraging AI-driven testing tools or AI assistants (e.g., GitHub Copilot ) to accelerate test case generation, write automation scripts, and optimize code quality. Experience using page object model patterns and reusable test framework architecture. Capable of analyzing comprehensive test reports ( Allure ), system logs, and screenshots to rapidly find, debug, and fix software defects. Hands-on experience with Azure DevOps for CI/CD pipeline integration of automated test suites Strong understanding of core software QA methodologies, test types (functional, regression, smoke, integration), and the complete Software Testing Life Cycle (STLC) Deep understanding of SOLID principles and architectural design patterns, with a proven ability to apply them to build clean, maintainable, and reusable automation code Experience with version control systems (Git, Azure Repos, GitHub) Preferred Qualifications: Familiarity with API testing tools ( Postman , Swagger ) Experience with Visual Studio , VS Code and debugging tools for test automation Good scripting skills in Javascript , PowerShell or other relevant languages Ability to collaborate closely with development and QA teams to integrate automation early in the SDLC Ability to define, measure, and improve test automation metrics, including code coverage, test execution time, pass/failure rates, and defect detection effectiveness Develop, implement, and maintain policies, procedures, and training plans for automation engineers Maintain and continuously improve upon deep technical expertise through training and constant exposure to evolving automation tools and best practices Soft Skills : Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail Self-reliant and self-driven individual who can work independently with minimal supervision Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) to collaborate with cross-functional teams Strong mentoring and training abilities to guide junior engineers Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment Additional

requirements: Principais Responsabilidades Gerenciar uma carteira estratégica de clientes B2B, sendo responsável pelo desenvolvimento de negócios, expansão de receita e retenção de clientes, garantindo o atingimento dos objetivos comerciais da Lenovo. Atuar como parceiro de negócios dos clientes, compreendendo seus desafios, identificando oportunidades de crescimento e propondo soluções alinhadas ao portfólio Lenovo. Desenvolver e executar planos de contas (Account Plans), definindo estratégias para crescimento de receita, aumento de participação e ampliação do relacionamento com clientes e parceiros. Conduzir negociações comerciais de média e alta complexidade, incluindo oportunidades de maior valor agregado, garantindo rentabilidade e alinhamento às políticas comerciais da companhia. Liderar o ciclo completo de vendas, desde a prospecção e qualificação de oportunidades até o fechamento, acompanhamento da entrega e pós-venda. Construir e manter um pipeline robusto e previsível, realizando gestão ativa das oportunidades e garantindo acuracidade das previsões de vendas (forecast). Trabalhar em conjunto com equipes internas (Produtos, Marketing, Operações, Supply Chain, Finanças e Serviços) para estruturar soluções que atendam às necessidades dos clientes e assegurem excelência na execução. Atuar como principal ponto de contato do cliente dentro da Lenovo, coordenando demandas internas e promovendo uma experiência diferenciada ao longo de toda a jornada comercial. Identificar oportunidades de cross-selling, upselling e renovação de negócios, ampliando a adoção do portfólio de soluções e serviços da Lenovo. Apoiar o Gerente de Contas na construção de estratégias comerciais para clientes estratégicos e grandes oportunidades, contribuindo com análises, recomendações e plano de execução. Monitorar indicadores de desempenho, tendências de mercado, movimentação da concorrência e comportamento dos clientes, propondo ações para maximizar resultados. Liderar iniciativas de melhoria contínua dos processos comerciais, compartilhando boas práticas e contribuindo para o desenvolvimento da equipe. Apoiar a integração e desenvolvimento técnico de profissionais menos experientes, atuando como referência em processos comerciais, ferramentas e melhores práticas. Requisitos Experiência consolidada em vendas B2B e gestão de carteira de clientes, preferencialmente no segmento de tecnologia. Vivência na condução de vendas consultivas e transacionais, com foco em geração de valor para o cliente. Experiência na gestão do ciclo completo de vendas, incluindo prospecção, negociação, elaboração de propostas comerciais, forecast, acompanhamento de pipeline e fechamento de negócios. Histórico consistente de atingimento ou superação de metas comerciais. Capacidade de desenvolver relacionamentos de longo prazo com clientes, atuando de forma consultiva e orientada ao negócio. Habilidade para conduzir negociações de média e alta complexidade com diferentes níveis de interlocutores. Perfil analítico, com capacidade para interpretar indicadores comerciais e transformá-los em planos de ação. Forte capacidade de organização, priorização e gestão simultânea de múltiplas oportunidades. Experiência em atuação multifuncional, colaborando com diferentes áreas para entrega de soluções ao cliente. Conhecimento do mercado de TI e das principais tendências em infraestrutura, dispositivos e soluções corporativas será considerado um diferencial. Inglês intermediário , com capacidade para participar de reuniões, treinamentos, utilizar documentação corporativa e interagir com equipes globais. Responsável por administrar uma carteira de clientes B2B de forma a se atingir os objetivos de venda estabelecidos da Lenovo, suportando o Gerente de contas. • Atingir as metas quantitativas e qualitativas estabelecidas. • Criar, planejar e executar as atividades comerciais relativas aos clientes, como planejamento de vendas, pipeline, acompanhamento de entregas de produtos, execução de campanhas de marketing, gerenciamento de estoque etc. • Garantir disponibilidade para o atendimento de ligações dos clientes. • Fazer o trabalho de backoffice (propostas, cotações, colocação de pedidos). • Cooperar com a área de Produtos da Lenovo para assegurar que a gama de produtos corresponde às necessidades do mercado em termos de características e competitividade. • Monitorar vendas, fornecer relatórios de para a gestão de vendas e tomar medidas adequadas para evitar situações de excesso ou falta de produto. • Alinhar com o time de Operações da Lenovo sobre as demandas de grandes deals a fim de adequar a entrega ao prazo necessário. • Contribuir, utilizando a experiência acumulada, na melhoria e otimização dos processos do ciclo de vendas junto às outras áreas da Lenovo. • Ajudar no desenvolvimento e aprofundamento do relacionamento com os clientes, criando uma relação de vínculo e confiança, e atuando como responsável por endereçar suas necessidades e solicitações dentro da Lenovo (OwnerShip). • Conduzir de maneira autônoma em processos de negociações e vendas transacionais (Run Rate) e no desenvolvimento de contas inativas ou com pouco relacionamento. • Dependendo da complexidade do componente a ser negociado e do processo de venda envolvida, é possível que haja necessidades de conhecimentos técnicos específicos. requirements: Descriptions & Requirements: This position will be reporting into the Lenovo’s Solutions and Services Group as part of the Global SSG Channel team driving the Channel & SMB business for Asia-Pacific (AP) The ideal candidate earns trust through performance and relationship building, thinks strategically and analytically about business challenges, has experience in business management and has a deep analytic background to assist the enablement of support services and New Go-To-Market Services & Solution Offerings for the SMB Segment & Channel Route to Market. The candidate will be a self-starter with a bias towards independent problem solving, a passion for identifying and eliminating bottlenecks, and has the foresight to anticipate business needs, make tradeoffs, and balance business dynamics despite constraints. The ideal candidate will thrive in a fast-paced and innovative environment, will be able to manage through ambiguity and complexity, and constantly seek ways to invent and simplify business processes. Maturity, strong judgment, and the ability to influence are all essential to success in this role. This position will work with stakeholders supporting the Support Services products development teams and ESG organizations, such as Sales, Service Operations, Business Development, Marketing, Business Transformation, IT, and the Channel team in Geos. The individual must have the ability to communicate effectively across multiple technical and non-technical business units, as well as across various markets within Geo. requirements: We are currently looking for a Sales Operations Analyst that is a central function that governs the sales operations of CEECA infrastructure solutions market. requirements: We are looking for Services and Solutions Training Manager for AP region. Learning and upskilling are a key area of focus for SSG and Lenovo. As we are moving to a more services and solution driven business, sellers will need to change their approach to a consultative sales methodology. The offers are much more customizable, so sellers need to learn about the different solution building blocks and how those can deliver business outcomes to our customers. Sellers need to address new personas in their account, learn to customize their messaging and must work with more stakeholders internally, requiring usage of new process and tools. We also need to train our partner account sales reps to sell to partners and train sellers of these partners. What You will Do: Develop and implement quarterly AP training plans by sales persona, aligned with organizational needs and global strategy. Act as the single point of contact (SPOC) for AP sales management, working closely with local enablement, global training teams, and sales leaders. Set learning objectives, select methodologies, manage content and trainers, and oversee logistics, budget, and KPIs for learning initiatives. Ensure sellers and partners are equipped to sell services and solutions through consultative approaches, addressing new personas and using updated tools. Measure training effectiveness, track consumption, and continuously improve programs based on feedback and performance data. Train partner account managers and partner sellers to effectively position and sell Lenovo’s service offerings. Participate in shaping the global training strategy and innovate with new approaches, including AI-supported learning experiences. Position Requirements: Ability to design and deliver sales-focused training programs tailored to business needs and continuously improve learning experiences. Skilled in various techniques including e-learning, workshops, role plays, and instructional design. Strong understanding of how adults learn to ensure training is engaging and effective. Experience in the IT sector, with familiarity in high-tech sales or services environments. Excellent presentation skills and the ability to lead, motivate, and support trainers and trainees. Capable of managing multiple learning programs, schedules, and operational tasks efficiently. Comfortable working in fast-paced environments with flexibility, dependability, and a creative mindset. BA/BS degree with 3+ years in training, curriculum design, and working with management and decision-makers. 3+ years using different training methodologies, incl ownership for F2F trainings (incl AI is a plus.) Additional

requirements: Responsibilities 1. Core Architecture & Delivery: Lead backend architecture design, core development, and delivery for AI & Agent projects. On-Device Integration & Optimization: Collaborate with core teams (e.g., Memory & Knowledge, Orchestration, Agent Runtime) to integrate AI modules (e.g., inference, Agent workflow, RAG, memory/context, Agent orchestration) on device. Ensure high-availability packaging and ultra-low-resource optimization. 2. Cross-Platform & Performance: Build cross-platform (Windows/Android) AI SDKs/apps, with a focus on memory, power, and latency optimization for mobile. 3. Engineering Efficiency: Write technical docs and API specs. Build and maintain automated testing and CI/CD pipelines to ensure quality and efficiency in cross-platform delivery. 4. Tech Leadership: Stay current with AI advancements. Mentor junior/mid-level engineers. Experience: 5-8+ years in software development, with proven 0-to-1 delivery of complex projects. Core Technical Skills Deep Go Mastery: Master Go core syntax and features, with deep understanding of Goroutine scheduling (G-M-P), Channel communication, GC principles & tuning, and memory allocation (TCMalloc & on-device leak prevention). Go + AI/Agent Experience: Hands-on experience building/integrating LLM backends, Agent Runtimes, or RAG systems with Go. Experience in extreme memory optimization (OOM protection, defragmentation) and CPU/GPU inference efficiency. Familiar with on-device inference engines (e.g., llama.cpp, ONNX Runtime, CoreML, ExecuTorch). High-quality contributions to Go/Rust/AI open-source projects on GitHub. Cloud-native familiarity with Server-side AI or large-scale distributed systems experience. Knowledge of Kubernetes, gRPC, service mesh, and able to adapt to Enterprise AI expansion. Good product sense (AI-friendly design) and HCI understanding for AI products. Additional

requirements: Senior Associate, Legal – Privacy (Marketing & E-Commerce) In this role, you will join a dynamic Legal and Privacy team focused on shaping how customer data is used responsibly across digital marketing and e-commerce channels. You will play a key part in ensuring that innovative marketing and advertising activities are built on strong, compliant, and privacy-conscious foundations. This is a highly impactful position where legal expertise meets digital innovation. You will work closely with marketing, e-commerce, and technical teams to enable data-driven growth while ensuring compliance with global privacy regulations. Your work will directly influence how customer trust, transparency, and privacy are maintained across online experiences. We are looking for someone who enjoys working at the intersection of law, technology, and digital business—someone who can translate complex privacy requirements into practical, business-friendly solutions. What You'll Do Advise marketing and e-commerce teams on privacy matters related to digital advertising, customer data usage, cross-device tracking, retargeting, email marketing, and social media campaigns. Review, draft, and negotiate privacy and data protection agreements with ad tech partners, analytics providers, marketing platforms, and data collaboration/cleanroom vendors. Develop and maintain privacy documentation including cookie banners, consent flows, privacy notices, and opt-out mechanisms. Ensure compliance with global privacy frameworks such as GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, ePrivacy Directive, and emerging digital regulation. Support the implementation and optimisation of Consent Management Platforms (CMPs) and user preference management tools across digital properties. Partner with regional legal teams, data governance, and engineering stakeholders to embed privacy-by-design principles into marketing and e-commerce initiatives. Monitor regulatory developments, enforcement trends, and industry standards impacting digital marketing and advertising technologies. Deliver privacy training and create clear, practical guidance and best practices for marketing, product, and digital teams. Act as a trusted advisor, helping teams balance innovation, customer experience, and privacy compliance. What We're Looking For Qualified lawyer (JD or equivalent) with active licence to practise law in relevant jurisdictions. Minimum 5 years of legal experience with a strong focus on privacy and data protection. Proven experience working with digital marketing, ad tech, e-commerce, or technology-driven businesses (in-house or law firm experience welcome). Strong understanding of global privacy laws and how they apply to online behavioural advertising and customer data ecosystems. Hands-on experience with ad tech environments, including cookies, pixels, DMPs, CDPs, identity resolution, and real-time bidding ecosystems. Familiarity with privacy technologies such as Consent Management Platforms (CMPs) and user preference management tools. Ability to translate complex legal and regulatory requirements into clear, actionable guidance for non-legal stakeholders. Strong communication, stakeholder management, and influencing skills across technical and business teams. Privacy certifications such as CIPP/E, CIPP/US, CIPM (or equivalent) are a strong advantage. What We Offer You Life Insurance Private Medical Insurance Pension Plan Multisport Card Employee Assistance Program (health, legal & financial consultancy support) Access to Internal E-Learning Development Platform We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: ***Please Note: This is a fully remote role; however, international travel is expected to collaborate with global R&D labs, business units, and pilot customers.*** About Lenovo CTO Org Lenovo CTO Org is the cutting-edge R&D and technology incubation organization of Lenovo, dedicated to advancing information technology, smart devices, and services. Our mission is to transform breakthrough innovations into scalable businesses, powering Lenovo’s most advanced products and shaping the future of AI, edge computing, intelligent devices, and enterprise solutions. About the Role This is a hands-on technical role within the AI Solution Delivery organization of Lenovo CTO Org’s Tech Incubation team, reporting to the Director, AI Solution Delivery. You will be responsible for deploying and operationalizing AI-powered solutions in real customer environments. Working closely with Product Management, Solution Architects, R&D teams, and customers, you will bridge the gap between prototype and production by implementing, integrating, tuning, and validating solutions on-site. Success in this role requires a combination of software engineering, applied AI, systems integration, and customer-facing problem-solving. You must be comfortable working directly with customers, troubleshooting complex technical issues, and adapting solutions to real-world operating conditions. This role requires extensive travel and extended customer-site engagements, which may reach 70–80% depending on project needs and incubation phase. Key Responsibilities: AI Solution Deployment & Integration Deploy AI-powered solutions in customer environments, including edge computing platforms, software applications, sensors, and supporting infrastructure. Integrate solutions with customer IT and operational systems, APIs, databases, and enterprise platforms. Develop and maintain integration code, automation scripts, and deployment tooling using Python and related technologies. Assess customer environments and ensure deployment readiness, connectivity, and system compatibility. Applied AI & Solution Optimization Configure, tune, validate, and troubleshoot Computer Vision and AI models in production environments. Analyze field performance, identify root causes of accuracy issues, and optimize solution performance for real-world operating conditions. Collect and analyze deployment data to support continuous improvement and model refinement. Partner with R&D teams to communicate field findings and accelerate product maturity. Customer Delivery & Technical Leadership Serve as the primary technical lead for customer deployment activities, driving implementation execution and coordinating with architects, product managers, R&D teams, and customer stakeholders. Lead complex technical issue resolution and coordinate cross-functional engineering teams when escalations are required. Conduct customer acceptance testing and validate deployment success criteria. Train customer teams on solution operation, best practices, and troubleshooting procedures. Field Documentation & Knowledge Management Create and maintain installation guides, deployment documentation, configuration records, and troubleshooting playbooks. Document lessons learned and best practices to improve future deployments and accelerate solution scalability. Feedback Loop & Product Hardening Serve as the critical feedback channel to the core R&D team. Translate on-site deployment issues, hardware limitations, integration challenges, and algorithmic edge cases into structured, actionable engineering insights. Capture and distill delivery experience into optimization recommendations for both software and hardware components, relaying timely feedback to Solution Architects and the R&D team. Compliance, Data Privacy & IP Protection Adhere to customer safety, security, privacy, and compliance requirements during all deployment activities. Protect customer data and Lenovo intellectual property in accordance with company policies, contractual obligations, and applicable regulations. What We Look For Hands-on problem solver who enjoys working at the intersection of software, AI, systems integration, and customer deployment. Strong customer orientation and ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfortable operating in fast-moving, ambiguous environments where solutions are evolving rapidly. Highly self-directed with strong ownership and accountability. Passion for transforming innovative technologies into real-world customer success. Required Qualifications: 5+ years of experience in solution deployment, systems integration, software engineering, field implementation, or related technical roles. Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, AI/ML, or a similar degree. Practical experience deploying, tuning, validating, and troubleshooting Computer Vision and AI-based solutions in production environments. Demonstrated ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues across software, infrastructure, and deployed systems. Preferred Qualifications: Strong communication and presentation skills with customer-facing experience. Experience in manufacturing, industrial automation, computer vision, AI platforms, or intelligent edge solutions is preferred. Willingness to travel extensively and support long-term customer-site deployments globally. Mandarin language proficiency is a plus. The base salary range budgeted for this position is $180k - $235k. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission. Lenovo’s various

requirements: Position Summary The Senior Manager, Sales Operations is responsible for supporting business performance management, forecasting, reporting, and operational execution across the Asia Pacific SSG organization. The role partners closely with AP sales leaders, finance teams, and Worldwide stakeholders to provide business insights, drive operational discipline, and ensure effective execution of sales strategies. This position plays a critical role in managing the regional business cadence, delivering actionable analytics, and supporting key growth initiatives across AI, Hybrid Cloud (HCS) Digital Workplace Solutions (DWS), Sustainability (SUS), and other SSG offerings. Key Responsibilities Business Management & Reporting Support and drive AP SSG Business Management System (BMS), Monthly Business Reviews (MBR) and leadership reporting. Prepare business performance analysis, identify risks and opportunities, and provide recommendations to leadership. Ensure consistency and accuracy of regional reporting and metrics. Forecasting & Pipeline Management Drive forecast consolidation and review processes across AP geographies. Monitor pipeline health, booking trends, and forecast accuracy. Partner with regional sales and operations teams to improve pipeline quality and execution discipline. Sales Analytics & Insights Develop and maintain executive dashboards and performance reports. Analyze business trends, growth drivers, and operational gaps to support decision-making. Provide regular insights on key metrics including Bookings, Revenue, PCON, and strategic programs. Planning & Operational Support Support annual planning cycles, quota deployment, and sales performance tracking. Coordinate with Finance, WW Operations, and regional teams to ensure alignment on targets and business assumptions. Assist with operational governance and business process improvements. requirements: The Senior Pre-Sales Solution Architect, Hybrid Cloud & AI is a customer-facing technical expert responsible for designing, positioning, and supporting Lenovo’s Hybrid Cloud and AI solutions across enterprise customer engagements. You will partner closely with sales, delivery, and solution teams to translate customer needs into technically sound, business-aligned architectures that drive successful outcomes. Operating as part of the regional pre-sales organization, you play a critical part in deal shaping, solution design, and technical credibility - supporting complex opportunities from early discovery through proposal and close. Your Key Responsibilities: Customer & Sales Engagement Partner with regional sales teams to support qualified opportunities and strategic accounts. Engage directly with enterprise customers to understand business requirements, technical environments, and desired outcomes. Act as a trusted technical advisor throughout the sales cycle, from discovery through solution validation. Solution Architecture & Design Design Hybrid Cloud and AI architectures incorporating infrastructure, cloud, data, and AI technologies. Develop solution concepts, architectures, and technical designs aligned to customer needs and Lenovo capabilities. Ensure proposed solutions are scalable, resilient, secure, and commercially viable. Pre-Sales Execution Support proposal development including technical responses, architectures, and solution narratives. Participate in customer presentations, technical workshops, and solution reviews. Collaborate with delivery and engineering teams to ensure smooth handoff from pre-sales to implementation. Deal Quality & Governance Ensure technical accuracy, feasibility, and clarity in all customer-facing materials. Support deal reviews and presales governance processes as required. Balance customer requirements with delivery realities and commercial considerations. Scope & Working Model Individual contributor role with no direct people management responsibilities. Work under the guidance of the Regional Director, Hybrid Cloud & AI Pre-Sales. Support multiple opportunities and accounts within an assigned region. High level of customer interaction; the role is predominantly external-facing. What you bring 7 - 12 years of experience in pre-sales, solution architecture, technical consulting, or related roles. Strong hands-on experience with Hybrid Cloud, AI, infrastructure, and enterprise architectures. Experience supporting complex enterprise sales engagements. Ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences. Why This Role Matters This role is essential to Lenovo’s ability to deliver credible, differentiated Hybrid Cloud & AI solutions to enterprise customers. As a Senior Pre-Sales Solution Architect, you directly influence deal success, customer satisfaction, and Lenovo’s reputation in the market by ensuring every solution is technically sound, business-aligned, and execution-ready. requirements: XClarity One is Lenovo’s cloud-native systems management platform for operating data center infrastructure , including servers and storage devices, at enterprise scale. It unifies hardware, firmware, OS, and virtualization management into a single SaaS experience built for hybrid environments. The platform integrates deeply with VMware and Windows to enable consistent lifecycle management, observability, and automation. Engineers working on XClarity One collaborate closely with real data center equipment and have hands-on access to the Lenovo lab in Bucharest, Romania . This role offers direct exposure to production-grade hardware and the systems that run modern data centers. Mandatory skills Extensive architectural leadership experience – proven ability to design and deliver complex software systems with significant architectural ownership, cross-team technical leadership , and end-to-end responsibility (from design through production). Expertise in enterprise infrastructure integration – deep understanding of virtualization and IT systems management (particularly VMware vSphere / vCenter or Microsoft Windows ecosystems), enabling you to build and integrate these platforms within the XClarity One SaaS environment. Strong back-end development skills – exceptional proficiency in Golang and/or Node.js (e.g. using Express or similar frameworks, with a track record of writing clean, production-quality code. Distributed systems & API design – hands-on experience architecting large-scale distributed services and microservices (including robust API design & contracts, event-driven architectures , and asynchronous processing ). Performance, scalability & reliability – in-depth knowledge of performance optimization, caching strategies (e.g. using Redis/DragonflyDB), concurrency, and designing for high availability and reliability at scale; skilled in troubleshooting and optimizing complex, high-performance systems. Engineering best practices & mentorship – history of rigorous code reviews , driving coding standards , and mentoring other engineers; a champion of maintainable, secure, well-documented, and well-tested code. AI-native development proficiency – comfortable embracing AI-assisted development tools and workflows: regularly using AI coding assistants (e.g. GitHub Copilot, Claude) to boost development speed and quality. Able to write clear specs and SKILL definitions (Markdown) to guide AI tools, and manage AI prompt tokens/context efficiently to achieve reliable, secure outcomes. Desired skills Cloud & SaaS experience – background in building or operating multi-tenant, cloud-based services or large-scale enterprise SaaS platforms; familiarity with major cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud). Broad virtualization knowledge – familiarity with additional hypervisor or infrastructure platforms (e.g. Proxmox, Nutanix, SUSE Harvester or similar) and eagerness to quickly learn new ecosystems. DevOps and automation – experience with DevOps practices and tools for continuous integration/delivery (CI/CD pipelines, automated testing) and infrastructure as code or GitOps workflows. Experience with local containerization solutions for development like Docker or container.d . Experience with Kubernetes as a user. Bonus skills Polyglot capabilities – proficiency with additional programming languages (e.g. Python ) and scripting ( Bash ), demonstrating versatility to work across multiple technology stacks. Basic understanding of networking skills, from IP families, to subnets, to routing in a Unix system. Advanced platform tools – hands-on experience with modern platform and operations technologies such as ArgoCD (or similar CI/CD automation), Grafana/Prometheus for monitoring, and identity/authentication frameworks like Keycloak is a strong plus.

Experiences Team - Job Description Job Description Summary Motorola software development philosophy is based on few key foundations: - Building on a foundation of pure Android - Software experiences that complement Android, not compete with it - Ensuring software brings value to the consumer - Constantly improve software experiences on the released phones by faster android upgrades and Motorola apps updates At Motorola’s Bangalore Software R&D Innovation Center, we use our talent and passion to help the company build world-class experiences and products for our users on the foundation of Android, the world’s best mobile operating system. Working at Motorola’s Bangalore Software R&D Innovation Center is an opportunity to be at the cutting edge of mobile software development. Being a part of Motorola Innovations and Experiences team in India, as a software development engineer, you'll play a pivotal role in crafting unique and value-driven software experiences across a range of Motorola devices, including smartphones, wearables (TWS earbuds/Watch/Glasses), and internet services. You will have an opportunity to develop signature experiences and internet services harnessing cutting-edge LLM (Large Language Models) AI-based technologies. Additionally, there will be an opportunity to build next-gen experiences in the technology areas of AR/VR/UWB/Fintech leveraging AI/ML Job Description Responsibilities: The successful candidate will be a highly motivated self-starter with experience in realizing the requirements for products. Strong desire for innovation, inter-group coordination and teamwork skills are must. Job responsibilities include, but are not limited to the following: Android Application development: Job responsibilities also include Android application/native software development across different subsystems of mobile device, requirements definition & planning, design, test, debug and competitive analysis. On-Device AI (Artificial Intelligence) Integration: Implement on-device AI frameworks and libraries to enable real-time AI processing, ensuring efficient utilization of device resources. Off-Device AI Infrastructure: Architect and maintain scalable infrastructure for off-device AI processing, leveraging cloud services and distributed computing techniques. Performance Optimization: Optimize AI algorithms and workflows to achieve optimal performance on mobile devices while balancing computational resources and battery life. Collaboration: Collaborate with product managers, designers, and other engineers to ideate and implement AI-powered features that enhance user experiences. Research and Innovation : Stay updated with the latest advancements in AI technologies and contribute to research initiatives aimed at driving innovation in mobile AI solutions. Candidate is responsible for requirements analysis, technical feasibility evaluation, development, delivery of the features and support till end of product life. Candidates must ensure seamless integration of software updates, enabling faster Android upgrades and timely Motorola apps updates. Candidate is responsible for creating new experiences on smart phones, which help consumers worldwide enrich their digital lives. These are in the space of cool innovative software-hardware pairings. Requirements: Essential: BE / MS / MTech with CGP 3.2+/4 in the field of Computer Science/Engineering or related field Experience of 5+ years in Android software development. Experience with development of Android applications and enhancement of Android framework components, to enhance user experience. Strong Coding knowledge with Java, Kotlin, Android Studio. Well versed with Object-oriented Analysis & Design, Design Patterns, Data Structures and Algorithms. Strong problem analysis and debugging skills with passion for innovation and a drive to create impactful software solutions. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) definition, analysis and improvement. Proven track record in delivering consumer-focused software experiences. Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to thrive in a dynamic, collaborative environment. Uses AI tools usage as a core part of their engineering lifecycle to achieve 3x–5x leverage. Hands-on experience using advanced AI coding tools like Gemini in Android Studio, or CLI-based LLM agents , Cursor, Github Copilot . Mastery of prompt engineering to generate clean, idiomatic Kotlin code with efficient design patterns. Ability to efficiently manage LLM context (managing files, referencing symbols, using system instructions). Expert at using AI to generate comprehensive U nit tests ( Junit, MockK, JaCoCo ) and using LLMs to rapidly diagnose complex stack traces or memory leaks. Strong awareness of the risks of AI-generated code, including: * Identifying and fixing "hallucinated" APIs or deprecated methods. * Ensuring AI-generated code strictly adheres to security standards (avoiding hardcoded secrets, protecting PII). * Understanding licensing implications of generated code snippets. Desirable: Experience in publishing Android Apps to the Google Play Store Working experience with Git/Jira Knowledge of web services, REST APIs (application programming interfaces) Knowledge of interfacing with Google Cloud Services / Amazon Web services. Knowledge of Windows based application development Strong sense of System Architecture as it relates to mobile apps Familiarity with AI/ML technologies and their application in software development. Additional

Join Lenovo’s Solutions & Services Group (SSG) and help organizations unlock the full potential of AI-driven transformation. As businesses increasingly invest in Digital Workplace, Data Center, and next-generation AI solutions, Lenovo is uniquely positioned to help customers turn innovation into measurable business outcomes. This is an exciting opportunity to work at the forefront of Lenovo’s AI and Services strategy, partnering with customers on complex transformation initiatives while helping them navigate evolving technology landscapes. You will collaborate with sales teams, solution experts, partners, and business leaders to drive meaningful impact and accelerate growth across the Services business. As a One Lenovo Services Sales Executive for AI Solutions you will be responsible for driving services and solutions primarily in SMB and Midmarket segments and will report to our Senior Manager Solution Sales DACH. What You Will Do: Lead AI-focused services opportunities across Digital Workplace and Data Center environments, helping customers accelerate their digital and AI transformation journeys. Partner with account teams to identify, qualify, and develop strategic opportunities, driving pipeline growth, new business, and revenue expansion across existing and new customers. Position Lenovo’s Hybrid AI Advantage portfolio by connecting customer challenges with innovative AI-enabled solutions and measurable business outcomes. Build trusted relationships with customers, including executive stakeholders, developing a deep understanding of their business priorities and long-term objectives. Shape and close AI-driven use cases such as automation, predictive analytics, digital assistants, and AI infrastructure services in collaboration with internal and partner ecosystems. Bring together Lenovo experts, business partners, and cross-functional teams to design high-value solutions that maximize outcomes for both customers and Lenovo. Act as a trusted advisor throughout the customer journey, articulating the value, ROI, and strategic impact of AI adoption while ensuring expectations are aligned from solution design through delivery. Drive complex opportunities through governance processes, ensuring strong commercial outcomes while contributing to pipeline growth, revenue generation, new customer acquisition, and account expansion. Capability to act as a trusted advisor, helping customers navigate digital and AI-driven transformation from strategy through execution. Experience leading large-scale IT initiatives, including consulting, transformational services, solution design, implementation, or complex technology projects. Proven track record of leading and closing complex sales opportunities involving multiple stakeholders, teams, and solution areas. Strong understanding of AI and Generative AI technologies, including enterprise use cases across Digital Workplace, Data Center, automation, AI infrastructure, and data-driven services. Familiarity with AI ecosystems and leading technology partners (e.g., Microsoft, AWS, Google), combined with knowledge of data, security, governance, compliance, and broader business transformation strategies. Professional fluency in German and English What Lenovo Can Offer You: Employee Share Purchase Plan Employee Assistance Program, e.g., for health, legal & financial consultancy Pension Plan Meal Allowance / Lunch Vouchers Internal E-learning Development Platform Available for Employees Specialized Development Trainings (based on nomination process) Employees Groups (LGBT+, WILL, etc.) Opportunity to Join/Create Employees Groups (inclusivity, well-being, sports, volunteering, charity, etc.) Job Rad (Bike Leasing) Mobile phone + 3 Sim Cards for Mobile Working Hybrid Working Model * Lenovo’s inclusive culture is shaped by strong voices and real stories — discover our Women@Lenovo here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZwcbxS0KS-w Please apply via our online tool with your CV (including cover letter and references). What you'll do: The position is for a Senior Hardware Development Engineer in the Lenovo Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), based in Morrisville, North Carolina. This is an exciting role in the ThinkSystem Server Development organization that has a strong focus to be the #1 trusted IT Partner in the Datacenter market. The Senior engineer will be looked upon to lead a set of highly motivated engineers to develop innovative and complex server designs to meet customer and business needs. These organic designs provide exposure to the latest technologies from Lenovo and its development partners to meet market demands. The role also entails solving unique client challenges through the custom development of solutions and direct engagement with customers. The candidate will be tasked to lead a team of engineers and engage with architecture and executive leaders in the development organization to design and deliver the ISG portfolio’s next generation of servers. Required skills: Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical/Computer Engineering. At least 10 years of in-depth experience in x86 or AMD architecture and hardware design. At least 10 years of experience in EDA schematic design tools for PCB schematic creation, support Physical design activity and then define/perform low level hardware validation. Experience in leading engineers/team wrt technical direction and product development. Ability to triage and resolve complex hardware issues and provide guidance to other engineers who are assigned tasks related to the problem investigation. Strong organizational skills to lead server subsystem designs with a team of highly motivated engineers that deliver innovative and challenging server designs. Preferred skills: Master’s Degree in Electrical/Computer Engineering Server design experience is strongly preferred. Previous experience in FPGA code development and validation, DC Power circuit design, and/or PCIe bus design, simulation, and validation. Proficiency in Linux/Windows Operating systems. Experience with OS virtualization is a plus. Strong proficiency in shell scripting (BASH/Perl/Python and Windows Power Shell) preferred. Demonstrated leadership on a project with engineering team members located in multiple geographies. Ability to provide mentorship to other engineers and support the growth and development of engineering talent applied to new technologies. Business acumen to engage with various functional teams required to take projects from concept to production. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Position Summary The Senior Manager, CMMC Program Governance serves as Lenovo’s enterprise authority for the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Level 2 program supporting North American Federal sales. This role holds full accountability for the governance, compliance posture, audit readiness, and sustained operation of Lenovo’s CMMC‑scoped enclave environment. This position functions as the authoritative owner of the CMMC System Security Plan (SSP) and the final decision authority for enclave boundary definition, control scope, and risk acceptance. The role ensures Lenovo’s CMMC posture remains defensible, auditable, and aligned with enterprise risk tolerance, while enabling scalable and compliant participation in the U.S. Federal market. Operating at a senior governance level, this role provides strategic direction, oversight, and accountability across IT, external service providers, and business stakeholders. While technical controls are implemented by IT teams and approved vendors, this role retains sole ownership of governance decisions, SSP integrity, boundary authority, and risk accountability for the CMMC environment. Key Responsibilities Enterprise CMMC Program Ownership Serve as Lenovo’s authority for the CMMC Level 2 program, with end‑to‑end accountability for compliance posture, audit readiness, and operational sustainability. Establish, maintain, and enforce the governance model for the CMMC environment, ensuring consistent execution across internal teams and external providers. Enclave Boundary & Scope Authority Define, approve, and enforce the CMMC enclave boundary, including authoritative decisions on system inclusion, exclusion, segmentation, and architectural constraints. Approve and formally accept control scoping decisions, limitations, and exceptions, ensuring they are documented, justified, and defensible to third‑party assessors. System Security Plan (SSP) Ownership Act as the authoritative owner and approver of the System Security Plan (SSP) for the CMMC environment. Approve all material changes to system scope, architecture, policies, procedures, or control implementation. Ensure SSP and programmatic adherence to expectations set by Certified Third Party Assessor Organizations (C3PAOs). Risk Accountability & Decision Authority Hold accountability for CMMC‑scoped risk decisions, including acceptance, mitigation, or escalation of residual risk. Ensure all risk decisions are explicitly documented and aligned with Lenovo’s enterprise risk management framework. Escalate material risks to leadership with clear impact analysis and recommendations. Incident Governance & Regulatory Oversight Serve as the governing authority for CMMC‑scoped security incidents, including incident declaration, coordination of response, and oversight of external notification obligations. Ensure incident handling aligns with SSP commitments, contractual obligations, and regulatory expectations. Audit Leadership & External Representation Act as Lenovo’s primary representative for CMMC audits and assessments, including direct engagement with C3PAOs. Lead audit strategy, readiness reviews, evidence validation, assessor engagement, and audit defense activities. Respond to regulator, customer, and partner inquiries related to Lenovo’s CMMC posture. Cross ‑Functional & Vendor Governance Direct and govern execution across IT, managed service providers, and compliance partners while enforcing clear separation of responsibilities. Ensure vendor engagements align with established governance models while maintaining ownership and authority over security and risk decisions. Ensure operational decisions align with compliance requirements, supporting audit defensibility and sound risk management. Executive Reporting & Strategic Enablement Provide regular reporting on CMMC program status, risks, resource needs, and certification readiness. Enable North American Federal sales by ensuring the CMMC environment remains credible, scalable, and audit‑ready. Contribute senior expertise to related Security Governance and Assurance initiatives as required. Required Skills & Competencies Senior‑level expertise in cybersecurity governance, regulatory compliance, and enterprise risk management. Deep, practical mastery of CMMC Level 2 and NIST SP 800‑171, including assessment objectives and evidence expectations. Proven authority in defining and defending system boundaries, control scope, and governance models in regulated environments. Demonstrated experience leading high‑stakes audits and regulatory assessments. Strong judgment with comfort making risk acceptance and escalation decisions. Ability to maintain a continuously audit‑ready posture in dynamic operational environments. Ability to operate effectively in a non‑technical senior governance role while retaining full accountability for outcomes. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in cybersecurity, information systems, or a related field required. 10+ years of progressive experience in cybersecurity governance, risk management, compliance, or regulated security programs. Direct experience serving as a program owner, SSP owner, or senior audit lead for CMMC, NIST SP 800‑171, or comparable frameworks required. Experience supporting U.S. Federal, defense, or defense‑industrial‑base (DIB) environments strongly preferred. Citizenship Requirement U.S. citizenship required Availability & Travel Availability for off‑hours engagement during incidents or audits. Limited travel may be required for assessments or executive engagements. The base salary budgeted range for this position is $160k - 190K. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission. Lenovo’s various

requirements: Position Summary The Senior Manager, CRA Compliance Program serves as the enterprise execution lead for the EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) compliance program across in‑scope products, services, and supporting systems. EU CRA is a priority regulatory program with defined timelines, regulatory obligations, and audit expectations. This role owns delivery execution, operating rhythm, and people leadership for the compliance and certifications function, ensuring consistent execution, prioritization, and quality across the portfolio. Key Responsibilities Compliance Portfolio Leadership & Program Execution EU Cyber Resilience Act is a priority regulatory program with defined delivery timelines and regulatory obligations that must be met across the portfolio. Lead execution of a portfolio of security compliance and certification initiatives, ensuring delivery against regulatory, certification, and customer requirements. Accountable for execution delivery across the compliance portfolio. Regulatory interpretation and final compliance positions are owned by designated central GRC, Legal, or Security leadership. Define execution strategies, operating models, and delivery plans for supported compliance programs. Maintain oversight of scope, timelines, dependencies, and milestones across the portfolio. Maintain end-to-end traceability between regulatory requirements, implemented controls, and supporting evidence across all supported programs. Provide compliance execution leadership across Product Security, IT, Legal, Privacy, Supply Chain, and external partners. Resolve execution conflicts, manage dependencies, and drive alignment across stakeholders. People Leadership & Organizational Management Directly manage and develop subordinate contributors. Ensure clear accountability for compliance deliverables, deadlines, and evidence quality across team members. Set priorities, allocate workload, and ensure clarity of roles and accountability across the team. Coach team members on regulatory interpretation, documentation quality, audit readiness, and stakeholder engagement. Documentation, Evidence & Quality Oversight Ensure development and maintenance of high‑quality compliance documentation, technical descriptions, policies, procedures, and evidence repositories. Define and enforce standards for documentation quality, traceability, and audit defensibility. Establish governance expectations for systems of record supporting compliance tracking and evidence management. Ensure supporting documentation is maintained in the system of record. Establish quality standards for documentation and evidence used in audits, certifications, and regulatory reviews. Review and approve key artifacts prior to external submission or assessment activities. Risk, Issue & Escalation Management Oversee identification, assessment, and tracking of compliance risks, gaps, and remediation activities across the portfolio. Has authority to escalation non-compliance and enforce remediation timelines through executive governance channels. Escalate material risks, blockers, or delivery concerns to leadership with impact analysis and recommendations. Ensure remediation activities are prioritized and validated. Audit, Certification & Regulatory Engagement Support Provide leadership for audit, certification, and regulatory readiness activities. Oversee preparation and coordination of responses to assessments, findings, and regulatory inquiries. Ensure the organization maintains a continuously audit‑ready posture. Reporting & Executive Communication Provide regular status and readiness reporting to leadership. Prepare executive‑level briefings, summaries, and decision materials. Communicate clearly and consistently to enable timely decisions and alignment. Required Skills & Competencies Senior‑level experience in cybersecurity compliance, assurance, or certification programs. Proven ability to lead multi‑initiative compliance portfolios. Strong execution and program leadership skills applied to regulatory and certification‑driven environments. Demonstrated people‑management and team‑development experience. Strong judgment, prioritization, and escalation capabilities. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in cybersecurity, information systems, engineering, law, or a related field required. 12+ years of experience in cybersecurity, compliance, risk, product security, assurance, or related disciplines. Demonstrated experience leading audits, certifications, or regulatory compliance initiatives. Experience with EU CRA regulations and compliance strongly preferred. Professional certifications such as CISSP, CISM, CISA, PMP, or ISO/IEC 27001 Lead Implementer strongly preferred. Availability & Travel Availability to support audit, certification, or regulatory activities during business hours, with occasional off‑hours engagement. Occasional travel may be required for audits, assessments, or executive stakeholder engagements. The base salary budgeted range for this position is $160k - 190K. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission. Lenovo’s various

requirements: Join Lenovo’s Solutions & Services Group (SSG) and help shape the future of technology in one of the world’s most exciting industries. As digital transformation and Artificial Intelligence continue to redefine the Sports ecosystem, organizations are increasingly investing in innovative solutions that enhance fan experiences, optimize operations, unlock data-driven insights, and drive business growth. This is a unique opportunity to join Lenovo at the forefront of this transformation, working with a market-leading portfolio spanning Digital Workplace, Data Center, AI, and Services solutions. As a Sr. Solutions & Services Executive - Sports Vertical , you will play a key role in accelerating Lenovo’s growth across the Sports market while collaborating with industry leaders, strategic partners, and customers on high-impact transformation initiatives. You will report to the Sr. Solutions & Services Executive Manager. What You Will Do: Own and grow Lenovo’s Services business across the Sports industry, building strategic relationships with clubs, leagues, federations, and sports organizations while expanding Lenovo’s market presence. Act as a true hunter proactively identifying new business opportunities, winning new logos, and developing a strong pipeline of opportunities across the Sports ecosystem. Leverage your industry network and expertise to position Lenovo as a trusted strategic partner for digital transformation and AI adoption. Lead complex sales opportunities across Digital Workplace, Data Center, AI, and Lenovo’s broader Services portfolio, delivering solutions that drive measurable business value for customers. Drive AI-focused conversations with customers, translating use cases such as fan engagement, smart venues, automation, predictive analytics, digital assistants, and AI infrastructure into scalable business solutions. Engage with senior decision-makers and C-level stakeholders, articulating the business value, ROI, and strategic impact of digital and AI transformation initiatives. Collaborate with Lenovo experts, partners, sales teams, and executives to design, shape, and successfully close high-value opportunities while ensuring strong deal governance and financial health throughout the sales cycle. Expand existing customer relationships, increase wallet share, manage customer expectations, and deliver measurable growth in pipeline, revenue, and Lenovo’s footprint across the Sports vertical. What We Look For: 5+ years of experience selling professional and managed services to sports customers, ideally across Device-as-a-Service (DaaS), Digital Workplace, Data Center, or related technology solutions. Proven track record of driving business growth through territory planning, account management, pipeline development, opportunity qualification, and strategic sales execution. Strong commercial and consulting skills, with the ability to identify customer needs, build compelling business cases, and create value-driven solutions. Confident communicator and presenter, capable of influencing stakeholders at all levels, from technical specialists and finance teams to executive leadership. Strong understanding of AI and Generative AI technologies, including enterprise use cases such as automation, AI infrastructure, data-driven services, and GenAI applications. About the Role In this pivotal, customer-facing role, you will be the primary driver of strategic account growth for Lenovo’s most advanced AI cloud customers with Lenovo’s Cloud Service Provider (CSP) segment. This position is a “hunter” and trusted advisor position, as you’ll identify new opportunities, expand partnerships and oversee complex solutions from concept to deployment. Collaborating across Engineering, Technical Sales, and Global Fulfillment teams you will delivered tailored infrastructure solutions that ensure strong Executive engagement and exceptional customer satisfaction. Foundational technical knowledge of rack-scale architecture. Proven ability to engage and sell to technical audiences and engineers. Strong verbal and written communication skills ensuring messages are received with clarity, timeliness and tact. Strong organizational skills and data-driven decision making ability. Experience leading large-scale RFP responses, bid strategy and tactical execution. Experience delivering reports with Finance, specifically P&L data. Experience leading in-person meetings: industry events, factory tours, site visits, offsite visits, business review sessions. Experience working across global teams. The base salary range budgeted for this position is 175-200k. Individuals will also be considered for commission. requirements: Join Lenovo and help shape the future of talent At Lenovo, our Campus Talent Acquisition team is at the forefront of building the next generation of innovators. In this role, you won’t just support recruiting—you’ll help design experiences, build programs, and connect early-career talent to meaningful opportunities around the world. If you're someone who loves bringing ideas to life, thrives in fast-paced environments, and wants to make a real impact early in your career, this role is for you. What You’ll Do You’ll get hands-on experience across global campus recruiting, events, and talent programs: Turn data into insights: Build and analyze reports on hiring metrics, program performance, and global campus budgets Support global programs, budget & operations: Partner with teams across the Americas, Europe, and AP to keep programs running smoothly Plan and execute events: Help organize and attend recruiting events—from campus career fairs to global talent programs Create impactful content: Develop presentations and communications for senior leaders, HR partners, and business teams Collaborate across teams: Work closely with HR, Marketing, IT, Procurement, and more to deliver seamless experiences Support recruiting activities: Assist with sourcing, screening, and offer processes for early-career talent Improve how we hire: Identify tools and ideas to make recruiting faster, smarter, and more effective. Help manage vendor relationships including RFP's, procurement and contract management. Drive compliance & inclusion: Partner with legal and inclusion teams to ensure fair, compliant hiring practices Build global connections: Collaborate with teams across time zones—flexibility and curiosity are key What You Bring (Must-Have) Bachelor's Degree and 1+ year of experience in program coordination, recruiting, HR, or event planning (can be via internships) Organizational skills—you can manage multiple projects at once Strong skills in Excel and PowerPoint Curiosity and experience exploring AI tools and automation (e.g., streamlining tasks, improving processes) Strong communication skills (written + verbal) Ability to work in a hybrid schedule (3 days onsite / 2 remote) and travel occasionally for events What Sets You Apart (Nice-to-Have) Experience in campus recruiting or early-career programs Comfort working in a fast-paced, global environment Advanced Excel or data analysis skills Familiarity with tools like Workday, ATS, or CRM systems A proactive, “figure it out” mindset (we love self-starters!) Passion for talent, employer branding, or DEI initiatives Why Join Lenovo? Work on global programs that impact early-career talent worldwide Gain exposure to senior leaders and cross-functional teams Build skills in data, events, recruiting, and program management Be part of a team that values innovation, inclusion, and growth Who You Are You’re curious, organized, and excited to grow your career in talent or HR. You’re not afraid to ask questions, take initiative, and try new things—and you want your work to make a real impact. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: Key Responsibilities: Group tax provision and forecasting Support the overall group tax reporting effort through preparation of the quarterly global tax provision Coordinate with various stakeholders including tax accounting, finance and IR teams as necessary Assist in maintaining a stable and predictable tax environment through timely and accurate provision reporting Assist in managing the group’s global tax position from an international perspective Tax provision experience is a core responsibility and a non-negotiable requirement for this role Tax optimization Use the provision and compliance work experience to identify opportunities for tax optimization; Monitoring and, where necessary, correct actions which could put Lenovo at risk. Assist in managing the group's global tax position while balancing compliance and business objectives. Tax model development and control Assist with the further development and implementation of the international tax model. Advise business units on the tax implications of proposed transactions and operating model changes. Provide advisory support on transaction structuring and business operating model considerations. Assist with the development and implementation of tax internal control processes. Support business developments, mergers and acquisitions (M&A), and internal restructuring initiatives. Transfer pricing Assist in managing overall intercompany transactions, uplift, and true-up processes. Coordinate with regional tax, legal, trade compliance, treasury, accounting, and finance teams to ensure proper implementation of transfer pricing policies. Manage transfer pricing risk associated with intercompany transactions. Ensure documentation supporting intercompany transactions aligns with the global tax model and supports tax audit defense. Assist in managing BEPS documentation requirements to mitigate potential tax audit exposure. Prior transfer pricing experience is preferred but not required; candidates with strong tax provision experience can develop these skills on the job. Tax information systems development and improvement Identify and clearly lay out systems capabilities required for the efficient production of tax (and related reporting) information; Work closely with the IT and accounting groups in the course of developing and implementing core systems capabilities. Basic Qualifications: University degree in accounting. Minimum 6+ years Big 4 / Mid-tier public accounting firm experience with emphasis on corporate MNC tax compliance and provision. Tax provision experience is a must Preferred Qualifications: International tax/ Transfer pricing work experience is a plus, but not a requirement (will train the right candidate). Strong sense of responsibility and accountability combined with ability to work on his/her own initiatives. Strong analytical and communication skills organized and focused approach, willingness to get into the detail and follow through. Rising to the challenge of a fast-moving multi-billion-dollar MNC Ability to manage multiple tasks while maintaining oversight; ability to deal with tax issues in wider business context Personable, team player, effective management of external service providers Good working knowledge of MS Office (Word, Excel, Powerpoint); working knowledge of SAP is an advantage Willingness to travel We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Can you turn customer challenges into elegant, high‑performing infrastructure solutions? Do you get energized by collaborating with sales teams and partners, shaping architecture, and guiding them toward the right technical path? And most importantly, are you ready to help shape the future of AI‑ready infrastructure across EMEA? If you’re passionate about designing end‑to‑end solutions that span compute, storage, networking, hybrid cloud, and modern AI workloads, then this role may be the perfect next step for you. As we are expanding our EMEA pre‑sales capacity, we’re looking for hands‑on Technical Pre‑Sales Engineer who thrive on translating business needs into technical clarity and enjoy being at the center of customer conversations, partner enablement, and solution design. What will you do in this role? Provide technical pre-sales support for Lenovo ISG (Infrastructure Solutions Group) opportunities across EMEA (internal teams + external channel partners). Translate customer requirements (RFPs, requirement statements, sizing inputs, constraints) into solution architectures and bill-of materials (BOM) configurations. Build accurate configurations using Lenovo tools/configurations; validate compatibility, performance, and scalability assumptions. Produce technical outputs: solution proposals, sizing documents, high-level designs, RFP responses, competitive comparisons, and configuration justifications. Run partner enablement: deliver technical briefings, configuration “how-to” sessions, and reusable playbooks/templates. Support opportunity qualification: ask discovery questions, identify risks/assumptions (power/cooling/rack, security, latency, resiliency), and recommend next steps (workshops, POC plans). Collaborate with specialists (storage/HCI/AI/HPC) to assemble end-to-end solutions and ensure consistent messaging while providing feedback to product management and services teams based on field/partner learnings. Maintain a library of reusable assets (reference architectures, configuration patterns, FAQs, competitive notes). What We’re Looking For: 3+ years in technical pre-sales, solution engineering, or technical account roles in enterprise IT (vendor, partner, or SI). Proven ability to design and configure infrastructure solutions (not just individual products), explain tradeoffs clearly and strong understanding of enterprise infrastructure fundamentals: compute, storage, networking, virtualization, resiliency. Experience responding to technical customer requests: RFP/RFI inputs, sizing data, constraints, and architectural requirements. oriented communication. Ability to produce crisp technical deliverables (slides/docs) suitable for customers and partners. Fluent English (written and spoken), another major European language (French, Spanish, German, Italian) is a plus. Depth in one or more of the following areas would be highly valued: artificial intelligence solutions, enterprise storage, high-performance computing, HCI or virtualization. Familiarity with one or more competitor ecosystems (e.g., Dell, HPE, NetApp, Pure, VMware alternatives), exposure to Kubernetes/containers and modern data platforms is a plus. Knowledge required in 1 or more of these areas: Compute: x86 servers, virtualization platforms, GPU accelerated systems for AI inference/training. Storage: block/file/object concepts, performance sizing, data protection/DR/replication; (Lenovo + 3rd party ecosystems). HCI/Private Cloud: hyperconverged stacks, virtualization modernization, hybrid cloud patterns. Networking: Ethernet fabrics (25/100GbE), basic design considerations for low latency and throughput. AI-ready infrastructure: GPU sizing basics, data pipelines for AI/RAG, monitoring and operational considerations. Services & lifecycle: deployment services, support contracts, consumption/financing options, sustainability considerations. What do we offer you? Compensation & Rewards Performance-based salary reviews and annual bonuses Meal tickets, service anniversary awards, and referral bonuses Employee share purchase program Scholarships for employees’ children Health & Well-being Fully covered private medical insurance Annual well-being allowance for sports, training, relaxation, and therapy ,and nd on-site chair massage Life & accident insurance with extra medical coverage Employee Assistance Program Training & Development Technical Ladder & Sales Excellence programs Individual Career Development Plans with mentoring Bookster subscription for continuous learning Time Away 21–28 vacation days depending on tenure Sick days and extra paid leave for public holidays on weekends Additional paid leave for employees with children Are you ready to help us build smarter infrastructure, accelerate innovation, and empower our partners to win? Join us and be part of the team defining what’s next. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

岗位职责： 1. 设计、开发并维护高可用、高扩展性的云原生后端服务及配套前端管理平台，持续优化系统架构与用户体验。 2. 基于微服务架构设计和开发 API 接口，提供高性能、高安全性的数据服务能力，满足业务增长需求。 3. 构建高并发、高可用的数据接收与处理管道，保障来自 Web 、 App 、小程序等多终端用户行为及反馈数据的实时采集、准确处理与低延迟存储。 4. 支持数据分析与可视化场景建设，提升数据流转效率和业务洞察能力。 5. 监控并保障云服务的稳定性、安全性及整体性能，参与故障定位、问题排查及应急响应工作。 6. 设计并实现 AI Agent （智能体）相关功能，包括基于大语言模型的对话交互、自主任务规划与执行、工具调用等能力建设。 7. 结合业务需求，将 AI Agent 能力应用于问卷调研、实验分析、用户画像等场景，推动智能化能力落地。 岗位要求： 1. 具备云原生应用开发经验，能够独立完成后端服务设计、开发、部署与维护工作。 2. 熟悉微服务架构设计理念及 API 开发实践，具备高性能、高可用系统建设经验。 3. 具备大规模数据处理相关经验，能够设计和优化高并发数据采集、传输及处理链路。 4. 熟悉云服务运维与监控体系，具备系统性能优化、故障排查及稳定性保障能力。 5. 具有良好的系统架构设计能力，能够兼顾系统的可扩展性、安全性和可维护性。 6. 了解或具备 AI Agent 、大语言模型应用开发经验，熟悉对话系统、任务编排或工具调用等相关技术方向。 7. 具备良好的沟通协作能力和问题解决能力，能够与跨职能团队高效合作，共同推动项目落地。 Additional
