requirements: The Cyber-Security Services Offering Development Manager will be responsible for creating cybersecurity offerings to help customers secure their digital workplace environment. This role will work with our security, software, and services teams to create and integrate new security offers into the services portfolio by driving standalone offers (professional and managed services) or integrating into our solutions. The team is also responsible for the creation of comprehensive marketing requirements documents, interact with geographies, guide the creation of marketing messaging, position security service offerings, and plan, launch, and drive offerings in global markets. The candidate should have prior services offering development experience in the security domain. An ideal candidate will have: 1) a deep understanding of Digital and Cyber Security Technologies, including how AI and other emerging technologies are defining the cybersecurity landscape 2) a global systems integrator (GSI) background 3) experience working with alliance and channel partners. Success will require the ability to influence and maintain open communication with all areas of the company, including worldwide business units, geo/country sales, marketing teams, pre-sales, and delivery teams. This person must be able to manage complexity, think creatively, deal with ambiguity, and thrive in a fast-paced environment. Individuals will have the opportunity to work with a variety of key security alliance partners and influence WW stakeholders, to drive key innovative and transformational offerings to our customers. Position Requirements: Enhance the security services strategy and create innovative security offerings that excel expectations from customers. Provide technology leadership and guidance for our offerings, with a primary focus the emerging cyber security landscape. Work closely with other development leaders within Lenovo to align strategies and ensure a cohesive product portfolio. Work closely with geographies and other services organizations worldwide to understand priorities & turn them into plans for creating compelling professional and managed security offerings. Strategically drive the portfolio and deal engagement to grow the business. Conduct competitive analysis to determine pricing strategy and product features that will be included in the offering, including key processes and resources. Maintain and analyze potential alliance and service partner relationships and develop long-term strategic requirements to support assigned services and solutions. Provide input to marketing staff to ensure they can generate high quality sales aids such as product guides, press releases and web content. Develop and maintain deep technical expertise in Digital and Cyber Security, including but not limited to Identity, access & trust management, visibility & risk management, compliance management, cyber detection and response, cloud security, endpoint protection & SIEM. Partner with pre-sales and sales to shape proposals Required Qualifications: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, engineering or related field. 7+ years of IT security technology offering development, or similar experience, preferably from a GSI 5+ years’ experience in designing and delivering solutions that leverage cyber security technologies. Deep understanding of IT outsourcing services, technology industry, digital and cyber security technologies. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to both technical and business stakeholders. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously. Collaborative approach to meeting business objectives and ability to influence at all levels of matrixed organizations Experience working with global teams and conducting business internationally, supporting different time zones. Certifications such as Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH), Certified Information Security Manager (CISM), or Certified Information Privacy Professional (CIPP) are a plus. Additional

Lenovo is seeking a Workstations Category Manager to develop and drive commercial strategy, optimize portfolio performance, and identify growth opportunities across our professional workstation business in Saudi Arabia. This role is responsible for managing the workstation category lifecycle, aligning product strategy with market opportunities, and enabling sales success through pricing, promotions, and market intelligence. Key Responsibilities Develop and execute the commercial strategy for the Lenovo Workstations portfolio, maximizing growth, profitability, and market share across Saudi Arabia. Serve as the subject matter expert for the Workstations category, including market trends, customer needs, competitive positioning, and product roadmap. Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify business opportunities, market trends, and customer requirements. Create, own, and communicate quarterly business plans, including product portfolio recommendations, pricing strategy, promotional activities, and sales enablement initiatives. Manage category pricing, profitability, and promotional investments to optimize revenue and margin performance. Equip sales teams with category insights, competitive intelligence, value propositions, and tools to support customer engagement and win rates. Collaborate with channel, distribution, and marketing teams on sales-in and sales-out planning, inventory management, and demand generation activities. Monitor category performance through key business metrics and recommend corrective actions to ensure continuous improvement and target achievement. Support workstation product launches and go-to-market execution, ensuring alignment between local market needs and regional strategy. Position Requirements 3+ years experience in the Workstation, PC, IT hardware, or technology industry in a product or category management role. Understanding of the Saudi Arabia IT market, commercial customer landscape, and channel ecosystem. Experience working with distributors, partners, and enterprise sales organizations and knowledge of enterprise technology solutions is an advantage. Strong analytical and commercial mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable business recommendations. Professional fluency in English and Arabic. Strong commercial acumen with experience in category management, product management, business planning, sales operations, or a related commercial role. Self-motivated, result-oriented, and comfortable working in a fast-paced, matrix organization with willingness and ability to travel (approximately 30%). We look forward to connecting with you! requirements: ■募集の背景 レノボ・ジャパンはスマートフォンからクラウドまで、幅広い製品を提供しています。法人向け事業においては、お客様のＩＴＣ環境の構築、 DX （デジタルトランスフォーメーション）をご支援することで、お客様の成功と豊かな社会の実現を目指しています。現在は VUCA と呼ばれる状況ではありますが、私たちは法人向け事業の更なる拡大のため営業組織を強化します。 ■組織について レノボ・ジャパン コマーシャル事業本部 広域営業事業部 中小企業から大企業まで、幅広いお客様を担当しています。チームワークを重んじ、メンバー同士でノウハウを共有、全員で与えられたミッションをクリアすべく日々業務に取り組んでいます。ハイブリッドワークを取り入れ、柔軟な働き方も可能です。 Manager （上司） : Japan Commercial SMB MM Inside Sales Manager Number of Peers （同僚の人数） : 15 ■職務内容 インサイドセールスとして電話、メール、 Web 面談等を効果的に活用してお客様にハードウェア、ソフトウェア、サービスなどを提案し、成約に結び付けていきます。外勤営業との連携は不可欠ですが、外勤営業のサポート役ではなく、お客様のフロントに立って担当セールスチームのハブとなる重要な仕事です。常に最先端の IT 技術に触れることができるので、営業力だけでなく、 IT の知識も自然と身に付きます。 ・お客様ニーズを分析・理解し、意思決定プロセスを把握することで、お客様にとって魅力的な提案をおこなう ・外勤営業、パートナーセールス、製品別市場開発マネージャー、ビジネスパートナーの担当者など社内外関係者と密に連携し、ビジネスチャンスを成功に導く ・個人の営業目標を達成することにより、会社の目標や目的を達成させる ・セールスパイプラインの的確な状況判断により、営業活動および収益達成を正確に予測する ・見込み客への提案からクロージング、製品・サービス導入後のフォロー、次回購入に向けたお客様との関係構築まで包括的にアカウントマネジメントをおこなう ・社内マネジメント層と連携し、お客様トップマネジメントとのリレーションシップ構築を実現する ■ Key Interaction with: ・外勤営業 ・パートナーセールス（ビジネスパートナーを担当する営業） ・ビジネス・ディベロップメント・マネージャー（製品別市場開発マネージャー） ・製品マネージャー ・カスタマーサポート ・オペレーション ・マーケティング ・ファイナンス ・需給管理 ■キャリアパス ・外勤営業 ・インサイドセールスマネージャー ・マーケティング ・製品担当 など Additional

岗位职责： 1.市场研究与用户洞察 - 深入研究游戏台机市场动态、消费者行为、竞品分析及行业趋势，为业务提供前瞻性产品策略建议。 - 定期进行目标用户画像构建、需求调研及满意度分析，确保产品规划与用户需求紧密结合，持续优化产品价值主张。 2. 游戏台式产品规划与营销 - 规划整体Portfolio及Roadmap，制定产品定位策略，核心竞争优势及差异化卖点。 - 与研发深度协同，参与产品开发设计，提供游戏台式市场及用户洞察建议，优化用户体验及粘性，提升品牌知名度，美誉度，NPS。 - 制定产品营销计划，包括定价、促销策略、渠道管理、新品上市等，确保达成整体经营目标。 - 开拓创新营销模式，与销售、市场营销等团队紧密协同，提升营销效果与投资回报率。 岗位要求： 1）本科及以上教育背景，5年以上PC产品业务管理经验， 2）熟悉产品规划或E2E项目管理经验，以及产品开发流程和生命周期管理 3）了解中国消费3C市场，用户需求和技术发展趋势； 4）优秀的策略深入思考能力，全局思维，经营意识； 5）优秀的中英文沟通能力和团队协作能力； Additional

requirements: 岗位职责 1. 负责整机产品开发工作，统筹制定结构核心技术方案，完整推进产品可行性研究、结构设计、数据分析、关键零部件选型等全流程研发工作，保障产品研发前期工作有序落地。 2. 主导研发阶段结构件生产跟进工作，全程负责工厂整机组装、调试、性能验证及各类技术问题排查，持续跟进各类技术问题整改落地，实现问题闭环管理。 3. 统筹推进产品从设计开发到量产的全流程转化工作，深度联动各跨部门团队，协同完成产品成本管控、质量把控及项目排期管理，保障产品顺利量产。 4. 主导编制、迭代维护设计规范、计算报告、测试报告、FMEA等各类专业技术文档，确保所有产品设计方案符合行业相关规范及标准要求。 5. 负责团队初级工程师的技术指导与能力培养工作，牵头团队核心、重大技术难题的攻坚工作，主导关键技术决策，助力团队技术能力提升。 岗位要求 1. 机械、电子、材料、自动化等相关工科专业本科及以上学历，硕士学历优先考虑。 2. 拥有5年及以上消费电子、智能设备等相关领域产品结构、硬件开发实战工作经验，熟悉相关产品研发流程。 3. 具备扎实的产品设计与三维建模能力，深度掌握塑料、金属等各类常用材料的加工工艺、模具结构原理，熟练开展产品公差分析工作。 4. 具备良好的英语读写能力，逻辑思维清晰，拥有优秀的沟通协调与项目管理能力，能够高效适配跨团队协同工作模式。 5. 优先考虑熟悉热设计、EMC、可靠性分析逻辑，且具备产品从试产到量产全流程落地实战经验的人员。 Additional

requirements: 岗位职责： 1. 负责相机模组的硬件交付，维护模组硬件的参数性能，DFX，进度基准。 2. 解决/改善相机硬件问题对相机IQ性能的影响，问题类型包括但不限于电子，结构，光学系统。 3. 配合相关部门完成相机硬件新技术预研和落地。 岗位要求： 1. 熟悉相机模组的开发流程以及相关二级物料的技术特性参数。 2. 熟悉相机系统，特别是消费电子类产品的相机通路。 3. 在以下领域至少有一项技术专长： a. 模组结构设计 b. 马达设计 c. CMOS 图像传感器 d. 图像处理的相关算法开发 e. 模组测试相关算法开发 f. 电子设计 4. 优秀的分析定位，解决问题的能力，有强烈的责任心，了解项目管理的基本流程 5. 英语能力满足基本交流通顺，书面表达准确 (CET-6 及以上) 。 Additional
responsibilities: 1. 负责相机模组的硬件交付，维护模组硬件的参数性能，DFX，进度基准。 2. 解决/改善相机硬件问题对相机IQ性能的影响，问题类型包括但不限于电子，结构，光学系统。 3. 配合相关部门完成相机硬件新技术预研和落地。

requirements: Job Responsibilities: 1. Responsible for project end-to-end management and delivery from product concept to launch for Motorola mobile or IoT products. 2. Familiarity with project management methodologies and skills. Excellent leadership, organization and time management skills. Having problem-solving, decision-making and risk management mindset. 4. Understanding of consumer electronics technology and trends. Having innovative spirit to break conventions. 5. Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple projects simultaneously to meet schedule and quality standards. 6. Fluency in English spoken and written. Strong verbal and written communication and negotiation abilities. Additional

requirements: 岗位职责： 1 负责企业内部全球办公园区IT智能服务和方案的产品全生命周期管理。包括IT软硬件及AI解决方案的产品规划、落地与持续优化，从需求分析、竞品调研、方案设计、PoC验证到全球推广和运维优化 2 识别适合引入AI的业务场景。主导AI产品的MVP验证持续跟踪产品效果，推动AI能力（大模型、Agent、数据分析）在园区场景的实际落地与应用。 3 跨团队协同与全球落地推进 协调研发、运维、供应商与各区域IT团队 支持不同国家/园区落地，平衡标准化与本地化需求 推动复杂项目按计划交付 4 IT服务与方案的持续运营维护 （1）通过数据驱动迭代优化产品功能，输出标准化产品方案、部署规范、用户使用手册及运营文档 （2）识别先进技术方案迭代，流程优化等运维降本增效的机会，持续提升IT运维与工程团队效率 5 行业趋势分析与产品路线规划 跟踪AI、IoT、智能办公领域最新发展 制定Smart Campus产品中长期Roadmap 持续优化产品竞争力与业务价值 岗位要求： 1. 基础背景 计算机、软件工程或相关专业本科及以上学历 3–5年产品经理经验，有IT软硬件或IoT产品经验优先 2. 产品与技术能力 熟悉产品全生命周期管理，有完整产品落地经验 对IT系统架构、智能硬件、IoT解决方案有基本理解 具备良好的竞品分析与方案设计能力 3. AI能力 （1）AI应用能力（必须） 熟练使用主流AI工具（如大模型、AI设计工具等）提升工作效率 能利用AI完成如PRD生成、方案调研、竞品分析、原型设计、数据分析与用户洞察等核心工作 （2）AI产品思维（必须） 具备基本Prompt设计能力或Agent使用经验 能参与AI产品PoC验证或简单Demo构建 理解AI产品的不确定性、数据依赖与评估机制 （3）AI理解能力（加分项） 理解大模型、AIGC、多模态等基本原理及应用场景 能判断哪些场景适合用AI解决，并评估ROI 4. 通用能力 较强的任务执行力，充分调动资源解决困难，目标导向。 优秀的跨团队沟通与项目推进能力 良好的逻辑分析与问题解决能力，比如能在2–5天内完成高质量方案调研与竞品分析 对新技术有好奇心与自驱学习能力，比如快速尝试新的AI工具和能力，快速理解新技术，新方案的能力 5. 语言能力 CET-6及以上，具备流利英语沟通能力，可以和各个国家的同事直接沟通协作 岗位亮点 参与全球智能园区建设，影响国际化办公体验 深度参与AI+IT融合场景落地 覆盖IoT、AI、大模型等前沿技术组合 面向平台化、全球化产品，而非单一项目 Additional

requirements: 岗位职责 ： 1、统筹云原生产品线，完成竞品分析、产品规划、设计落地及商业化跟进全流程工作； 2、负责基础云原生产品迭代，挖掘用户痛点，设计并落地标准化功能，优化使用体验； 3、跟进前沿技术，规划应用层云原生产品，推动新品发布与商业化落地； 4、跟踪行业动态与竞品，明确产品迭代及演进方向。 岗位要求： 具备云原生领域产品全流程操盘经验，熟悉容器化、微服务、DevOps 等核心技术及相关生态，能独立完成产品规划与落地。 拥有扎实的用户需求挖掘、痛点分析能力，具备优秀的产品设计思维，可高效输出标准化功能方案并推动落地。 具备敏锐的行业洞察力与竞品分析能力，能快速捕捉行业动态、技术趋势及市场机会，明确产品差异化竞争方向。 具备良好的跨部门协作能力，能有效联动研发、市场、销售等团队，推动产品研发与商业化目标达成。 Additional
responsibilities: 1、统筹云原生产品线，完成竞品分析、产品规划、设计落地及商业化跟进全流程工作； 2、负责基础云原生产品迭代，挖掘用户痛点，设计并落地标准化功能，优化使用体验； 3、跟进前沿技术，规划应用层云原生产品，推动新品发布与商业化落地； 4、跟踪行业动态与竞品，明确产品迭代及演进方向。

requirements: What you'll do: The position is a AI Hardware Development Engineer position in the Lenovo Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), based in Morrisville, North Carolina. This is an exciting role in the AI Server Development organization that has a strong focus to be the #1 trusted IT Partner in the Datacenter market. Opportunities on the team include developing innovative and complex server designs to meet customer and business needs. These organic designs provide exposure to the latest technologies from Lenovo and its development partners to meet market demands. The role also entails solving unique client challenges through the custom development of solutions and direct engagement with customers. requirements: ■ ポジション概要 教育機関（自治体・大学・私立学校など）に対し、ITソリューションを通じて教育DXを推進いただくアカウント営業ポジションです。GIGAスクール構想などを背景に急成長する教育市場において、顧客の課題に応じた最適な提案を行い、長期的な関係構築と売上拡大を担っていただきます。 ■ 業務内容 首都圏・東日本エリアの教育機関・自治体を担当し、社内外の関係者と連携しながら営業を推進します。 ・教育委員会・大学・私立学校へのハイタッチ営業 ・GIGAスクール関連案件の提案・推進 ・PC / タブレット / サーバー / ストレージなどのインフラ提案 ・Microsoft 365 / Autopilot / クラウドを活用した教育DX提案 ・LCM / DaaSなどサービス型ビジネスの提案 ・パートナー企業との協業および案件推進 ・売上目標達成に向けたアカウント戦略立案・実行 ※Inside Sales、Pre-sales、Product部門などと連携して進めます ■ 組織・カルチャー 公共・文教市場に特化した営業組織で、長期在籍メンバーも多く、ノウハウを共有しながらチームで成果を出す環境です。社内育成や内部登用も重視しており、中長期的なキャリア形成を支援しています。 ■ 出張について 担当エリアに応じて出張は発生しますが、長期出張や常駐は基本的にありません。 ■就業場所 : 東京都千代田区 ＜応募要件＞ 【必須（MUST）】 ・3年以上の法人営業経験（B2B）※IT業界経験は不問 ・顧客の業務課題やビジネス背景を理解し、課題解決に向けた提案・アクションプランの立案および実行経験 ・社内外の関係者を巻き込みながら、主体的に案件を推進した経験 ・数値目標に対するコミットメントを持ち、継続的に成果創出へ取り組める方 ・自発的・自律的に行動し、変化のある環境でも柔軟に業務遂行できる方 ＜労働条件＞ ■雇用主 : レノボ・ジャパン合同会社 ■就業場所 : 東京都千代田区 ■雇用形態 : 正社員 (試用期間: 6ヵ月） ■募集人数 : 若干名 ■給与条件 : 経験、スキルなどを考慮し決定 ■就業時間 : 9:00-17:45 (休憩時間: 1 hour (12:00-13:00)) ■休日 : 土日祝日、会社創立記念日、特別休暇 ■福利厚生 : 社会保険完備（健康保険、厚生年金、雇用保険、労働災害補償保険(労災)、教育費補助計画制度、財形貯蓄制度、医療給付制度、保養レクリエーション施設あり、確定拠出年金制度 ■受動喫煙対策 屋内禁煙 Additional

requirements: Lenovo is seeking an accomplished Advisory AI Infrastructure Engineer to take a leadership role within our Advanced AI Technology Center. In this position, you will architect, build, and evolve the large-scale infrastructure and platforms that underpin AI model development, deployment, and operation across the organization. You will serve as a technical authority on infrastructure strategy, mentor junior engineers, drive best practices, and collaborate cross-functionally with research, engineering, and product teams. Your deep expertise will be instrumental in scaling our AI capabilities and ensuring production-grade reliability, performance, and security. If you are passionate about making Smarter Technology For All, come help us realize our Hybrid AI vision! Responsibilities AI Infrastructure Strategy and Architecture: Define and drive the long-term infrastructure strategy for AI workloads. Architect scalable, cost-efficient, and resilient compute , storage, and networking solutions that support the full AI lifecycle from experimentation through production. Technical Leadership and Mentorship: Serve as a technical lead and mentor to infrastructure engineers, establishing engineering standards, conducting design reviews, and fostering a culture of operational excellence across the team. AI Model Deployment and Platform Engineering: Design and own the platforms, frameworks, and processes for deploying, monitoring , and managing AI models at scale in production environments, including model serving, A/B testing infrastructure, and rollback mechanisms. Advanced Automation and CI/CD: Architect and maintain sophisticated automation pipelines for AI model training, evaluation, testing, and deployment. Champion infrastructure-as-code practices and drive continuous improvement of CI/CD workflows. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with data scientists, ML engineers, product managers, and leadership to align infrastructure capabilities with research and business objectives . Act as the primary infrastructure point of contact for cross-team initiatives. Performance Engineering: Lead efforts to profile, benchmark, and optimize AI infrastructure and model serving for throughput, latency, GPU utilization , scalability, and cost efficiency at scale. Security, Compliance, and Governance: Establish and enforce security best practices, access controls, and compliance frameworks across AI infrastructure. Ensure adherence to relevant regulatory requirements and internal governance policies. Capacity Planning and Cost Management: Own capacity forecasting, resource planning, and cost optimization for GPU clusters and associated infrastructure, balancing performance needs with budget constraints. Qualifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Engineering , Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field. Advanced degree preferred. 8+ years of experience in software engineering, infrastructure engineering, DevOps/SRE, or a related field, with at least 4 years focused on AI/ML infrastructure. Demonstrated experience leading or mentoring engineering teams on infrastructure projects. Deep expertise in computer systems, distributed systems, and cloud computing architectures. Extensive experience designing, deploying, and managing multi-node distributed GPU clusters using Slurm , Kubernetes, or equivalent orchestration platforms. Expert-level Linux system administration, including package management, user/group management, file system internals, shell scripting (e.g., Bash), networking, and system configuration (e.g., systemd , kernel tuning). Strong proficiency in Python and at least one additional systems language (e.g., Go, C++, Rust, Java). Deep experience with AI-specific hardware and software stacks (e.g., NVIDIA GPUs, CUDA, cuDNN , NCCL, InfiniBand/RoCE networking). Proven track record managing high-performance computing (HPC) environments, including job scheduling, resource allocation, cluster maintenance, and performance tuning. Significant experience with AI model deployment, serving infrastructure, and lifecycle management in production. Strong architectural thinking with the ability to balance trade-offs across performance, reliability, security, and cost. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence technical decisions across teams and present to senior leadership. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and drive clarity through technical leadership. Bonus Points Experience with AI and machine learning frameworks at scale (e.g., PyTorch , DeepSpeed , Megatron-LM, vLLM ). Hands-on experience with major cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure) and hybrid cloud architectures. Advanced experience with containerization (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes), including custom operators, Helm charts, and GPU scheduling plugins. Expertise with observability stacks (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/OpenSearch, Datadog) for infrastructure and model monitoring. Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools (e.g., Terraform, Ansible, Pulumi ). Contributions to open-source infrastructure or ML tooling projects. Experience with large language model (LLM) training and inference infrastructure. #LATC We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: LATC Summary: Lenovo AI Technology Center (LATC) is Lenovo’s global AI Center of Excellence, fueling the enterprise’s transformation into an AI-first organization. As a world-leading computing company, Lenovo offers a full spectrum of technology products and solutions, covering wearables, Motorola smartphones, ThinkPad/Yoga laptops, PCs, workstations, servers, and end-to-end services. This unparalleled product breadth creates a unique canvas for AI innovation, enabling rapid deployment of cutting-edge foundation models and flexible hybrid-cloud, agentic computing across the entire product portfolio. LATC is building the next wave of AI core technologies and platforms that evolve with the fast-moving AI ecosystem, focusing on novel model and agentic orchestration & collaboration across mobile, edge, and cloud resources. Our mission is to position Lenovo and its customers at the forefront of the global AI generational shift, advancing Lenovo’s Hybrid AI vision and delivering Smarter Technology for All. Job Summary: As a core engineering delivery role, you will be responsible for the end-to-end engineering implementation, global cloud deployment and full-stack system optimization of Lenovo’s enterprise AI systems. Focused on back-end development with matched front-end technical capabilities, you will convert the AI agent system Reference Design (prototype/demo) into production-level, deployable products, undertake hybrid cloud/edge-cloud orchestration and deployment, and ensure the stable landing and large-scale operation of AI products across Lenovo’s hybrid cloud, edge and end-device ecosystem. This role is the key link connecting AI prototype development and actual business application, and is critical to the engineering realization and global delivery of LATC’s Hybrid AI and agentic computing strategy. Key Responsibilities: Back-End Service Development: Based on the enterprise AI agentic system technical specifications and Reference Design, build enterprise-level distributed services using mainstream technical stacks; design and develop APIs and SDKs, implement core business logic, and ensure the high availability, high scalability and high performance of back-end services for AI agentic systems. Full-Stack System Integration: Complete front-end interface development and human-computer interaction design for AI products (adapted to PC/mobile/cloud terminals); realize seamless data interaction and full-stack integration between front-end applications and back-end services, and ensure the fluency and usability of the entire system. Hybrid Cloud & Edge-Cloud Deployment: Combine Lenovo’s hybrid cloud technical architecture, complete containerization and orchestration of AI products based on Docker/K8s; implement global deployment on public cloud (AWS/Azure), private cloud and edge devices, and solve cross-regional deployment environment compatibility, network latency and resource scheduling problems. System Performance Optimization: Optimize the running performance of AI products from the engineering level, including back-end service tuning, database query optimization, cache strategy design and front-end rendering optimization; reduce system latency, improve throughput, and adapt to the hardware performance characteristics of Lenovo’s end devices/servers to achieve optimal running effect. Deployment Documentation & Post-Delivery Maintenance: Compile detailed technical documents including product deployment manuals, operation guides and maintenance specifications; provide technical support for the post-delivery operation of enterprise AI products, track system real-time running status, and carry out version iteration and function optimization according to business needs and technical updates. Required Qualifications: Minimum 5 years of Python backend development experience; proficient in asynchronous programming (asyncio) and high-concurrency I/O models. Hands-on experience building gateway services with FastAPI or Starlette; familiar with streaming output technologies including SSE, WebSocket and NDJSON. Skilled in Pydantic, type annotations and rigorous type checking workflows (mypy strict mode). Well-versed in Python engineering standards: pytest, asynchronous testing, packaging & release (pyproject), dependency and version management. Experienced in operation and maintenance of mainstream middleware, at minimum Redis and PostgreSQL; solid production knowledge covering connection pools, transactions & data consistency, index optimization, timeout & retry logic, idempotent design, fault recovery and data migration. Practical Docker containerization experience; capable of independently building images, optimizing runtime parameters and implementing environment isolation. Proficient with Docker Compose for building and maintaining multi-service local and test environments. Familiar with Linux development and deployment environments; able to write Shell scripts for automation of build, deployment, troubleshooting and log collection. Mandatory Vue frontend development skills; able to independently develop admin dashboards and debugging pages, conduct API joint debugging and troubleshoot issues. Strong system design and problem diagnosis capabilities, able to resolve complex cross-module and cross-service failures. Preferred Qualifications: Familiar with LangGraph or equivalent graph orchestration frameworks; experience designing state machines or workflow engines. Production observability implementation experience, proficient in OpenTelemetry (traces, metrics, context propagation). Engineering experience building LLM applications, with deep understanding of multi-Agent orchestration, tool calling, structured output validation and correction mechanisms. Experience integrating vector search and memory modules (pgvector, ONNX Runtime, tokenizers, etc.). Hands-on experience with Langfuse or comparable evaluation & observability platforms. Multilingual development proficiency in Java / Python / C+

Full-Stack System Integration: Complete front-end interface development and human-computer interaction design for AI products (adapted to PC/mobile/cloud terminals); realize seamless data interaction and full-stack integration between front-end applications and back-end services, and ensure the fluency and usability of the entire system. responsibilities: Set the technical vision and architecture for the agentic security platform — make foundational design decisions (planning, orchestration, tool use, memory, guardrails). Own the most complex, highest-impact agents end-to-end — from problem framing and prototyping through evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement in production at scale. Define the evaluation discipline: ground-truth datasets, offline and online evals, regression suites, replay tooling, and observability for non-deterministic agent behavior. Work directly with frontier models (Claude, GPT, Gemini) — benchmark them on security tasks and architect around their strengths and failure modes. Build shared patterns, frameworks, and infrastructure (prompting, tool use, retrieval, guardrails, cost controls) to reduce the cost of shipping the next agent. Partner with security and platform leadership to identify high-leverage opportunities and translate ambiguous problems into robust agent designs. Mentor and level up engineers through design reviews, pairing, and by setting standards for agent quality, safety, and reliability. Stay ahead of the field — track research, model releases, and emerging techniques, and convert relevant insights into a concrete roadmap.

requirements: The AI Solution Delivery Lead is accountable for the on-time, secure, and successful delivery of AI agent solutions into production. This role manages the delivery lifecycle, enforces strict security and compliance controls, and serves as the primary liaison with DT/IT, Legal, and Security. The focus is on predictable, high-quality execution that manages risk and delivers value. Job Responsibilities: 1 、 End-to-End Delivery Management: Oversees the planning, execution, and delivery of high-priority agent projects. Manage project timelines, resources, funding allocation, and cross-project dependencies to ensure on-time, on-budget delivery. 2 、 DT/IT Liaison & Integration: Act as the primary point of contact for all engagements with DT/IT. Facilitates requirements communications for APIs, data and integration with enterprise systems. 3 、 Security, Legal & Compliance Gatekeeping: Integrate security, legal, and Responsible AI (RAI) reviews into the delivery pipeline. Ensure all solutions pass mandatory security assessments, privacy reviews, and ethical AI checks before deployment. 4 、 Delivery Excellence & Process Improvement: Define and implement robust delivery processes, including funding, pipelines, quality gates, and scaling readiness reviews. 5 、 Risk & Issue Management: Proactively identify, log, and mitigate project risks (technical, operational, regulatory). Lead incident response planning and post-mortem analyses for delivery-related issues Job Qualifications: 1 、 Expertise in Agile/Scrum, and project management tools. 2 、 Solid understanding of RAI fundamentals, software compliance, and IT governance. 3 、 Excellent negotiation and collaboration skills to manage dependencies across multiple teams. 4 、 Strong problem-solving skills and a calm demeanor under pressure. 5 、 Familiarity with AI-specific risks and mitigation strategies. requirements: 岗位职责： AIOps高级工程师将负责公司智能应用运维（AgenticOps）的AI架构设计、核心引擎实施及AI技术体系落地。 该岗位要求具备深厚的AI系统架构功底、优秀的工程实现能力以及对大模型、智能体（Agent）与高性能计算的系统化理解。 候选人将在智能运维、智能体体系构建及行业解决方案设计等多个方向上发挥关键作用。 主要职责包括： 新技术研究与引入 ：持续跟踪AgenticOps及业界AI新趋势，评估前沿AI、大模型、智能体等技术在运维领域的应用价值。 架构设计与技术规划 ： 主导应用AIOps的整体AI技术架构设计，制定AI赋能运维的系统化规划与落地路线。 负责重点项目及典型业务场景的智能化运维方案设计与实施推进，输出行业级AI应用运维解决方案。 AIOps技术实现与优化 ： 设计与实现多智能体（Multi-Agent）协作架构，实现复杂编程任务的自动拆解与分布式执行。 深入研究并应用大语言模型（LLM）、RAG、Prompt工程与智能体编排技术，推动AIOps系统向自学习、自优化方向演进。 构建AI知识图谱与智能决策引擎，实现根因分析、异常检测与自愈自动化。 应用运维AIOPs场景的落地实现及持续优化 跨部门协作与落地推进 ：与研发、运维、数据及平台团队紧密合作，确保AI方案在生产环境中的稳定性与可扩展性。 知识传播与业界交流 ：组织内部AI知识分享，参与行业研讨与合作交流，持续提升公司在AIOps领域的技术影响力。 岗位要求 1. 教育与经验： 计算机科学、人工智能、数据科学或相关专业； 具备5年以上AI架构、数智化转型或AIOps相关经验 ，拥有千万级大型AI项目的架构与落地经验。 2. 专业技能要求： AIOps与架构能力： 深入理解智能运维体系架构、日志分析、异常检测、根因分析及自动化闭环体系。 AI与大模型技术： 精通LLM、RAG、Prompt Engineering、智能体（Agent）编排、模型微调与分布式训练。 高性能计算与系统优化： 掌握异构计算（GPU、CPU、NPU等）、分布式调度、图计算与模型加速技术。 AI全链路工程化： 熟悉特征工程、数据管线、模型训练、推理部署与MLOps工具链（Kubeflow、Azure AI、ServiceNow AI等）。 编程与系统实现能力： 具备扎实的代码实现与调优能力，精通Python、C++或Rust，能独立完成复杂AI系统的研发与调试。 开源与生态理解： 熟悉AI与AIOps主流开源框架（如Transformers、PyTorch、Ray、LangChain、Kedro、OpenTelemetry等）。 AI算法能力： 深入研究并创新应用于AIOps场景的关键算法，包括但不限于异常检测与时序预测算法、事件关联与根因分析算法、日志与告警智能分析算法、强化学习与自动决策算法等。 3. 综合素质要求： 具备良好的中英文沟通与文档撰写能力，可参与国际项目与技术交流。 拥有强烈的技术探索精神和创新意识，对新技术具备快速学习与验证能力。 具备系统化思维和架构全局观，能协调多团队资源推动复杂项目落地。 具有良好的知识分享与技术影响力，能够引领团队在AI与运维结合领域持续创新。 四、加分项（Preferred） 有参与或主导AIOps平台或AI基础设施从0到1建设经验。 具备多行业AI落地经验（如金融、电信、制造、能源等）。 参与主流开源AI项目或智能体框架开发经验者优先。 Additional

requirements: Principais responsabilidades da posição Reportando-se ao Gerente de Engenharia de Testes, as principais responsabilidades são: Planejar, implementar e suportar a introdução de novos produtos (NPI), garantindo a estabilidade e disponibilidade dos sistemas de testes utilizados na manufatura. Administrar, monitorar e manter a infraestrutura de testes, incluindo servidores, switches, redes, fibras ópticas e estações de teste, assegurando alta disponibilidade para as operações produtivas. Desenvolver, validar e sustentar planos de testes para novos produtos, garantindo conformidade com os requisitos técnicos, de qualidade e manufatura. Atuar na análise e resolução de falhas complexas em sistemas de teste, infraestrutura e processos produtivos, utilizando metodologias de análise de causa raiz e ações corretivas e preventivas. Liderar iniciativas de melhoria contínua voltadas para produtividade, redução de downtime, aumento da disponibilidade dos equipamentos e otimização dos processos de testes. Implementar e sustentar soluções, ferramentas e estratégias definidas pelo time global de Engenharia de Testes, garantindo sua aplicação no ambiente local. Monitorar indicadores de desempenho (KPIs) relacionados à infraestrutura de testes, disponibilidade dos sistemas e eficiência operacional, propondo planos de melhoria. Atuar em parceria com as áreas de Engenharia, Produção, Qualidade, TI e fornecedores para implantação de projetos, padronização de processos e resolução de problemas técnicos. Elaborar e manter documentação técnica, procedimentos operacionais e soluções de automação para suporte aos processos produtivos e de engenharia. Informações acadêmicas e pessoais Idioma : Inglês Intermediário. Formação : Ensino Superior completo em Engenharia Elétrica, Eletrônica, Computação, Software, Telecomunicações ou áreas correlatas. Conhecimentos Técnicos Arquitetura de hardware de PCs, notebooks e servidores (Intel e AMD). Sistemas operacionais Windows, Linux, Windows Server e ambientes de virtualização. Infraestrutura de redes, switches, servidores e cabeamento estruturado. Desejável conhecimento em SQL, Python, C++, Shell Script e SAP. Desejável certificação ou conhecimentos em Lean Six Sigma (Yellow Belt ou Green Belt). Principais competências pessoais Perfil Analítico Organização Atenção aos Detalhes Proatividade Foco em Resultados Resolução de Problemas Trabalho em Equipe Boa Comunicação Capacidade de Atuação em Ambientes Complexos Senso de Responsabilidade Local de trabalho: Indaiatuba/SP – 100% presencial. Additional

requirements: 岗位职责： 1、对接底层驱动团队，负责整机温控、功耗、稳定性三个方向的优化 2、针对设备发热、耗电快、待机功耗高、续航劣化等问题，输出解决or优化方案，主导or协同底层驱动团队落地 3、针对整机死机、重启、卡顿、ANR、Crash 等稳定性问题，独立梳理问题链路，输出分析结论与整改方案并主导落地 岗位要求： 1、具备 Android整机温控、功耗、稳定性相关经验，熟悉Android系统架构与电源管理机制 2、深入理解整机温控策略、功耗耗电模型、电池电量管理、待机 / 亮屏功耗链路，熟悉常见发热、续航、稳定性问题根因定位思路 3、熟悉 Log、Kernel log、Trace、功耗维测工具，能独立分析温控、耗电、死机重启类复杂问题 4、了解 Linux 进程调度、内存管理、电源休眠、温控调频调压基础原理，能看懂底层相关配置与策略逻辑 5、具备良好跨团队协同能力，可独立对接底层各技术团队，能够输出技术方案、对齐接口与策略、推动协同优化落地 6、逻辑清晰、责任心强，有主动优化意识，能主动发现版本潜在风险，擅长复盘沉淀、推进问题闭环。 Additional

requirements: Why Work at Lenovo We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers. Lenovo is a US$69 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its success as the world’s largest PC company with a full-stack portfolio of AI-enabled, AI-ready, and AI-optimized devices (PCs, workstations, smartphones, tablets), infrastructure (server, storage, edge, high performance computing and software defined infrastructure), software, solutions, and services. Lenovo’s continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. Lenovo is listed on the Hong Kong stock exchange under Lenovo Group Limited (HKSE: 992) (ADR: LNVGY). To find out more visit www.lenovo.com and read about the latest news via our StoryHub. Business Area Description: The Lenovo Workplace Services serves as a Single Point of Contact (SPOC) for End User Managed Services and delivers comprehensive IT support to Customer’s end users. Integrated with our customer’s IT functions and processes, Lenovo Workplace Services performs end to end support, both locally and remotely on behalf of our customers. Key Objectives of Position: Asset Management Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and governance of all hardware asset data by overseeing projects, service processes, equipment collection rounds, and asset movements, maintaining up-to-date records in the management system. Day-To-Day Tasks: Oversee projects, service processes, and asset movements to ensure data accuracy in asset management systems Manage the registration and lifecycle tracking of IT hardware (desktops, notebooks, VDIs, and monitors) Control equipment disposal and ensure proper inventory write-off in administrative systems Proactively identify and correct data inconsistencies, and support users with inventory-related requests Coordinate hardware collection, reuse, and full lifecycle management (HLCM), including reporting and KPIs Manage leasing contracts and support for refresh programs Prepare reporting for regular customer and Lenovo meetings. Perform as a customer satisfaction escalation point for your team. Attend regular customer meetings. Key Competencies Needed: Good customer communication skills, verbal and written. Possesses and applies more specialized knowledge in hardware and software support technologies. Handles intermediate-level tickets, performing diagnosis and fault resolution. Understands how their work impacts results within their area. Demonstrates knowledge of internal operations and builds relationships to facilitate workflow. Prepares detailed technical reports. Experience with ITSM toolsets (ServiceNow preferred). Experience with CMDB (ServiceNow preferred). Experience with Flexera tool Desired experience with MS SCCM and MS Intune Previous Experience: 2-5 years previous experience in either an internal or outsourced operation as a Field Services Technician. Qualifications: Undergraduate degree completed in an IT related discipline or Technical high school completed with other IT Certifications. ITIL Foundations IAITAM certification is desired Additional

requirements: Role Overview We are seeking a Software Developer (Back-End / Front-End / Full-Stack) to design, build, and maintain scalable applications and integrations that enable modern digital service delivery. This role focuses on platforms such as ServiceNow, automation technologies, and cloud environments, helping to drive innovation across IT services, automation, and user experience. requirements: About the Team We are a hardware engineering team supporting hyperscale server programs, primarily working on Microsoft-related projects. The team focuses on new product introduction (NPI), covering end-to-end server system development from design to production. Engineers collaborate closely across regions, with most cross-functional meetings aligned to Asia working hours. Role Summary We are looking for a Hardware Engineer with experience in server or system-level design to support rack-level server development (L10/L11). This role will be responsible for system hardware design, motherboard development, and cross-functional issue resolution throughout the product lifecycle. Candidates with desktop (DT) or notebook (NB) experience are also welcome, especially if they have solid motherboard design exposure. Key Responsibilities Design and develop hardware systems for server products, including motherboard-level design Execute system hardware design, bring-up, functional verification, and validation activities Develop test fixtures and support debugging, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis Create and maintain system and motherboard documentation Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g., firmware, manufacturing, quality) to resolve hardware/software issues Support product lifecycle stages including NPI, pilot run, demo, and mass production Participate in design reviews, propose technical solutions, and align with customer requirements (e.g., Microsoft) Work with global teams to ensure design readiness and manufacturing success Skills & Experience Must-have 3–10 years of hardware design experience Hands-on experience in motherboard or system-level hardware design Good understanding of server system architecture Experience with hardware bring-up, debugging, and validation Ability to work across product development stages (design → production) Strong problem-solving and cross-functional communication skills Good-to-have Experience in server platform development (preferred) Experience with rack-level server systems (L10/L11) Background in NPI programs Exposure to hyperscale/datacenter customers Experience collaborating with global teams Additional

requirements: The Brand Project Specialist is the operational engine of Motorola's Global Brand team — ensuring that campaigns, creative workstreams, and cross-functional processes move with clarity, speed, and precision. This role sits at the intersection of project management and brand execution, partnering closely with campaign and brand Managers to keep complex, multi-track work on schedule and on brief. This is a role for someone who thrives in structured complexity — equally comfortable building a workback schedule and facilitating a cross-functional alignment meeting. You'll be the connective tissue across our Premium, Core, and Commercial campaign tracks, supporting the team's evolution from a downstream execution function to an upstream author of brand strategy and creative direction. Key Responsibilities: • Manage end-to-end project timelines and workback schedules across multiple simultaneous campaign tracks, ensuring milestones are met and risks are flagged early. • Serve as the day-to-day operational point of contact between the Global Brand team and internal stakeholders including regional marketing teams, product leads, and agency partners. • Build and maintain clear project tracking systems using tools such as Asana, Jira, or equivalent platforms — ensuring the full team has real-time visibility into status, ownership, and dependencies. • Develop regular reporting rhythms to provide timely updates to the Campaign Team Lead and Brand Lead on campaign progress, blockers, and upcoming decision points. • Support budget tracking and forecasting for global brand campaigns, maintaining accurate records of spend against plan and flagging variances proactively. • Coordinate briefing and intake processes to ensure briefs are complete, aligned, and distributed to the right partners at the right time. • Contribute to the continuous improvement of team workflows and operating processes — identifying inefficiencies and proposing solutions that increase team velocity and output quality. • Ensure consistent application of project management standards and documentation practices across all active workstreams. Basic Qualifications: • 3+ years of experience in project management, marketing operations, or brand/creative production — ideally within a global consumer brand, agency, or tech environment. • Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field. Preferred Qualifications: • PMP, Agile, or equivalent certification is a plus, not a requirement. • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with overlapping timelines, stakeholders, and deliverables Proficiency in project management tools (Asana, Jira, Monday.com, or similar); comfort adopting new platforms as needed. Must be based in or willing to relocate to Chicago, IL. This is an on-site role with no remote flexibility. Skills & Competencies • Strong organizational skills with a bias toward structure, clarity, and follow-through — you close loops and don't let things fall through the cracks. • Clear, confident communicator — written and verbal — with the ability to manage up, across, and externally without losing the thread. • Collaborative by nature; able to work with creative, strategic, and operational stakeholders without defaulting to any one mode. • Comfortable working in ambiguity — especially during a period of organizational evolution — while still bringing order and momentum. • Foundational understanding of marketing metrics, campaign anatomy, and creative production timelines. • Detail-oriented without losing sight of the bigger picture; knows when to escalate and when to solve. The base salary budgeted range for this position is $85k-95K. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission. Lenovo’s various

Job Description Summary: This role is focused on strengthening the Lenovo and Intel partnership across North America REL Commercial by turning joint priorities into meaningful business outcomes. You will help connect segment strategy, seller engagement, customer needs and alliance investment to accelerate revenue growth, improve share position and increase the impact of Intel-based solutions across key commercial routes to market. The ideal candidate will operate as a strategic connector across Lenovo, Intel and the sales organization, bringing business insight, product fluency and strong stakeholder management to drive alignment and execution. Success in this role will require the ability to influence across teams, simplify complex product portfolio, enable sellers with confidence and ensure Lenovo is positioned to capture growth opportunities in a fast-moving commercial IT market. Your Responsibilities will include: Collaborating with REL Commercial segments – Enterprise/Corporate, Global Accounts, Public Sector in both CDA and the US to drive key priorities for Intel & Lenovo across the PCSD portfolio Driving use of bid bucket dollars to drive revenue and share growth, including measuring usage of seeds/perm evals investments Increasing product knowledge of the Intel platform portfolio across the sales teams Engage with key stakeholders in B6 & Markham sales teams – delivering training, presentations and understanding of joint priorities Be an advocate for Lenovo sellers to Intel Be the “go-to” resource for internal teams on Intel product knowledge Participate in joint business updates and QBR’s Organization: You will be a core member of the Lenovo North America REL Category Organization and will collaborate closely with product management, segment business management, sales, sales leadership, sales operations, alliances and marketing. Required Qualifications: Minimum 5-7 years of previous experience in sales/sales management, category management or product management within the IT industry Familiarity with the North American IT business landscape. Exceptional multitasking ability and adeptness in analyzing diverse market scenarios in a dynamic, fast-moving environment. Excellent interpersonal and communications skills Entrepreneurship and with a high degree of self-motivation and drive Proficiency in Excel, and other office applications Proficient in Qlik, Dynamics, PowerBI Preferred Qualifications: Strong analytical skills and detail oriented Strong collaboration skills and ability to work cross functionally Ability to deal with ambiguity and identify approaches & solutions Creative Problem-solving Strong Operational Skills We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Job Description Summary: In partnership with Qualcomm, you will drive the creation and implementation of successful program strategies, plans, and processes that fuel collaborative sales success with Lenovo. Your informed vision will resonate across Lenovo's Alliances, Category, Operations, Marketing and Sales organizations, shaping business outcomes and steering strategic direction. Expect to lead and contribute to customer briefings, sales trainings and both industry and reseller events to optimize pre-sales activities for joint AI PC sales efforts that align with both Qualcomm and Lenovo. Your role will also encompass liaising with cross-functional segments and sales organizations to understand account sales plans while thoroughly managing the go-to-market AI PCs by Snapdragon sales strategy. Your influence will extend throughout the organization, making you a pivotal and visible team member across Lenovo. Detailed Responsibilities: Regular Business Management System (BMS) Reporting: Provide regular updates on the status of the QC commercial business across key KPI’s. Ensure alignment on priorities across both internal and external teams to drive the overall Lenovo QC commercial business priorities. Pilot/Seed Program management: Manage the end-to-end pilot program by working collaboratively with internal sales and external sales and technical engineers to ensure seeding requests get fulfilled, customers are moving through the sales cycle, and capture reporting. Customer Briefing Events: You'll deliver engaging events targeting new customer lines of business conversions, highlighting the return on investment and appealing total cost of ownership potential of Lenovo AI PCs powered by Snapdragon. Sales Training and Enablement: Leverage your expertise to equip our sales team members with the Lenovo and Qualcomm joint value proposition, enhancing their capabilities to scale Snapdragon-based PCs within their customers through consultative positioning techniques and overcoming objections. Quarterly Business Reviews: Participate in business reviews, presenting to executive leadership, working closely with Field and Inside Sales managers to identify opportunities, align strategies, course correct initiatives, measure success and foster growth Industry Vertical Sales Plans and Strategies: Craft and execute curated go-to-market sales plans, enablement, and initiatives to penetrate strategic customer segments effectively. Organization: You will be a core member of the Lenovo North America Category Organization and will collaborate closely with product management, segment business management, sales, sales leadership, sales operations, and marketing. Basic Qualifications: 5+ years 'of previous experience in international sales/sales management/marketing within the IT industry. Familiarity with the North American Workstation business landscape. Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint and other office applications Proficient in Qlik, Dynamics, PowerBI Preferred Qualifications: Strong analytical skills and detail oriented Strong collaboration skills and ability to work cross functionally Ability to deal with ambiguity and identify approaches & solutions Creative Problem-solving Strong Operational Skills Exceptional multitasking ability and adeptness in analyzing diverse market scenarios in a dynamic, fast-moving environment. Superb communication skills and English fluency Excellent interpersonal and communications skills Entrepreneurship and with a high degree of self-motivation and drive This position requires candidates to be based near our headquarters in Morrisville, NC and adhere to a 3:2 hybrid work schedule. This means three days on-site for in-person collaboration and two days remote each week, ensuring flexibility while maintaining strong team engagement. . We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Retail Partnerships Manager - Smartphone Financing/Consumer Finance · Fintech · Brazil · LATAM Expansion 📋 Overall Description: We are looking for a commercially driven and relationship-oriented Business Development Manager for retail partnerships to expand our smartphone financing footprint across Brazil and, progressively, across Latin America. Working at the intersection of sales, business development, and consumer credit, you will be responsible for opening new retail partnerships, structuring commercial agreements, setting and monitoring sales targets, and designing incentive programs that drive volume and partner engagement. This role works in close collaboration with the Sales team, the Senior Credit Risk Manager and fintech partners to ensure that commercial growth is aligned with credit quality and operational sustainability. You will be a connector, someone who understands how retail works on the ground and can translate that into scalable partnership models. 🚀 Key Responsibilities: Retail partner development: Prospect, negotiate and onboard new retail partners — including independent stores, regional chains, telecom retailers and electronics distributors — to expand the smartphone financing distribution network. Manage the full partner lifecycle: from initial pitch and commercial agreement through onboarding, training and ongoing performance management. Build and maintain a qualified pipeline of partnership opportunities across Brazil and target LATAM markets. Develop tiered partnership models and commercial structures aligned with partner size, geography and product mix. Sales targets and performance management: Define, cascade and monitor sales targets by partner, region and product segment — ensuring alignment with the company's financing volume and credit quality goals. Design and manage commercial incentive programs (rebates, commissions, co-marketing, performance bonuses) that motivate partners and drive sell-through. Produce regular performance reports covering activation rates, financed volumes, approval rates and partner-level P&L indicators. Work closely with the Sr Credit Risk Manager to monitor portfolio quality by partner and channel — flagging concentration risks or adverse selection patterns early. Strategic partnerships and fintech collaboration : Manage strategic relationships with fintech partners involved in credit origination, payment processing, or embedded finance infrastructure — ensuring SLAs, commercial terms and integration quality meet business needs. Identify and develop new partnership opportunities that can enhance the distribution or financing capabilities of the platform. LATAM expansion: Support the entry into new LATAM markets by mapping the retail landscape, identifying anchor partners and adapting the commercial model to local dynamics (pricing, distribution norms, regulatory constraints). Coordinate with the Sr Credit Risk Manager to ensure that credit policies, approval rates and risk appetite are calibrated for each new market before commercial launch. Build local partner networks in Mexico, Colombia, Argentina and other target markets — acting as the primary commercial contact during market ramp-up. ✅ Skills & Requirements: 5-8 years of experience in commercial, business development, or partnerships roles — preferably in consumer finance, fintech, telecom or electronics retail. Proven track record of opening and managing retail or channel partnerships at scale, with measurable impact on sales volumes. Experience designing and executing commercial incentive programs and sales target frameworks for retail or distribution networks. Solid understanding of consumer credit products — particularly CDC (Crédito Direto ao Consumidor), installment financing, or BNPL (Buy Now Pay Later) — and how credit approval dynamics affect retail conversion. Strong negotiation skills and experience structuring commercial agreements with retail chains, distributors, or technology partners. Data-driven mindset — comfortable working with sales dashboards, funnel metrics and partner-level performance reports. Fluency in Portuguese, English and Spanish languages. Availability to work in a hybrid schedule: 3 days on-site and 2 days remote. Preferred Qualifications (considered a plus): Direct experience with smartphone financing or handset distribution in Brazil or LATAM. Existing network of contacts within electronics retail chains, telecom operators, or regional distributors in Brazil or other LATAM markets. Experience with embedded finance or BNPL partnership models in retail contexts. Exposure to fintech partner management — API integrations, white-label credit products, or revenue-share commercial structures. Passion for financial inclusion and expanding credit access to underserved consumers through technology. 📍 This position is located at Av Chedid Jafet, 222 - Vila Olimpia - São Paulo . #MBG Additional

requirements: Are you customer-oriented? Willing to learn and take on a new challenge? In this role you will be able to grow, expand your career and become a future leader within supply chain customer operations. What You Will Do: Own business operations for the German region Own and drive planning / operations excellence with sales Work with supply planner to maximize revenue attainment for German region Order management / scheduling, short-term and long-term planning, driving customer delivery performance and satisfaction Demand Planning, Collaborative Customer Planning etc Lead region in market life cycle planning from NPI through EOL Support the territory general managers, customer fulfillment and revenue attainment as well as wider staff team Help support the growth in Germany and adapting the supply chain to accommodate the organization What We are Looking for: 3+ years of previous experience in a similar position Economics/ Business/ Analytical Bachelor's degree Previous experience in a planning role – CPFR (collaborative planning, forecasting and replenishment) experience an advantage Previous experience of working in Telecoms Manufacturing an advantage Flexible and accustomed to ambiguous situations, motivated to learn, and comfortable working as part of a team of professionals Confident Excel user – you will be presenting data & outcomes from them Fluent English and German, any other language is advantage Communication skills as this will be your everyday too; Knowledge of any extra languages is also an advantage What We Offer: Life Insurance Well-Being Allowance Children of Lenovo Employees Scholarship Program Medical Insurance Employee Referral Bonus Meal Vouchers Employee Assistance Program, e.g., for health, legal & financial consultancy Internal E-learning Development Platform Available for Employees We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. responsibilities: Develop a deep understanding of customer business objectives, challenges, and priorities to deliver tailored solutions Own the end-to-end client relationship and engagement strategy across assigned accounts Identify, develop, and close new business opportunities while expanding existing relationships Build and execute comprehensive account plans to achieve revenue and growth targets Drive revenue performance across the full Lenovo portfolio (ISG, IDG, Services, Solutions) Own forecasting accuracy and financial performance management for assigned accounts Establish and maintain senior-level (C-suite) relationships, positioning yourself as a trusted advisor Lead cross-functional teams to advance opportunities and remove barriers throughout the sales cycle Continuously adapt to Lenovo’s evolving product and services portfolio Deliver exceptional customer experience through proactive engagement and responsiveness Utilize CRM tools (e.g., Microsoft Dynamics) and reporting systems to maintain pipeline visibility and operational excellence Required

requirements: This role is a full‑time position focused on designing and delivering intelligent capabilities for Lenovo’s hybrid cloud platform. The ideal candidate is an experienced AI/ML engineer who enjoys solving complex problems at scale and collaborating closely with product and engineering partners. The role has an international scope and works in close collaboration with global teams in a hybrid working environment. Key Responsibilities Design and develop intelligent features for Lenovo’s hybrid cloud platform using machine learning, data analytics, and generative AI techniques. Build and apply models for classification, anomaly detection, time‑series forecasting, and natural language processing across platform use cases. Collaborate with product managers and engineering teams to translate requirements into scalable, user‑centric AI solutions. Contribute to the full AI lifecycle, including experimentation, iteration, evaluation, and continuous improvement of models. Leverage Lenovo’s data and cloud infrastructure to enable data ingestion, labeling, cleaning, training, and inference workflows. Implement robust evaluation frameworks to ensure models meet accuracy, reliability, and performance expectations. Support deployment of AI solutions into production environments with a focus on stability and scalability. Mentor junior team members and provide technical guidance on AI/ML best practices and implementation approaches. Position Requirements Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field. Minimum of 5 years of professional experience in AI/ML engineering or applied machine learning roles. Strong proficiency in Python; working knowledge of Java or Go is considered an advantage. Hands‑on experience in at least three of the following areas: data modeling, time‑series forecasting, classification, anomaly detection, natural language processing, MLOps, or agentic AI. Experience with data wrangling and analysis using tools such as SQL and Python. Proven experience delivering end‑to‑end machine learning systems with a strong focus on data‑driven evaluation. Experience deploying, monitoring, and troubleshooting AI systems on public or private cloud infrastructure is highly preferred. Excellent communication skills, fluent English reading and communication ability, and a proactive mindset for exploring and applying emerging technologies. What can Lenovo offer you: Compensation & Rewards Performance-based salary reviews and annual bonuses Meal tickets, service anniversary awards, and referral bonuses Employee share purchase program Scholarships for employees’ children Health & Well-being Fully covered private medical insurance Annual well-being allowance for sports, training, relaxation, and therapy, and on-site chair massage Life & accident insurance with extra medical coverage Employee Assistance Program Training & Development Technical Ladder & Sales Excellence programs Individual Career Development Plans with mentoring Bookster subscription for continuous learning Time Away 21–28 vacation days depending on tenure Sick days and extra paid leave for public holidays on weekends Additional paid leave for employees with children You will report to SSG (Solutions & Services Group) organization structure. SSG has been focusing on the expanding IT service market, especially the digital workplace services opportunity, the growing demand for aaS (as a Service) model, and customers’ stronger preference for sustainability services. Meanwhile, SSG has continued to invest in software tools, platforms, and repeatable vertical solutions with our own IP, and focus on vertical solutions in manufacturing, retail, healthcare, education, and Smart City. We are expanding TruScale as a Service to include Digital Workplace Solutions, developing our Hybrid Cloud solutions, and exploring Metaverse solutions We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Lead cross-functional teams in new product cost management for notebook products, including setting cost targets, managing RFQs, handling the cost impact of design changes, and presenting cost outlooks for key product milestones. 2. Lead cost competitiveness analysis and conduct product teardowns to identify cost-saving opportunities in product design and procurement links for notebook products. 3. Check and improve the data accuracy of multiple systems, cooperate with the IT team to fix data issues and prevent data inaccuracies. Job Requirements: 1. Be able to accurately identify the pain points of cost, efficiency and commercialization in each link of the industrial chain and effectively manage cost impact. Additional

requirements: Lenovo’s fundamental belief is that life rewards those who Never Stand Still. Every day at Lenovo we are moving forward always seeking a new way to make the world better. We're seeking a business planning manager responsible for driving market share, revenue, and profit for the North America consumer business. In this role, the candidate will be r esponsible for managing the cycle and annual plans for the consumer Lenovo PC business. This includes planning and execution of quarterly/semi-annual campaigns tied to Lenovo objectives for units, revenue, and profitability. The position will work closely with the Lenovo consumer sales, product management and business management teams. The candidate will need a broad skill set including analytical horsepower, excellent business judgment and product knowledge. They will need strong communication skills, which will enable them to become a trusted advisor in the organization and present to internal executives on a regular basis. Business Planning Responsibilities: • Align with North America category team and Business Units to set key quarterly/annual business objectives including units/share, revenue and profit • Manage Cycle planning process, ensuring key dates and deliverables are met • Work closely with Business and Product management teams to coordinate and consolidate cycle P&L inputs; provide guidance on key metrics (units, rev, profit) to multiple levels of management • Provide relevant market analysis, including sell in and sales out, utilize PFV to optimize go-to-market strategy, etc… • Work closely with sales account executives to plan, execute, and track retail cycles. • Drive initiatives to maximize share, mix, margin and overall product contribution to the portfolio & business. • Evaluate performance against plan at appropriate intervals (e.g., weekly, monthly) and make corrective actions to keep on track to achieve goals. Present details to management teams on a regular basis. • Identify opportunity to partner with alliances to grow business Product related: • Work closely with Product management team on annual STAMP plan to ensure product level TAM and share objectives are captured within our overall plan objectives • Effectively monitor Product Transitions with a view to maximizing revenue & profit • Effectively differentiate Lenovo offerings from competitive alternatives and create distinct customer preference for Lenovo offerings. Knows when to bring elements of competitors' offerings or products into the equation to create the highest yielding deal for the customer and Lenovo. Position Requirements: Technical: • Strong understanding of product life cycle management & retail business model. • Strong understanding of promotional programs mechanics for consumer markets • Good understanding of consumer buying behavior (seasonality) • Good understanding of the PC industry - products and technology • Maintains mastery of competition's offerings, strategies, and plans Non Technical: • Strong drive for results • Strong interpersonal skill & keen attention to details • Ability to manage through influence, negotiation • Strong verbal (presentation) and written communication skills We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: 1. 組織・人材管理 ROチームの業務管理および人材マネジメント（評価、育成、配置） メンバーが適切なタイミングで計画・対応を実行できるようリード ベストプラクティス共有、コーチング、トレーニングの提供 高度な案件における意思決定および支援 2. 問題解決 重大案件（Crit-Sit含む）の解決を企画からクローズまで統括 問題・障害の原因分析および対応策の立案・実行 必要に応じた顧客訪問・説明対応の実施 顧客満足度の回復と再発防止の推進 3. 顧客・ステークホルダー対応 顧客およびパートナーへの進捗報告 顧客向けレポートの作成・レビュー 営業・サービス営業・開発・製造などとの関係構築・改善 4. インシデント統制・ガバナンス ROが対応する案件の進捗監視およびエスカレーション管理 優先度・リスクに応じた対応方針の決定 組織としてのインシデント対応品質の維持・向上 5. リソース・コスト管理 人員・予算・設備の最適配分 部門のコスト管理、計画および実績報告 解決活動の効率化および生産性向上の推進 6. クロスファンクショナル連携 サービス、営業、開発、製造、製品部門と連携し解決策を提供 顧客対応と企業影響のバランスを考慮した意思決定 7. レポーティング インシデントや重要案件の状況を統合し、上位管理層へ報告（週次・月次・四半期） 経営層向けの要約およびリスク可視化 Additional

職務内容 1. インシデント対応サポート 顧客からの問い合わせ・障害対応のサポート 指示に基づいた調査・情報収集の実施 対応状況の記録および進捗更新 必要に応じたエスカレーション対応 2. 顧客対応・コミュニケーション 指示に基づき顧客・パートナーへ進捗報告を実施 顧客向け資料・レポート作成の補助 正確かつ丁寧なコミュニケーションの実施 3. 社内連携 開発・製造・サービス部門からの情報収集のサポート 関係部門との連携による問題解決支援 指示に基づいたタスク実行 4. ナレッジ・改善活動 対応履歴・情報の整理およびナレッジ蓄積 類似案件の対応効率化に向けた改善サポート Lessons Learnedの共有支援 Additional

requirements: Position Summary The DWS Sales Enablement Support role provides administrative and sales support for recurring and managed service opportunities. Working closely with Sales Leads, Account Managers, and Service Delivery teams, the role assists in customer engagements by helping explain service offerings, preparing supporting sales materials, coordinating information requests, and supporting the execution of sales strategies. This role is suited to an individual who enjoys working with people, communicating information clearly, and supporting sales activities. The position provides exposure to customer-facing engagements, managed services, sales processes, and cross-functional collaboration across the business and is located in our Morrisville, N.C. Corporate Office. Key Responsibilities: Sales Support Support Sales Leads and Account Managers throughout the sales cycle for recurring and managed service opportunities. Assist with the preparation of customer presentations, service summaries, proposals, and supporting sales materials. Coordinate information required for customer discussions and sales opportunities. Maintain and organize sales collateral, service information, and supporting documentation. Help ensure customer-facing information is accurate, current, and aligned with approved service offerings. requirements: We are Lenovo! We’re a leader in genuine innovation, dreaming up – and building – the technology and services that enable and inspire progress around the world. Our innovative high-quality PCs & Smart Devices, Data Centers, Mobile and Smart Office products are designed and built with the customer in mind. And it’s our people who make this all happen - we believe different is better and our strength lies in this diversity. We are currently hiring for a Data Center System Administrator in Gujarat, India , with hands-on experience in GPU Infrastructure installation and Support. This role assists customers in monitoring and optimizing the health of the cluster, ensuring the smooth operation, connectivity, reliability, security, and performance of the system. Additional responsibilities include debug any HW or System SW issues, roll out any new SW packages, including upgrading SW and Firmware versions to the latest Best-Recipe (BR) of tested, approved and agreed SW and FW versions by Lenovo Scalable Infrastructure (LeSI) team. The role reports 3rd party issues and follow up on the progress on customer behalf. They will participate in all technical review meetings, serving as customer’s single point of contact, actively attending business review meetings, provide an update on all open cases, advise how to run the System optimally, and pro-actively liaise to coordinate maintenance windows. Other tasks include responding to alerts, using Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) for monitoring, performing preventative maintenance, rolling out and upgrading firmware versions, managing any issues that may arise to minimize downtime and optimize data availability, and ensuring the ongoing support of the cluster. Position Requirements: - Hardware and System Software Debugging and Troubleshooting - GPU Installation, Break-Fix, and Replacement - Server, Storage, Networking Support - Linux System Administration & Monitoring (RHEL, CentOS, Suse) - Opening trouble tickets against different vendors. - Following up and reporting the progress on all issues - Investigate and correct defects in the cluster as reported, adhering to service level. - The effort includes resolving errors through developing, testing and implementing changes to the system. - Provide corrective and preventive maintenance, troubleshoot and isolate defects. - Perform Software and firmware testing for any fixes, upgrades, security patch - Assistance with solution design - Update the documentation when and as necessary to reflect the changes made to the system What Lenovo can offer you: - An exciting job with great opportunities for success - A good potential to grow both professionally and personally - You will be a part of the Number 1 PC vendor in the world - Stimulating data center and office environment At Lenovo we are proud to be an equal opportunity company. This vacancy certainly applies for people with disabilities, too. Additional

requirements: We are currently hiring for a Data Center System Administrator in Chicago, Illinois, with experience in Linux, Unix, RedHat, to work a mix of on-site and remote support to our customers. This role will require you to be on site at the customer's office 3 days a week. As part of the Lenovo Data Center Operations Support team your responsibilities will include: Monitoring, maintaining, and managing the physical infrastructure of a data center, ensuring its smooth operation, reliability, and security. Monitoring power and cooling systems and network connectivity Hardware and system software debugging and troubleshooting. Operating System Management and Deployment Resolving hardware and software issues Responding to alerts, performing preventative maintenance, rolling out and upgrading firmware versions, and managing any issues that may arise to minimize downtime and optimize data availability Become the customers’ Single Point of Contact (SPOC). Following up and reporting the progress on all issues. Respond to users and provide support to them on the daily operations of the cluster. Daily system administration tasks, including granting, deleting access, Investigate and correct defects in the cluster as reported, adhering to the service levels. Resolve errors through developing, testing and implementing changes to the system. Provide corrective and preventive maintenance, troubleshoot and isolate defects. Perform Software and firmware testing for any fixes, upgrades, security patch. Update the customers’ documentation when and as necessary to reflect the changes made to the system. Compile Monthly Reporting and take part in monthly customer Service Reviews where required. Basic Qualifications 3+ years of experience experience in Linux (ie Suse, RHEL & CentOS) and system administration Preferred Qualifications: Experience in Confluent & Slurm Experience in Server, storage and networking Able to perform OS installation and upgrades with no supervision Experience in system troubleshooting, monitoring and support. Customer service skills including written and oral communication with client In CA, CO, CT, DC, HI IL, MD, MA, MN, NV, NJ, NY, OR, RI, WA the base salary range budgeted for this position is $98,000 – $120,000 (annually). requirements: About the Position The Director of NA REL PCSD Business Management is a strategic and transformational leader responsible for driving share, revenue, and profit outcomes for PCSD across the North America Relationship (REL) business. Reporting to the Executive Director for Commercial Product & Business Management, this leader partners closely with Segment Sales Leaders, Product Management, Sales Operations, Finance, and Supply Chain to shape strategy, drive execution discipline, and lead a high-performing business management organization. This role requires a confident executive presence, strong business acumen, and the ability to lead through influence in a complex, matrixed environment, as well as translating strategy into measurable results while developing a best-in-class team. Key Responsibilities 1. Strategy & Business Leadership Own and drive the REL PCSD business strategy across segments, ensuring alignment to NA and WW priorities and delivery of fiscal commitments Lead annual planning, mid-term strategy, and in-quarter adjustments to maximize performance in a dynamic market Translate market insights, competitive dynamics, and portfolio opportunities into clear, executable growth plays Serve as a trusted advisor to Sales Leadership , shaping priorities, identifying risks, and driving accountability 2. Transformation & Growth Execution Identify, incubate, and scale strategic growth initiatives (e.g., acquisition, AI PC, workstation, services attach) Drive end-to-end integration of programs across sales, marketing, channel, and solutions teams Remove structural barriers and accelerate execution to improve speed to market and seller productivity Lead the business through change, continuously evolving operating models, processes, and tools 3. P&L Ownership & Performance Management Own REL PCSD business performance with accountability for revenue, profit, and share outcomes Lead forecasting processes, including quarterly outlook calls and ongoing risk/opportunity management Develop deep expertise in segment and product-level P&L, including margin drivers and investment trade-offs Drive disciplined decision-making on pricing, investments, and large deals to maximize long-term returns 4. Data-Driven Insights & Market Intelligence Establish a best-in-class business management system (BMS) with clear KPIs, inspection cadence, and transparency Leverage internal and external data to generate actionable insights on performance, competition, and growth opportunities Provide clear, executive-level storytelling on business health, trends, and required actions 5. Cross-Functional Leadership Align Product, Sales, Finance, GSC, Marketing, and Channel teams around a single, integrated business plan Ensure inventory, demand planning, and supply strategies align with market opportunities and financial goals Drive cross-functional accountability for delivering results against commitments 6. Team Leadership & Talent Development Lead, coach, and develop a team of high-performing Business Managers Set a high-performance culture focused on ownership, accountability, and continuous improvement Build bench strength and elevate the team’s capability in strategy, analytics, and executive engagement Foster a culture of collaboration, innovation, and proactive problem-solving Basic Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred 10+ years of experience across sales, business management, product, finance, or related roles Proven track record of driving business outcomes (revenue, profit, share) in complex environments Strong financial acumen with deep understanding of P&L management and forecasting Preferred Qualifications Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills with a data-driven mindset Excellent executive communication skills and ability to influence senior stakeholders Demonstrated ability to lead teams, drive transformation, and operate with high ownership and autonomy Comfortable navigating ambiguity and making high-impact decisions at speed This position requires candidates to be based near our headquarters in Morrisville, NC and adhere to a 3:2 hybrid work schedule. This means three days on-site for in-person collaboration and two days remote each week, ensuring flexibility while maintaining strong team engagement. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class.
