requirements: Director, Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) The Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG) is hiring a Director of S&OP to align demand, supply, and inventory across our ISG ISO ESMB organization. Reporting to the Executive Director, ESMB Commercial Group Operations, this leader connects GEO planning, Sales, and the Global Supply Chain (GSC) to translate sales forecasts, demand plans, and incoming supply into a single executable plan — and leads a team of demand, supply, and inventory planning experts who own its execution. Key Responsibilities 1. Own the S&OP Process Lead and manage the monthly S&OP cycle Facilitate cross-functional S&OP meetings Build consensus across business functions on a single, executable plan Lead and develop a team of demand, supply, and inventory planning experts (full people-management responsibility) 2. Demand & Supply Alignment Develop and refine sales forecasts (short-, mid-, and long-term) Align forecasts with production capacity and inventory plans Balance supply and demand while meeting service and cost objectives Ensure production and capacity plans meet demand Support product launches and supply readiness 3. Manage Inventory and Excess Optimize inventory levels and availability to meet service and turn targets Identify excess inventory by reviewing stock positions against demand forecasts, and partner with Sales, Marketing, and Operations on mitigation — price adjustments, promotions, bundling, and liquidation — to protect profitability 4. Initiate and Manage Buy Aheads Partner with GEO Planning and GSC to develop and execute strategic component buy-aheads ahead of supply constraints or pricing inflections Monitor buy-ahead inventory positions against unit and margin targets, and adjust as conditions change 5. Continuous Improvement and Transformation Drive transformation initiatives across the planning organization, including: Improving forecasting processes and planning tools Implementing data-driven planning systems and automation Advancing S&OP process maturity against industry benchmarks 6. Analytics & Performance Management Analyze forecast accuracy, demand variability, and supply constraints to identify risks, gaps, and opportunities Define, track, and report S&OP KPIs (forecast accuracy, plan attainment, inventory turns, service levels) to executive leadership Basic Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Business Administration or relevant degree 8+ years previous experience in supply chain Previous experience in a planning role ERP / planning tools (e.g., SAP IBP, Blue Yonder, etc.) Strong understanding of supply chain processes Preferred Qualifications: Strong communication skills at all levels, including with cross-functional executives Ability to quickly solve ambiguous problems with limited data by utilizing analytic skills and judgment gained during prior experiences Requires strategic agility, business acumen, and functional expertise to resolve problems of highly complex nature We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: 职务描述 Key Responsibilities Design, develop, and maintain backend services for Sales IT systems using Java and Spring Framework/Spring Boot . Build and support microservices (REST APIs, integration patterns, service reliability/observability basics). Develop AI/ML-related code in Python , including data preparation, training experimentation, and evaluation. Participate in AI projects with hands-on model training experience (e.g., feature engineering or embeddings, training runs, metrics, iteration, reproducibility). Integrate trained models into applications (online inference APIs or batch jobs), ensuring performance, stability, and maintainability. Work with relational databases and SQL; collaborate on data modeling and query/performance considerations. Use modern engineering practices and tools (Git, code reviews, testing, CI/CD) to deliver production-quality software. Partner with stakeholders to clarify business needs, translate them into technical solutions, and iterate based on feedback. Demonstrate ownership: break down ambiguous problems, propose options, and drive tasks to completion with minimal supervision. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field (or equivalent experience). Graduates from top-tier universities or those holding a Master’s degree are preferred. Solid programming skills in Java and Python . Familiarity with Spring Framework (Spring Boot preferred). Understanding of microservices architecture and API development. Hands-on experience with at least one ML/DL framework, e.g. PyTorch , TensorFlow, or scikit-learn. Real project experience in AI training (internship, academic, or industry), including training and evaluation of models. Working knowledge of relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL/MySQL/Oracle) and SQL. Proficiency with common development tools and workflows (Git, debugging, build tools, testing). Strong self-motivation and collaboration skills. Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Have) Experience deploying AI models to production (batch/streaming/online inference) and basic MLOps concepts. Familiarity with containers and cloud (Docker/Kubernetes; AWS/Azure/GCP). Experience with distributed systems, messaging, or caching (Kafka/RabbitMQ/Redis, etc.). Ability to speak Chinese (Mandarin) is a plus. What We’re Looking For (Behavioral / Mindset) Ownership & self-directed execution : able to work independently, manage priorities, and communicate progress/risks clearly. Product mindset : understands “why” behind requirements, cares about user/business impact, and can propose improvements instead of only implementing tickets. Ideas & curiosity : brings viewpoints, experiments responsibly, and continuously learns new AI and engineering practices. Team collaboration : works effectively in a global team and contributes to a healthy engineering culture. Additional

INTEL, AMD, NVIDIA. • Working knowledge for Virtualized (VMWare, Nutanix, Red Hat OpenShift, Proxmox), Linux and Windows server environments and applications. • Professional proficiency in Spanish and English is required. • Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills, with the ability to influence and align stakeholders in a matrix organization. • Collaborative, customer-focused, and organized approach, with persistence, sound account discipline, and high professional standards. requirements: The Europe+META Commercial Software 4P Business Manager will be accountable for driving growth, profitability, and market share for Lenovo’s commercial software portfolio in Europe+META. This high-impact, cross-functional leadership position manages the full 4P suite—Product, Price, Promotion, and Place—and works closely with executive leaders, sales, marketing, and operations, reporting directly to the Global Head of Commercial Software Business Manager. Key Responsibilities: Define and execute the go-to-market strategy for the commercial software portfolio, ensuring alignment with global objectives and tailoring to the market. Develop and manage the product portfolio roadmap, lifecycle, and transitions to maximize revenue, margin, and market share. Analyze market trends, customer needs, and sales performance to identify new opportunities and optimize business strategies. Construct and monitor business plans, including forecasting, budgeting, and sales target setting. Oversee product pricing, promotions, and channel strategies in close collaboration with the sales, operations, and marketing teams. Drive execution of demand generation programs, marketing campaigns, and tactical initiatives to achieve financial goals. Conduct regular business reviews with key partners, resellers, and major customers. Serve as the software subject matter expert, supporting sales enablement and delivering training. Monitor and report on KPIs, taking corrective action as necessary to ensure achievement of regional objectives. Provide feedback to global product and pricing teams, and represent the voice of the customer in internal strategy sessions. Support cross-functional leadership in business planning and strategic projects as required. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing, computer science, or a related field. MBA preferred. 5+ years of experience in product management, business management, or commercial portfolio roles—ideally in B2B software or the IT industry. Strong understanding of software product life cycles, pricing models, and channel strategies. Demonstrable analytical skills with experience managing business KPIs, forecasting, and financial planning. Proven ability to deliver results in a matrix, cross-regional environment. Fluent in English; additional languages a plus. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to influence and execute with distributed teams. High degree of self-motivation, initiative, and strategic agility. What Lenovo Can Offer You: Opportunities for career advancement and personal growth Access to a diverse range of training programs to sharpen your skills Performance-based rewards that celebrate your achievements Enjoy the best of both worlds with our hybrid setup: 3 days connecting with the team in the office and 2 days working from the comfort of your home We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. モトローラ・モビリティ・ジャパン合同会社 レノボ傘下でAndroidスマートフォン（edge、gシリーズ）やウェアラブル機器を開発・販売する企業です。2025年6月より北原秀文氏が代表に就任し、日本市場向けに高性能かつコストパフォーマンスの高い端末を供給しています。Motorolaは、意義あるイノベーションを設計し、創造し、世界中の人々に提供することで、人々が周りの世界を発見し、共有し、つながる方法を変えていくことを目指しています。 1928年以来、私たちは業界を前進させ続けてきました。Motorolaにおいて、私たちは誰もが日常生活をもっと楽に、もっと明るく、もっと楽しむために必要な、直感的に使えるモバイル製品とサービスを手軽にご利用いただけるよう努めています。 【社員記事】 もう一度、スマートフォンを面白くしたい_AI時代、“次のインターフェース”を再定義する https://www.talent-book.jp/lenovo/stories/62372 採用背景・ ポジション概要 飽和 市場と言われる日本スマートフォン市場において、Motorolaはチャレンジャーブランドとしてさらなる成長を目指しています。大きな裁量を持ちながら、新たな市場機会を創出し、シェア拡大をともにけん引頂くポジションです。 具体的にはMotorola Japanの成長ドライバーを目指す 、通信キャリアビジネスの中核ポジションです 。 NTT docomo 、KDDI、SoftBank、Rakuten Mobileを担当し、端末採用の獲得から販売拡大（Sell-in / Sell-out）、GTM戦略の立案・実行までを一気通貫でリードいただきます。単なるアカウント営業ではなく、キャリア戦略、製品ローンチ、販売計画、パートナーアライアンスを通じて、日本市場での事業成長を推進するビジネスオーナーとしての役割を期待しています。 主要 顧客 NTT docomo KDDI（au ） SoftBank Rakuten Mobile 各キャリア販売代理店 主な業務内容 キャリアアカウントマネジメント 通信キャリアとの関係構築・強化 端末採用に向けた提案・交渉 キャリア戦略やニーズを踏まえた販売提案 新規採用機会の創出 GTM戦略・販売拡大 新製品ローンチ計画の策定・実行 販売戦略およびチャネル戦略の立案 キャリアとの共同販促・マーケティング推進 Sell-in / Sell-out最大化施策の企画・実行 事業管理 売上・利益・販売台数の管理 販売計画および需要予測 KPIモニタリングおよび事業レビュ ー 市場・競合・キャリア動向分析 ステークホルダーマネジメント Product Marketing Supply Chain Finance Global Team との連携を通じて事業目標達成を推進 新規ビジネス開発 サービス・コンテンツ・流通パートナーとのアライアンス推進 新たなビジネス機会の創出 このポジションの魅力 Motorola Japanの成長戦略を直接リードできる キャリア営業だけでなく、GTM・事業戦略・販売計画まで幅広く経験できる グローバルチームと連携しながら最先端のスマートフォン・AI技術に携われる 裁量が大きく、自ら市場を切り拓くことができる 将来的にBusiness LeadやSales Directorなど事業責任者へのキャリアパスが期待できる KPI 売上（Revenue） 利益（Profit） Sell- in台数 Sell- out実績 通信キャリア採用件数 必須要件 日本国内通信キャリア向けビジネス経験（5年以上） スマートフォンまたはモバイルデバイス業界での営業、事業開発、プロジェクト推進経験 通信キャリアの商流および意思決定プロセスへの深い理解 キャリアとのリレーション構築経験 販売計画の立案・実行経験 社内外関係者を巻き込んだプロジェクト推進力 歓迎要件 KDDI、SoftBank、 docomo 等 キャリア通信社 との強固なネットワーク GTM戦略立案および製品ローンチ経験 P/ L管理経験 アライアンスビジネス経験 グローバルチームとの協業経験 ビジネスレベルの英語力 求める人物像 指示待ちではなく、自ら考え行動できる方 不確実性の高い環境でも成果創出にコミットできる方 高い交渉力と調整力をお持ちの方 数字に基づいて意思決定できる方 チャレンジャー環境を楽しみながら市場を開拓できる方 限られたリソースの中でも創意工夫をもって成果を出せる方 Additional

Lenovo is seeking a business-solutions-driven General Counsel for our growing Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG). The position will report to Lenovo’s Senior Vice President, Chief Legal & Corporate Responsibility Officer (CLCRO). Responsibilities include proactively advising ISG senior leadership and their global business teams in areas such as product and service development and offerings, complex M&A (including CFIUS/FIRRMA), strategic partnerships, security requirements, regulatory compliance and commercial disputes. Further, the General Counsel, ISG will act as an interface and liaison to the Business Group for other Corporate Legal functions such as IP, Ethics & Compliance, Data Privacy, Contracting Center of Excellence and Litigation, the Geo Legal teams and Government Relations & Trade. The candidate should be knowledgeable in the mechanics and economics of the electronics manufacturing industry, and familiar with the pace of an innovative technology company. S/he is expected to be a trusted business advisor and collaborative team member in pursuing key global business initiatives. The GC ISG is a member of the CLCRO’s Senior Leadership Team and also participates in leadership meetings of the EVP & President, ISG. Basic Qualifications: JD Degree from a top-tier US law school. Currently licensed to practice in US. 15+ years post-qualification experience in international business and contract law, along with strong business and financial acumen. Preferred Qualifications: High degree of professional ethics and integrity. Strategic agility. Strength in “issue-spotting” based on broad exposure to multiple fields of law. Sound judgment and advanced analytical skills. Ability to anticipate, proactively manage and mitigate legal risks, and to effectively advise on risk management and mitigation. Capable of managing multiple matters simultaneously, setting priorities and meeting deadlines. “Executive presence” and experience engaging with senior-level colleagues and clients globally. Supervisory experience, serving as coach, sponsor and ally. Excellent oral and written communication skills (including legal drafting). Desired Skills: Undergraduate degree in business or engineering a plus. Combination of law firm and in-house experience. Innovative/creative and practical in crafting solutions that both enable and protect business interests. Deep understanding of dynamics and key business drivers in the technology industry. Track record of structuring and negotiating commercial transactions involving complex technologies. Mandarin, Spanish, Portuguese, other language skills a plus. The base salary budgeted range for this position is $200k - 350K. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission. Lenovo’s various

requirements: The Regional Director, Hybrid Cloud & AI Pre-Sales leads Lenovo’s regional pre-sales organization, driving execution of Hybrid Cloud and AI strategy across a defined geography. This senior leadership role manages a team of solution architects and forward-deployed engineers, partnering closely with sales and delivery teams to accelerate pipeline conversion and win rates. The position operates in a highly collaborative, matrixed environment with regional and global stakeholders. Hybrid working and regional travel are expected. Key Responsibilities Lead and develop a regional team of pre-sales professionals, including solution architects and forward-deployed engineers. Translate global Hybrid Cloud & AI pre-sales strategy into effective regional execution plans. Partner with regional sales leadership to drive pipeline growth, opportunity prioritization, and deal progression. Provide leadership on strategic and complex deals, ensuring high-quality solution design and technical credibility. Support creation of proposals, architectures, and executive-level customer presentations. Engage directly with enterprise customers as a senior technical advisor, aligning solutions to business outcomes. Establish consistent pre-sales processes, standards, and governance across the region. Monitor key performance indicators (e.g., win rate, pipeline conversion, customer satisfaction) and drive continuous improvement. Position Requirements Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred. 10–15 years of experience in pre-sales, solution engineering, technical consulting, or technology services. Proven expertise in Hybrid Cloud, AI, infrastructure, and enterprise solution architectures. Demonstrated success supporting large-scale, complex enterprise sales engagements. Prior experience leading and developing technical or consulting teams. Experience working in a regional and/or matrixed organizational structure. Fluent in English (written and verbal); additional European languages are an advantage. Strong communication, stakeholder management, and coaching skills, with the ability to translate complex technologies into customer value. Why This Role Matters The Regional Director plays a critical role in bringing Lenovo’s Hybrid Cloud & AI strategy to life in the field. By leading a high-performing regional pre-sales team, this role directly impacts customer success, revenue growth, and Lenovo’s reputation as a trusted solutions provider. The Regional Director serves as the key link between global strategy and field execution, ensuring customers receive technically sound, business-aligned solutions at scale. What We can offer You: An international team with a high focus on Gender Diversity.​ Employee Assistance Program, e.g., for psychological, legal & financial consultancy​ You are joining a company that prioritizes sustainable solutions like CO2 Offset, Asset Recovery Services, and the Lenovo Certified Refurbished portfolio.​ Access to training for personal development - Internal E-learning Development Platform Available for Employees ​ Mentorship program and many more...! We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: Motorola - a Lenovo company - is one of the fastest-growing smartphone vendors in Europe, both in market share and branding. With high-impact partnerships like FIFA, Formula 1, Ducati and EuroLeague. We’re looking for a senior leader with solid experience in the smartphone industry to keep the momentum in our French division. The ideal candidate brings a strong and well-established network within the French mobile business, combined with deep market insight and strategic vision. In this role, you will lead the France business , managing a team across sales and marketing, driving performance, developing talent, and shaping the overall strategy with our key customers to deliver strong results. Your responsibilities once you join our team: • Be responsible for handling P&L and assist in the resolution of market access and customer issues as necessary • Lead local team to ensure profitable business growth in the market and bring the organization to the next level • Report business status to the Europe HQ level • Identify, develop, and close key sales opportunities with new customers • Responsible for sales and marketing strategy planning and execution • Develop, create and execute joint business plans with key customers • Create cooperative business plans with customers and their partners and help to define new business models to drive growth • Research, define & recommend an overall “go-to-market" strategy •Leverage AI-driven insights and tools to enhance decision-making, optimize sales performance, and improve customer engagement • Drive the One Lenovo strategy by collaborating across different Business Groups to deliver integrated solutions and maximize overall customer value Position Requirements: • Minimum 10 years of business management experience; People management skills • Experience with technology products, ideally from the smartphone industry • Strong understanding of channel management or channel-based sales models • Experience with customers/channel partners – excellent analytical and communication skills; • Comfortable with financial management and P&L • Dynamic and proactive, with a positive outlook. Self-driven and motivated. Fluency in French and English language Organization: You will be reporting to the Vice President / General Manager of MBG Europe and META. The position requires presence in our office. What we can offer you: • An open and stimulating environment within one of the most forward-thinking IT companies • A multitude of professional and personal opportunities • Flat structures and fast decision-making processes • A modern and flexible way of working to combine personal and professional life • An international team with a high focus on Diversity & Inclusion We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: About the Role Lenovo's Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG) is transforming the way technology is delivered. This critical role sits within the Global Supply Chain (GSC) and focuses on end-to-end (E2E) manufacturing processes for our high-volume Cloud Service Provider (CSP) platforms, Servers, and Storage portfolio. The CSP Test Development Engineer is a highly technical, hands-on position responsible for developing, validating, and sustaining the manufacturing test process to ensure the quality, reliability, and efficiency of our infrastructure products as they move through global manufacturing lines. This role requires a tactical readiness to resolve production issues and a strategic mindset focused on continuous process optimization. As a CSP Test Development Engineer, you will also work with Hardware and Software Development Engineers and external partners to build custom processes as required. Tactically, you must be ready to react to any production issues as they arise and support worldwide manufacturing operations. Strategically, the expectation is that the successful candidate will continually seek ways to optimize the overall process to improve manufacturing efficiency. This is a 100% Onsite position based in our facility in Whitsett, North Carolina. Lenovo has manufacturing facilities in China, Hungary, Mexico, and Brazil, and travel to some of these sites is expected for this role. Key Responsibilities Test Development and Automation Design, develop, write, and maintain test cases and automated scripts to test complex product hardware and firmware within the manufacturing environment. Utilize scripting languages (primarily Python and Java) to automate test flows, data collection, and integration with the manufacturing test platform. Implement and maintain code using version control systems (Git, CVS, SVN) to manage test development assets. Employ Representational State Transfer Application Programming Interface (Rest API) for seamless integration of test tools and processes. Manufacturing Process and Quality Lead the development and deployment of new manufacturing test processes for New Product Introduction (NPI) to ensure high product quality and manufacturability. Collaborate closely with Hardware and Software Development Engineers and external partners to define and build custom processes as required by CSP clients. Provide technical expertise and hands-on debug support for server product hardware and firmware during production and ramp-up phases. Optimization and Support Tactically react to any production issues as they arise, performing root cause analysis and implementing immediate corrective actions to minimize line downtime. Strategically identify opportunities to optimize and improve the overall manufacturing test process for maximum efficiency and reduced cycle time. Support worldwide manufacturing operations with technical guidance and expertise, which will require periodic international travel to Lenovo's sites (China, Hungary, Mexico, Brazil). Basic Qualifications Education: Bachelor’s degree in engineering (Computer Engineering, Computer Science), or a similar technical discipline. Experience: Minimum 5 years of experience in a related technical role, ideally in manufacturing or server hardware product testing environments for computer systems or electronic devices. Technical Skills: Must have demonstrated experience in writing test cases for complex enterprise/server-class hardware. Proficiency in scripting with Python (3.x preferred) and familiarity with Java. Experience with code versioning and tracking systems (CVS, Git, SVN). Solid understanding of server product hardware and firmware operation and debug. Preferred Qualifications Extensive experience with Rest API development/integration for test systems. Direct experience with hardware manufacturing test process development and deployment in a high-volume setting. Proven track record of improving manufacturing efficiency through process development and automation. Familiarity with High Availability (HA) requirements and the specific needs of CSP/Hyperscaler environments. Strong technical Writing skills for documentation and process definition. Visa Sponsorship: This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship, including Optional Practical Training (OPT) or Curricular Practical Training (CPT). All applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States for any employer. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Lenovo is seeking a Hybrid Cloud Power and Cooling Support Manager. This role is focused on supporting key relationships with globally based clients, supporting sales, and managing as well as scheduling the Hybrid Cloud Services (HCS) Power and Cooling (PNC) support team while contributing to the business review and revenue recognition This position represents a rewarding opportunity to use your experience and skills in a dynamic, complex organization and to deliver across our major customer sites. Responsibilities: As the Global PNC Support Manager, you will lead the technical strategy, operational support, and lifecycle management for the high-density infrastructure powering our most advanced Neptune® liquid cooled AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) clusters. This is not a traditional facility management role. You will be the primary authority on Liquid Cooling (LC) technologies—including Direct-to-Chip (DTC) and Rear Door Heat Exchangers (RDHx)—ensuring that our global power and thermal envelopes can support the extreme demands of next-generation GPU and TPU architectures. You will bridge the gap between hardware engineering, facility operations, and sustainability teams Activities include Management of the global technical support team performing HPC and AI cluster administration Coordinate with delivery organizations to maintain high performance levels for service-related processes and continuous improvement Partner with internal R&D and silicon vendors (e.g., NVIDIA, AMD, Intel) to align facility capabilities to support future hardware thermal design power (TDP) roadmaps. Manage relationships with global cooling OEMs (e.g., Vertiv, Schneider Electric, nVent, etc) to ensure rapid spare-parts availability and specialized technician support. Act as the highest escalation point for thermal, liquid or power-related outages. Lead Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for leaks, pressure drops, or thermal excursions, implementing global remediations. Serve as the global lead for supporting Lenovo’s solution for Liquid-to-Liquid and Liquid-to-Air cooling systems, overseeing Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), secondary fluid loops, and manifold integrations. Develop and standardize global Maintenance Window (MW) procedures for "hot-swapping" liquid-cooled components in production AI environments. Review, Statement of Work (SoW), projects size and scope. Take ownership of the most critical incidents, coordinate with resolution parties, and establish effective communication between stakeholders Provide accurate and regular reports on the performance of service delivery Define the best operating model along with KPIs ensuring the best customer experience utilizing different teams within Lenovo. Basic Qualifications 5+ years internal or external experience in applicable field of expertise, ideally in the information technology, high-performance computing and support services industry. Experience managing a technical support team. Experience managing infrastructure across different regulatory environments (EUROPE, META, North America, APAC and LATAM). Preferred Qualifications Ability to explain the "Thermal Resistance" of a cold plate to a C-suite executive or a facility technician with equal clarity. Remains calm and analytical during high-stakes infrastructure failures. Excellent interpersonal skills and strong relationship building with proven ability to motivate and influence across teams. Strong support service delivery, time, project, and priority management skills. Experience with managing critical and complex customer situations or incidents. Project Management/Change Management/ ITIL certification is a plus. Commercial experience in information technology and high-performance computing, with familiarity with the use of cloud services economics is a plus. Training or experience in negotiation ad project accounting Additional languages such as German, Spanish or French would be an advantage Professional Engineer (PE) license. CDCP (Certified Data Centre Professional) or specialist cooling certifications. Experience with Immersion Cooling (Single or Two-Phase) is a significant plus. In CA, CO, CT, DC, HI IL, MD, MA, MN, NV, NJ, NY, OR, RI, WA the base salary range budgeted for this position is 150,000-195,000. Individuals may also be considered for bonuses and/or commissions. Lenovo’s various

requirements: We are Lenovo! We’re a leader in genuine innovation, dreaming up – and building – the technology and services that enable and inspire progress around the world. Our innovative high-quality PCs & Smart Devices, Data Centers, Supercomputing, Mobile and Smart Office products are designed and built with the customer in mind. And it’s our people who make this all happen - we believe different is better and our strength lies in this diversity. We are currently hiring for an HPC System Administrator in London, United Kingdom, with experience in Linux, Unix, RedHat, Server, Storage, Networking, to work a mix of on-site and remote support to our customers. As part of the Lenovo Managed Services team your responsibilities will include: Monitoring, maintaining, and managing the physical infrastructure of a data center, ensuring its smooth operation, reliability, and security. Monitoring power and cooling systems and network connectivity Hardware and system software debugging and troubleshooting. Responding to alerts, performing preventative maintenance, rolling out and upgrading firmware versions, and managing any issues that may arise to minimize downtime and optimize data availability Performing software and operating system upgrades to latest Best Recipe Resolve errors through developing, testing and implementing changes to the system. Provide corrective and preventive maintenance, troubleshoot and isolate defects. Perform Software and firmware testing for any fixes, upgrades, security patch. Position requirements: Strong experience in Linux environments (Suse, RHEL, CentOS) including RedHat distributions Hands-on experience with HPC tools and schedulers such as Slurm and PBS Pro, as well as Confluent Solid scripting and automation skills using Ansible, Bash, and Python Experience with distributed storage systems and file systems (GPFS, Weka, DDN, NFS, SMB) Knowledge of advanced networking technologies (InfiniBand, RDMA, ROCE) Experience with version control and automation practices (GitLab, GitOps) Strong background in system administration, monitoring, troubleshooting, and operational support, including security patching Hands-on expertise with hardware and infrastructure (server, storage, networking), including installation, break-fix, OS deployment, and firmware/software upgrades independently What Lenovo can offer you: Holiday purchase Private medical Income protection Attractive pension scheme Positive work life balance Learning and development Life insurance Lenovo and Motorola products discounts Lifestyle discounts Cycle to work MyGymDiscounts Mortgage advice and support Referral bonus Free onsite parking We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: Job Responsibilities： Deep Business Relationship Management & Expectation Setting Strategic Alignment & Proactive Enablement: Regularly participate in business unit operating reviews to deeply understand annual KPIs and pain points. Proactively propose IT and AI solutions that address these challenges. Shift from “passive order‑taking” to “active value‑delivery,” positioning yourself as a business partner rather than a back‑office supporter. AI Use‑Case Discovery & Value Articulation: Actively engage with each business function to identify and prioritise high‑impact AI/GenAI applications (e.g., intelligent Q&A, contract comparison, demand forecasting, RPA‑based auto‑entry). Translate AI’s ROI into business to eliminate the perception. Stakeholder Mapping & Influence: Identify key decision‑makers, influencers, and potential blockers within each business unit. Tailor your communication strategy accordingly. Maintain executive trust through regular one‑on‑one lunches or quarterly business reviews (QBRs), ensuring that business heads come to you first with system or AI concerns, rather than escalating complaints to senior management. Internal Negotiation & Conflict Resolution Priority Bargaining (Resource Allocation): Facing multiple business units submit urgent IT or AI demands simultaneously – and IT resources (development, operations, compute, budget) are limited – lead an internal negotiation. Use data and objective criteria (urgency, business impact, expected value) to guide stakeholders toward a consensus on sequencing, persuading lower‑priority requesters to defer or scale back, ensuring resources are concentrated on the company’s highest‑value strategic initiatives. Scope Definition & Anti‑Scope‑Creep: Faced with vague or ever‑changing requirements, employ professional negotiation techniques to define a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) boundary. Strike the right balance between “everything we want” and “technical feasibility/cost.” AI Expectation Management (Critical Addition): Counter overly optimistic expectations about AI (especially LLMs by presenting quantitative metrics . Use live demos, industry benchmarks, and data samples to “cool down” expectations, clearly defining the capability boundaries, accuracy ceilings, and the need for human‑in‑the‑loop fallback mechanisms, thus avoiding trust erosion due to unmet expectations. Data Readiness & Governance Cost Bargaining: Convince business owners to invest the necessary human resources for data cleansing, labelling, and structural remediation, articulating clearly that “high‑quality data in = high‑value AI out.” Change Management & Crisis Communication Pre‑launch Expectation Guidance: Before new systems or AI features go live, proactively communicate the “pain period” to business heads, managing their desire for perfectionism and persuading them to embrace an iterative, phased rollout. Major Incident Negotiation & Buffering: In the event of a critical system failure or severe AI “hallucination” that disrupts business operations, act as the primary interface to business executives. Use transparent yet strategic communication to defuse anxiety and anger, buy precious time for the technical recovery team, and minimize negative publicity. Job Requirements： Experience & Background Major in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Software Engineering, Information Management, or a related field (Master’s preferred). 8+ years of experience in enterprise IT / digital transformation, with at least 3+ years in an IT Business Partner (or equivalent stakeholder‑facing technology management) role. Proven track record of delivering 3+ AI/LLM projects from PoC (Proof of Concept) to production . Hands‑on experience with AI enablement in at least one core business domain (Sales, Supply Chain, Finance, Customer Service, etc.). AI Technical Architecture Skill Model Selection & Evaluation Framework RAG (Retrieval‑Augmented Generation) Implementation Supervision AI Agent & Tool‑Calling Design Security, Compliance & Content Guardrails Cost Modelling & Performance Optimization Core Soft Skills (Negotiation & Relationship) Exceptional upward and cross‑functional influencing skills – ability to drive alignment without formal authority, using data, trade‑offs, and empathy. Master of conflict resolution – remains calm and rational in high‑pressure, emotionally charged situations; skilfully turns confrontation into constructive dialogue using “follow‑and‑lead” techniques. Additional

requirements: This role is open for the Edinburgh, Scotland
responsibilities: Strategic Business Partnership • Act as a trusted strategic advisor to senior leaders within the CTO Organization across the UK, Israel, and KSA, translating business priorities into people, organizational, and capability strategies. • Contribute directly to business and functional strategy by identifying organizational needs, assessing capability gaps, and shaping solutions aligned to local and global objectives. • Support leaders through periods of growth, transformation, and increasing technical complexity. Organization, Talent & Workforce Strategy • Partner with leaders to design and evolve organization structures, workforce plans, and role architectures that enable innovation and scalable delivery. • Shape and deliver talent strategies in partnership with Talent Acquisition, ensuring access to scarce technical and AI related skills across Europe and Meta. • Support leadership succession, high potential identification, and development planning within critical technical populations. Culture, Capability & Engagement • Drive initiatives that build inclusive, high performance cultures, aligned to Lenovo values while respecting local market dynamics. • Coach leaders on people leadership, change management, and decision making in complex, matrixed environments. • Use engagement insights and qualitative feedback to inform targeted actions that improve employee experience and organizational effectiveness. Employee Relations, Risk & Compliance • Provide expert guidance on employee relations, local employment legislation, and people risk management across UK, Israel, and KSA, partnering closely with Legal and local HR teams. • Ensure consistent, fair, and legally compliant application of HR policies while applying sound judgement in complex employee matters. • Balance commercial agility with strong governance and risk awareness. Global & Regional Collaboration • Work closely with Europe and Meta HR colleagues and global HR partners in the US and China to align regional execution with global people strategies. • Contribute to global HR initiatives and pilots, bringing Europe and Meta market insight and practical application experience.

requirements: Band 6: Hardware Engineer (EE Role) What you'll do: The position is for a Hardware Development Engineer in the Lenovo Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), based in Bangalore, Karnataka. This is an exciting role in the ThinkSystem Server Development organization that has a strong focus to be the #1 trusted IT Partner in the Datacenter market. The hardware development engineer will be an integral member of a team responsible for developing innovative and complex server designs to meet customer and business needs. These organic designs provide exposure to the latest technologies from Lenovo and its development partners to meet market demands. The role also entails solving unique client challenges through the custom development of solutions. Required skills: • Bachelor’s Degree in Electronics/Computer Engineering. • 2+ years of in-depth experience in x86 architecture and hardware design. Server design experience is strongly preferred. • Knowledge and design experience of DDR, PCIe, USB, Ethernet, SPI, I2C along with High-speed PCB design and signal integrity will be an essential requirement. • Experience in EDA schematic design tools for PCB schematic creation, supporting physical/Layout design activity, hardware bring-up and validation will be required • Strong problem-solving skills with an ability to triage and resolve complex hardware issues, working seamlessly with other engineers on problem investigation will be essential. • Strong written and verbal communication skills for a variety of tasks, potentially including customer engagements, architecture design/feedback, and executive updates. • Demonstrated ability to work effectively in both a team environment and self-directed; passionate about learning new technologies and skills. Preferred skills: • Master’s Degree • Previous experience in High-speed board design, Signal Integrity, FPGA code development and validation, DC Power circuit design, and/or PCIe bus design, simulation, and validation. • Proficiency in Linux/Windows Operating systems. Experience with OS virtualization. • Strong proficiency in shell scripting (BASH/Perl/Python and Windows Power Shell) • Demonstrated successful hardware design execution on a project with engineering team members located in multiple geographies. • Business acumen to engage with various functional teams required to take projects from concept to production. Additional

requirements: Lenovo is currently seeking an experienced Solutions and Services Executive. As a Solutions and Services Executive (SSE), you will be entrusted with nurturing and owning the SSG relationship with the customer across the lifecycle of a deal. The SSE will orchestrate deals that resonate with customer needs and bring the very best of the SSGs offerings. The candidate will also evaluate customer opportunities as presented in, RFPs/RFQs or engagements, from the Services and Hardware Sales teams and use this information to manage the sales engagement process to ensure we meet the customer’s requirements. Lenovo solutions can include Services, Hardware, Software, Security, ISVs, and additional 3rd Party Services from Lenovo Business partners. This role will provide dedicated support across our Healthcare R&D and Acquisition efforts. While the position is fully remote, we are seeking candidates located within the Central and Western regions to ensure strong alignment with regional hiring needs, time zones, and stakeholder collaboration. Candidates should be comfortable working remotely while partnering closely with cross-functional teams across the territory. Key Responsibilities: Sales Expertise: Partner across multiple organizations including, but not limited to, sales; finance; service delivery; customers and business partners and lead all elements of the services sales cycle Define framework to manage long term strategy & forecasting by effectively and consistently use Microsoft Dynamics and other reporting tools to track key sales metrics and consistently meet those metrics Develop, implement, and execute an effective sales strategy to achieve sales goals Account Engagement: Work within dedicated territory/segment and work in concert with the Client Manager who is the lead person on the account and, if applicable, the Service and Solution Domain Specialist (SSDS) Partner with the Client Manager to develop, implement, and execute an effective account plans and customer engagement strategy to achieve sales goals Team with the Client Manager to develop C level relationships and serve as a trusted consultant to customers Portfolio Acumen: Display deep understanding of E2E strategic portfolio for example DWS Ability to understand customers business and IT challenges and position the appropriate Lenovo solutions Ability to discuss complex solutions, software, Device as a Service (DaaS) and Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS). Strong knowledge of Digital Workplace Solutions and Device as a Service. Exceptional communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. Self-motivated, results-oriented, and able to work independently. Posses a proactive and driven approach to identify and pursue new sales opportunities Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, competitive sales environment. Willingness to travel as needed Experience selling technology services Experience growing master service agreements and statements of work Proven ability to develop strategies to penetrate and sell to large companies Trusted advisor for industry-specific insights and thought leadership to customers We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Manage project timelines, deliverables, risks, budgets, and resource allocation. Coordinate with business stakeholders, technical teams, vendors, and leadership throughout project lifecycles. Drive Agile/Scrum project execution and ensure adherence to delivery best practices. Oversee configuration, customization, workflows, integrations, and platform optimization activities. Ensure quality assurance, testing, documentation, and deployment readiness. Provide guidance and mentorship to ServiceNow developers, administrators, and analysts. Support governance, change management, and process improvement initiatives. Facilitate workshops, status meetings, and executive reporting. requirements: Role Overview The Change Management Intermediate Analyst plays a key role in service delivery operations, supporting and executing ITIL/ITSM Change Management processes. This role is responsible for ensuring that all changes to IT services and infrastructure are properly assessed, approved, implemented, and reviewed in a controlled manner to minimize risk and business impact. requirements: Key Responsibilities Interconnect Solution Development Develop and support interconnect solutions for AI servers, GPU, rack-level, and cluster-level platforms. Define cable and connector requirements based on system architecture, SI requirements, power delivery, thermal, and mechanical (and manufacturability) constraints. Support advanced interconnect applications, including: High-speed copper and optical interconnects Patch panels Optical cable assemblies Cable cartridge solutions Inter-rack DC link high-power connectivity Collaborate with SL, EE, SI, ME, Thermal, and System teams (and Manufacturing) to ensure robust (and scalable) interconnect architecture integration. High-Speed Interconnect Technology Support high-speed interconnection technologies, including (400G / 800G) interconnect solutions and future high-bandwidth AI infrastructure connectivity solutions. Evaluate and drive implementation readiness for emerging technologies such as PCIe Gen6/Gen7, UALink,NVLink, high-speed optical interconnects, and next-generation GPU cluster connectivity solutions. Drive technology readiness assessment and solution feasibility studies according to industry technology trends. Develop strategic supplier technology roadmaps aligned with future AI infrastructure interconnect technologies and deployment requirements. NPI & Engineering Support Lead and drive interconnect implementation activities throughout all NPI phases from concept development through EVT/DVT/PVT and sustaining. Own cable and connector component’s part list management, and interconnect technical documentation. Conduct NUDD analysis, engineering trade-off studies, risk analysis, and design reviews for Interconnect solutions. Lead sample verification, prototype bring-up, debugging, issue resolution and second source introduction during development phases. Supplier Development & Component Qualification Lead and develop strategic suppliers include technical engagement, capability assessment, and manufacturing readiness reviews. Define and execute qualification plans and reliability requirements for cable and connector components. Assess technical impact of PCN/PCR changes and define mitigation actions. Maintain interconnect qualification records, databases, and lifecycle documentation. Drive cost optimization, AVL strategy, and supply continuity planning. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Materials Science, or related field. 5+ years of experience in server, networking, storage, HPC, AI infrastructure, or high-speed interconnect engineering Strong knowledge of: High-speed cable and connector architectures Signal and power interconnect systems Qualification and reliability validation methods Rack-level and cluster-level interconnect solutions Experience working with ODM/OEM, EMS, and component suppliers. Strong cross-functional communication, technical problem-solving, and project execution skills. Preferred Qualifications Experience supporting AI server, GPU server, HPC, networking, or data center platforms. Familiarity with high-speed interconnect technologies (400G/800G, optical interconnect, UALink, NVLink, PCIe Gen6/Gen7, and next-generation AI fabric connectivity). Experience in NPI lifecycle management and supplier qualification processes. Understanding of signal integrity, power delivery, and thermal considerations in AI infrastructure systems. Experience supporting and bring-up supplier’s manufacturing and development sites is a plus. Additional

About the Position Lenovo is seeking a Marketing Director to lead marketing strategy for the International Sales Organization (ISO). This role will define and drive the end-to-end marketing strategy for Lenovo’s cross-business group solutions, enabling sales growth and advancing Lenovo’s transformation to a solutions-led company. As a strategic leader, you will own the holistic marketing plan across strategy, messaging, demand generation, sales enablement, and lead management, ensuring alignment between Lenovo’s global portfolio strategy and ISO sales priorities. This position is remote with a 10% traveling expectation. We're Looking for a Leader Who Can Build credibility quickly and influence senior stakeholders across multiple organizations. Create momentum and bring people along through organizational change. Develop One Lenovo messaging, sales enablement frameworks, and marketing operating models that align cross-functional teams. Balance strategic leadership with a player-coach mindset, rolling up their sleeves to develop key deliverables when needed. Thrive in a lean environment by driving execution through collaboration and influence rather than direct authority. Key Responsibilities Marketing Strategy & Transformation Define and lead the global marketing strategy for ISO, aligning messaging, demand generation, and sales enablement to support Lenovo’s cross-business group solutions and growth priorities. Drive marketing initiatives that support Lenovo’s ongoing transformation toward solutions-led selling and integrated portfolio value. Customer Journey & Demand Generation Develop strategies that influence the full customer buying journey, using insights and analytics to identify propensity to buy and optimize demand generation programs. Establish performance frameworks that connect marketing investments to pipeline creation, conversion, and revenue impact. Sales Enablement & Lead Lifecycle Lead development of solutions sales enablement that equips sellers to articulate Lenovo’s differentiated value and accelerate customer engagement. Own the lead management strategy and process, ensuring alignment between marketing and sales to improve lead quality, pipeline visibility, and conversion. Global Collaboration & Leadership Partner with business groups, geo marketing leaders, and ISO sales leadership to deliver integrated marketing programs at global scale. Lead through influence in a highly matrixed environment, mobilizing cross-functional teams and shared resources to deliver business outcomes. Identify gaps and advocate for incremental investment, building business cases to scale successful programs and capabilities. Build strong cross-organizational relationships to drive alignment, execution, and measurable business impact. Lenovo is a US$69 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Business Area Description: The Lenovo Service Desk serves as a Single Point of Contact (SPOC) and delivers comprehensive IT support to Customer’s end users. Integrated with our customer’s IT functions and processes, Lenovo Service Desk performs end to end Incident and Request management on behalf of our customers. Key Objectives of Position: The Training Management Senior Analyst is a key role within delivery operations responsible for identifying and assessing training needs, creating tailored training plans, and implementing various training methods to enhance Agents' skills and performance. They monitor training program effectiveness and stay updated on training trends. Knowledge Champion Agent Service Readiness Improve Performance (i.e., First Call Resolution) Identify potential areas for continuous improvement. Collaborate with Quality Control teams on increasing performance and productivity Day-To-Day Tasks: The trainer role is to provide technical or soft skills training to new and seasoned service desk analysts to improve performance and increase first call resolution. The responsibility includes creating educational material, conducting training sessions, identifying skills gaps, and ensuring team members stay motivated. The Technical Trainers are expected to be the knowledge champions of the organization. The goal is to make sure that the services delivered are within the standard level agreements. Perform or supervise the performance of DWS training activities. Coordinate, develop, and participate in an ongoing training. Performs activity that is intended to increase the knowledge, skills, and abilities of Agents and Technicians Provide necessary training, coaching, and mentoring to Agents and Technicians. Plan, develop, conduct, and implement training activities. Create, Revised training plans, modules, and scorecard. Develop training objectives, select training methods, and evaluate training results. Identify areas for improving individual and team performance. Identify skills gaps and fill them. Develop presentations, training materials, and handout materials. Provide training guidance and feedback to Agents, Technicians and Leaders Key Competencies Needed: Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical information into user-friendly language. Continuous learning mindset to adapt training strategies to evolving technologies and business needs. Intermediate knowledge of hardware, software, and enterprise IT environments. Attention to detail and strong organizational skills. Ability to build strong relationships and influence multiple technical and non-technical teams. Candidate Pre-Requisites Previous Experience: 3-5 years previous experience in either an internal or outsourced DWS Delivery. Continuous learning mindset to adapt training strategies to evolving technologies and business needs. requirements: 【岗位职责】 1、负责 MCU 产品软件架构的整体规划、设计、落地与版本迭代； 2、负责MCU芯片平台、RTOS系统、通信协议、存储方案、升级方案、低功耗方案的选型与论证； 3、负责解决MCU产品研发、测试、量产阶段的各类核心疑难问题，包括系统性死机、随机复位、高低温异常、EMC兼容问题、功耗超标、通信丢包、内存溢出、系统调度异常等技术难点； 4、负责产品架构级性能与功耗优化，统筹系统调度优化、任务优先级设计、内存管理优化、IO吞吐优化，解决产品卡顿、响应延迟等问题； 【任职要求】 1、本科及以上学历，电子信息、自动化、嵌入式、计算机相关专业，5年以上 MCU 嵌入式开发经验及1年以上架构设计经验； 2、熟练使用主流单片机平台，如STM32、GD32、ESP32、NXP、TI 等，熟悉标准库、HAL库、LL库开发； 3、熟悉 I2C、UART、SPI等通信协议； 4、熟悉单片机、ARM及其外围电路驱动设计； 5、熟悉蓝牙BLE、WiFi等无线通信开发，加分项：精通MCU低功耗架构、固件OTA升级架构设计； 6、良好的英文读写能力，加分项：口语交流能力 Additional

Role Summary The Manager, Engineering Delivery & Platform Operations is responsible for leading the engineering teams that develop, deploy, operate , and scale Lenovo's Sports & Entertainment technology portfolio. Working closely with Sports Strategists , Principal Engineer, Product Leaders, and cross-functional stakeholders, this role translates strategic objectives and solution architectures into executable engineering plans while ensuring successful delivery across all products and initiatives. The successful candidate will provide both technical leadership and people management, overseeing engineering execution, platform operations, release management, resource planning, and engineering governance across the Sports & Entertainment portfolio. Key Responsibilities Engineering Leadership & Team Management Lead and develop multidisciplinary engineering teams including backend, platform, AI/ML, cloud, and integration engineers. Establish engineering standards, development methodologies, and delivery processes. Manage team performance, career development, hiring, and workforce planning. Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, and operational excellence. Engineering Delivery Own engineering execution across the Sports & Entertainment portfolio. Translate business priorities and architectural designs into engineering roadmaps and delivery plans. Coordinate engineering activities across multiple concurrent initiatives. Manage risks, dependencies, resource allocation, and delivery timelines. Platform Operations Oversee deployment, operation, monitoring, and lifecycle management of all platforms and environments. Drive reliability, scalability, and performance improvements. Establish operational readiness, support models, and incident management practices. Ensure solutions are production-ready and capable of supporting commercial deployment. Cross-Functional Leadership Partner closely with the strategists/SAs to support innovation initiatives, customer engagements, and commercialization efforts. Collaborate with the Principal Engineer on technical planning, architecture implementation, and engineering governance. Coordinate with product, data science, AI, infrastructure, and external partner teams. Support customer workshops, executive briefings, and technical reviews as required . Required Experience 8+ years of software engineering, platform engineering, cloud engineering, or technical delivery experience. 3+ years managing engineering teams. Experience leading enterprise-scale software and platform development initiatives. Experience delivering cloud-native and AI-enabled solutions. Experience managing multiple products, programs, or engineering workstreams simultaneously. Experience working directly with executive stakeholders and customers. What Lenovo Can Offer You Compensation & Rewards Performance-based salary reviews and annual bonuses Meal tickets, service anniversary awards, and referral bonuses Employee share purchase program Scholarships for employees’ children Health & Well-being Fully covered private medical insurance Annual well-being allowance for sports, training, relaxation, and therapy, and on-site chair massage Life & accident insurance with extra medical coverage Employee Assistance Program Training & Development Technical Ladder & Sales Excellence programs Individual Career Development Plans with mentoring Bookster subscription for continuous learning Time Away 21–28 vacation days depending on tenure Sick days and extra paid leave for public holidays on weekends Additional paid leave for employees with children We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: This role is part of Lenovo’s Services organization and is responsible for managing field service delivery operations in the East India region. The Service Delivery Manager will lead operational performance with Authorized Service Partners (ASPs), drive customer experience improvements, and support Lenovo’s post-sales services strategy across the Personal Computing and Smart Devices (PCSD) segment. The position requires strong coordination with sales teams, partners, and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure alignment with organizational objectives and to foster continuous improvement in service delivery standards. Key Responsibilities: Drive daily operations of field service delivery through close collaboration with ASPs in the assigned region. Ensure adherence to key performance indicators including: End-to-End Cycle Time (ECT) for open work orders Customer Experience (CX) metrics, especially Top 3 Box (T3B) scores Repeat repair rates and service quality Spare parts consumption, return rate (RMA), and inventory hygiene Compliance with Customer Care Index (CCI) standards at service centers Promotion and achievement of After Point of Sales (APOS) revenue through billable services and upsell offerings Execution of strategic ASP programs and initiatives Engage regularly with Consumer and SMB sales teams and channel partners to support Post-Sales Audit Tracking (PSAT) survey targets and ensure continuous improvements. Align tactical and strategic service initiatives with overall business objectives for the region. Qualifications & Experience: Graduate in any discipline; technical background preferred. Minimum 10 years of relevant experience in the product services or customer support industry. Proven ability to manage field service operations, including third-party partners. Strong customer-centric mindset and high ownership to deliver superior customer experience (CX). Capable of working in a dynamic, cross-functional environment and managing multiple priorities. Key Behavioral Competencies: Positive Attitude: Maintains a constructive and can-do mindset in all situations. Execution Speed: Ability to drive timely outcomes and manage urgent service escalations effectively. Team Collaboration: Works well within and across teams; encourages collective success. Risk-Taking Ability: Displays initiative, embraces challenges, and is comfortable with ambiguity. Additional
responsibilities: Drive daily operations of field service delivery through close collaboration with ASPs in the assigned region. Align tactical and strategic service initiatives with overall business objectives for the region.

requirements: 岗位职责/Job Responsibilities： 1. 软件需求范围 (Scope) 定义与管控： • 主导Moto手机产品软件需求的端到端定义。与产品管理团队、业务部门深度沟通，在项目早期锁定软件需求边界。 • 负责需求的可行性评估与优先级排序，产出高质量的技术设计规范 (Design Spec)，确保软件 Scope与项目商业目标高度对齐，严控需求蔓延。 2. 软件技术方案规划与评审： • 针对项目复杂特性，主导全栈软件技术方案的制定。组织各领域专家 (SME) 进行深度技术评审。 • 确定核心模块的技术架构与分工方案，解决方案执行中的高复杂度难题，为研发团队提供确定的、具备前瞻性的技术底座。 3. 软件核心卖点 (KSP) 与差异化特性规划： • 深度洞察市场竞品趋势，识别并定义具有竞争力的软件 KSP 以及 NUDD (New/Unique/Different/Difficult) 特性。 • 制定 KSP 的核心技术 KPI 指标及 Benchmark 策略，确保规划出的卖点不仅具有市场吸引力，且具备极高的技术完成度。 4. 跨团队协调与决策推动： • 作为软件技术规划的Owner，协调研发、设计、测试及外部供应商，平衡理想化方案与工程实践，推动关键技术决策的快速落地。 岗位要求/Job Requirements： 1. 具备10年以上 手机/智能硬件软件研发相关经验 。 2. 有成功带过大型复杂项目经验者优先。 3. 精通 Android 架构及系统原理，对移动终端软硬件架构有深刻理解。 4. 具备优秀的文字输出能力，能够产出逻辑严密的技术规划文档及商业建议书。 5. 良好的英语听说读写能力（CET-6 或同等水平），能够与全球团队协作沟通。 技能与素质要求 (Skill Requirements) 1. 具备优秀的结构化思维，能从杂乱、模糊的原始需求中迅速理清脉络，并在压力环境下做出清醒、正确的判断。 2. 强烈的自我驱动力和使命必达的进取心，对攻克技术难点和打造卓越产品充满热忱。 3. 极强的责任心， 对规划的项目结果负全责，不推诿，能主动识别并化解规划落地过程中的各类风险隐患。 4. 卓越的推动力与影响力， 善于协调资源，通过专业性和个人影响力推动跨职能团队达成共识，确保技术规划目标按时按质达成。 Additional

requirements: The North America RTR Accounting Process Specialist will be responsible for strengthening financial reporting governance and providing accounting operations support across North America (Canada and U.S.). This role will review deliverables prepared by the outsourced shared services team in collaboration with the RTR team, ensuring reporting quality, identifying risks, and driving improvements in governance and control. In addition, the role will support monthly close activities as needed, including review of manual journal entries and validation of SAP postings, partners with finance teams and ensure compliance with corporate accounting policies, IFRS/US GAAP, and local statutory requirements. Key Responsibilities: Financial Reporting Governance & Oversight Strengthen governance across all aspects of financial reporting in North America Review deliverables from the outsourced shared services team with robust governance frameworks, in collaboration with the RTR team, to ensure compliance, accuracy, and quality Identify reporting risks from reconciliations, entry reviews, and fluctuation analysis and drive remediation actions Conduct quarterly financial review sessions with business and finance leaders to reinforce accountability Accounting Operations Support: Support monthly close activities as needed, ensuring timely and accurate reporting Review and approve manual journal entries prepared by the outsourced shared services team and post entries when required Validate booking details in SAP to confirm transactions are properly recorded Partner with RTR teams to resolve late entries, exceptions, and other closing issues Policy & Compliance: Ensure alignment with IFRS/US GAAP, corporate accounting policies, and local statutory requirements Provide accounting guidance, with support from the Accounting Policy Guidance team, on complex transactions and operations Maintain oversight of internal controls to minimize compliance gaps Risk Management: Review reconciliations, entries, and analysis prepared by the outsourced shared services team to assess reporting risks Monitor recurring issues and track remediation progress Escalate significant control or compliance concerns to senior stakeholders Audit Support: Partner with RTR and outsourced shared services teams to ensure deliverables are audit-ready Coordinate with external auditors to provide timely responses and address findings Support internal audit reviews related to accounting and reporting governance Analytics & Insight: Review financial fluctuations and variances, providing oversight commentary and identifying risks Support decision-making by highlighting accounting impacts and control considerations Continuous Improvement & Projects: Lead initiatives to improve the quality, efficiency, and governance of financial reporting Provide accounting support on new business models, process enhancements, and system implementations Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance; CPA/CA or equivalent certification preferred 7+ years of progressive accounting or finance experience, ideally within a multinational corporation (MNC) Strong knowledge of IFRS/US GAAP and accounting/reporting standards Hands-on experience with SAP ECC/S4, including ability to check booking details, review/post manual entries, and support monthly close Experience reviewing reconciliations, journal entries, and reporting analysis for risks and compliance Exposure to outsourced shared services teams or shared service centers preferred Experience in people management, both locally and remotely, with ability to work effectively in a multinational, multicultural, and BPO environments Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills Excellent communication skills with ability to partner effectively with RTR, business, finance, and audit stakeholders Demonstrated ability to balance governance oversight with hands-on accounting operations support Basic Requirements: Bachelor's degree 7+ years of accounting/finance experience #LI-MM5 Additional

requirements: The Lenovo Design Innovation Team is creating a new generation of AI Ambient devices- products that move beyond traditional screens and interact through presence, context, intelligence, and behavior. And we are seeking an operation specialist to work closely with the operation team and management team to ensure impeccable finance and procurement operations that directly supports the core business. Job Responsibilities ： WW & NA Procurement Operations (40%) Support DIT North America (NA) procurement activities for DIT, cooperating closely with the NA GP team to continuously improve the DIT NA procurement process. Execute end-to-end NA procurement payment processes via Ariba and IO, including purchase requests, approvals, goods receipt, and payment follow-up, ensuring compliance with Lenovo policies. Communicate and collaborate with Worldwide suppliers on business coordination, order execution, issue resolution, and ongoing operational alignment. Execute procurement-related audit requirements , including documentation preparation, data validation, and timely response to internal and external audit requests. Financial Operations & Budget Management (60%) Support DIT overall financial operations , including routine finance tasks and ad-hoc financial actions to ensure smooth day-to-day execution. Ensuring alignment with budget owner expectations and compliance requirements. Co-work closely with Finance (FA) and Operations teams on overall budget maintenance, including budget tracking, variance monitoring, and financial governance support. Own and manage DIT Worldwide CAPEX budget, including tracking, reporting, coordination with Finance and Procurement teams, and supporting approval and control processes. Contribute to continuous improvement of financial workflows , controls, and operational processes to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and transparency. Job Requirements ： Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Operations, or a related field. 1–3 years of experience in finance operations, procurement operations, shared services, or related roles. Hands-on experience with procurement or payment processes, including requisition, approval, and settlement flows. Practical working knowledge of Ariba, IO, or similar procurement and payment systems. Basic understanding of financial controls, audit requirements, and internal compliance standards. Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite and standard enterprise systems. Strong communication and stakeholder coordination skills, especially across Finance, Procurement, and Operations teams. Ability to work independently, demonstrate ownership, and handle multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Preferred Qualifications Experience supporting global or North America–based procurement and finance operations. Prior exposure to CAPEX/OPEX management, budget tracking, or audit support activities. Experience working in a multinational or matrix organization with cross-region stakeholders. Additional

requirements: Position Summary This role is a key contributor to the MDSC global supply planning process, data and system. The role delivers major planning data—including One Plan commitment, overseas EMS factory ship plan commitment, global CTB, stoplight consolidation, PDP with offset rules. The role also leads digital tool development (e.g., Smart Supply Planning, SP workspace) and integrates cross-functional tools into one platform as a One Team solution. Job Responsibilities 1. Digital Transformation & Intelligence - Lead and bridge DT/IT teams to identify automation, digitalization, and AI opportunities; empower daily operations with AI technology. - Develop and enable the Smart Supply Planning tool, contributing to upstream/downstream digital projects; extend to GSC level for platform synergy and resource leverage. - Create Power BI dashboards to visualize MDSC supply planning data, measure business results and performance, and predict risks/issues; centralize into a global data pool for worldwide visibility and traceability. 2. Process Optimization & Innovation - Enhance processes in a dynamic industrial environment; initiate new processes (e.g., risk buy process across NPI, BU, procurement, planning) and enhance existing ones (e.g., KD ship plan centralization, NPI PSS integration into SSP, digital huddle integration into planning). - Standardize planning timelines, logic, and templates across all overseas EMS factories. 3. Data-Driven Decision Making - Analyze business results and performance by managing master data and planning data; initiate improvements accordingly. - Ensure accurate system/BOM operation and resolve issues to sustain customer fulfillment timelines. - Monitor, track, and predict planning issues with effective cross-functional solutions. 4. Team Development & Training - Train new members on general planning instructions. Job Requirements 1. Functional/Technical - Supply chain management, planning logic, cross-functional knowledge (procurement & sourcing, NPI, quality, manufacturing, logistics), inventory management, planning systems, data analysis | 2.Business - Market/industry dynamics, financial acumen, risk management, sustainability 3. Managerial - Team leadership, project management, effective cross-organizational communication 4.Behavioral & Leadership Expectations 1). Business Insight – Identify opportunities and risks in dynamic markets; deploy new tools/systems for new business models or demand-supply networks. 2). Process Optimization – Redesign processes using automation or AI-driven analytics to improve efficiency and reduce costs. 3). Digitalization & Intelligence – Proactively integrate advanced technologies into daily operations; provide customized solutions for organizational needs. 4). Collaboration – Develop innovative collaboration models with suppliers, EMS partners, and cross-functions in new planning scenarios; lead or contribute as a key member of E2E digital projects. 5). Data-Driven Decision Making – Use AI, machine learning, and interactive dashboards to predict demand spikes, identify hidden patterns, and deliver actionable insights. 6.Advanced Challenges & Expected Outcomes 1). AI Implementation – Define practical AI opportunities in daily E2E planning operations; lead solutions with DT/IT teams for intelligent, dynamic, and fuzzy planning; maintain industry-leading operational levels. 2）. Advanced Process Design – Design and establish E2E processes supporting diverse business models, leveraging Lenovo’s internal best practices (e.g., PCG) and industry benchmarks to keep performance competitive. 3）. New Business Model Integration – As business expands, bring new business models into E2E planning with necessary enhancements to existing processes, systems, and tools, working cross-functionally. 4）. Data Value Discovery – Uncover true value from decades of MDSC planning data; provide deep insights behind business performance to support success in highly competitive markets. 5）. Timeline & Efficiency Management – Follow strict weekly timelines, drive and influence multiple functions, set key monitors and pre-alerts, react quickly to exceptions; continuously improve efficiency to match business complexity and growth. Additional
responsibilities: 1. Digital Transformation & Intelligence - Lead and bridge DT/IT teams to identify automation, digitalization, and AI opportunities; empower daily operations with AI technology. - Develop and enable the Smart Supply Planning tool, contributing to upstream/downstream digital projects; extend to GSC level for platform synergy and resource leverage. - Create Power BI dashboards to visualize MDSC supply planning data, measure business results and performance, and predict risks/issues; centralize into a global data pool for worldwide visibility and traceability. 2. Process Optimization & Innovation - Enhance processes in a dynamic industrial environment; initiate new processes (e.g., risk buy process across NPI, BU, procurement, planning) and enhance existing ones (e.g., KD ship plan centralization, NPI PSS integration into SSP, digital huddle integration into planning). - Standardize planning timelines, logic, and templates across all overseas EMS factories. 3. Data-Driven Decision Making - Analyze business results and performance by managing master data and planning data; initiate improvements accordingly. - Ensure accurate system/BOM operation and resolve issues to sustain customer fulfillment timelines. - Monitor, track, and predict planning issues with effective cross-functional solutions. 4. Team Development & Training - Train new members on general planning instructions. Job

requirements: Job Responsibilities Supplier Qualification & Audit Lead new supplier assessment, audit, and qualification for mobile battery suppliers, evaluate quality system, technical supplier assessment, reliability test, and safety compliance. Conduct periodic on-site audits (process, quality system, safety) and drive corrective actions to closure. Quality Assurance & Control Define and maintain incoming quality standards, inspection criteria, and reliability test plans for batteries. Monitor PYSR, LAR, VLRR, TCRR and ORT results, ensure compliance with spec, safety standards, and customer requirements. Collaborate with suppliers to stabilize production processes, implement SPC, FMEA, and control plans. Quality Issue Resolution Lead root cause analysis and CAPA for incoming defects, line rejects, and field failures using 8D, 5Why, DDDW, and statistical tools. Track and verify corrective actions to prevent recurrence; manage quality claims and supplier improvement plans. Supplier Performance Evaluation Management Establish and track supplier KPI system, incoming quality rate, on-line defect rate, CAPA closure rate. Conduct regular quality reviews, drive continuous improvement and cost reduction in supply chain quality. NPI & Engineering Support Work on new part risk analysis, monitor pilot run, and mass production readiness for battery components. Work with R&D, test, and sourcing teams to validate design, process, and quality risks. Job Requirements Bachelor’s Degree in engineering, automation, science or related technical field and years of relevant work experience. 3+ year experience in a battery manufacturing or development role that has delivered various products for mobile phone / tablet industry. Knowledge of quality methodology and tools, supplier process audit, process control plan, PFMEA, MSA, SPC, PPAP, 8D and/or similar tools. Experiences with cell/pack manufacturing process and testing automation in the manufacturing factory, familiar with failure analysis process steps is a plus. Excellent communication, collaboration, reporting, analytical and problem solving skills. Fluent in written and spoken English; able to communicate and coordinate with global team. Preferred qualifications Ability to triage issues, react well to changes, work with teams and ability to multi-task on multiple products and projects. Experience working closely with development and business teams to understand business requirements and communicate quality problem impacts proactively. Knowledge of battery PCM IC and MOSFET protective circuit architecture. Shows creativity and initiative to improve product test coverage and effectiveness. Familiarity with mobile phone assembly processes. Additional
