requirements: About Lenovo Lenovo is a global technology leader in AI infrastructure, high-performance computing (HPC), and data center solutions. Through its AI Cloud and Data Center Services (AICDS) organization, Lenovo enables customers to design, deploy, and scale next-generation accelerated computing environments. Role Summary We are hiring a Technical Project Manager to join the Global AICDS team. This role leads the delivery of complex AI infrastructure and data center projects, while also supporting bid and pre-sales activities to ensure successful project execution. You will manage end-to-end deployments, validate delivery assumptions, and coordinate across global teams, customers, and partners to deliver projects on time, within scope, and within budget. Key Responsibilities Lead end-to-end delivery of AI infrastructure projects, including GPU clusters, HPC environments, and data center deployments Manage project scope, timelines, budgets, risks, and dependencies Ensure alignment with Statement of Work (SOW) and project deliverables Support bid and pre-sales activities by validating technical and delivery assumptions Collaborate with sales, solution architects, procurement, and delivery teams Coordinate multi-vendor and cross-functional teams across geographies Establish project governance including reporting, communication plans, and escalation management Track financial performance and drive resolution of project risks and issues Support transition of completed projects into operations or managed services Skills & Experience Must-have 5+ years of project management experience in technical or infrastructure environments Experience delivering AI infrastructure, HPC, GPU clusters, or data center projects Strong stakeholder management across customers and global teams Experience in bid support, proposal validation, or pre-sales project planning Knowledge of project governance, risk management, and delivery frameworks PMP / PRINCE2 or equivalent certification Strong communication skills (written and verbal English) Good-to-have Experience with NVIDIA ecosystems or GPU-based platforms Exposure to hyperscale or cloud service provider environments Knowledge of data center infrastructure (power, cooling, networking) Experience with Agile or hybrid delivery models Familiarity with cluster deployment, benchmarking, and validation Additional

requirements: Position Description Partner Account Manager - Volume This position involves defining and executing the “Run Rate” channel & distribution business in Delhi / NCR region. Volume channel business is critical to the success of Lenovo’s strategy to be the most trusted advisor for data center customers. Small and medium business customer segment, which this channel motion serves, is an exponentially growing segment. Scaling this growth in partnership with the channels has been identified as a key priority. This position offers an excellent opportunity to grow with an organization that thrives in owning opportunities and driving breakthrough growth. Key Responsibilities Work with Product & Marketing Managers to: To create annual and quarterly business plans that meet or beat revenue and profit objectives for the Autonomous channel business Define a market strategy and related programs to ensure proper market coverage Work with the regional team to create quarterly Promotions to drive sell-through & sell-out Drive demand generation activities in a timely manner Identify business issues before they occur and execute mitigation plans Ensure interaction and feedback from T2 Partners Compare with trends in the local marketplace for workloads and seasonal demand changes Provide feedback to product teams on local needs and demands Track and report business within the business management framework Ensure tight alignment with control and compliance processes Review and report business performance on a regular basis Skills and Knowledge • Ability to drive sales through channel partners and have 7+ years of relevant experience • Familiarity with driving sales through Volume/MOQ (stock & sell) motion • Able to translate strategic objectives into a business plan • Assists with defining training and development plans for volume channels • Expert understanding of partner landscape and climate • High level of competitive environment • Mature individual with strong influence skills to work across a matrixed organization • Personal leadership to stay focused on delivering to a sales plan while navigating a high-growth, dynamic environment • Strong presentation skills to be able to present current business status and future plans to management. • Bachelor's Degree Additional

Based in Essen, you will drive team performance, enhance technical capabilities, and continuously improve service quality and customer experience. You’ll manage a team handling complex technical issues across Lenovo’s client portfolio, while working closely with cross-functional stakeholders to meet KPIs, optimize operations, and represent the voice of the customer across the business. requirements: We are seeking a Principal Architect to lead the architecture of Lenovo's platform capabilities into SAP's HANA Enterprise Cloud (HEC 3.0) and Customer Data Center (CDC) program. This is a named-account lead architect role embedded with the SAP engagement, accountable for end-to-end design across compute, storage, network, disaster recovery, high availability, backup, and integration. You will drive Lenovo's CDC capability roadmap from Planned and Partially Enabled status to Generally Available, working jointly with SAP engineering on shared capabilities and independently on Lenovo-owned items. The role sits within Lenovo's Hybrid Cloud Solutions & AI Offering Engineering (OA&E) organization. Deliverables are reference architectures, validated configurations, joint engineering artifacts, and operational runbooks consumed by Lenovo Professional Services and Managed Services Key Responsibilities Architecture Design & Engineering • Compute Architecture: Design Lenovo’s compute architecture for SAP PE CDC (Cloud Delivery Center), including virtual machine and bare-metal deployment patterns, hardware model selection, precise sizing guidance, and validated configurations. • Storage Architecture: Define storage architecture across block and NFS tiers for both virtual and physical workloads; establish file system layouts, self-service provisioning patterns, and volume-to-bare-metal mappings in close partnership with SAP Storage Engineering. • Network Architecture: Define comprehensive network architecture capabilities, covering core datacenter networking topologies, Load Balancer as a Service (LBaaS), and network-layer micro-segmentation. • Data Protection & Backup: Lead the architectural design for enterprise backup and data protection using Cohesity and/or Veeam, ensuring seamless integration across NFS and object storage tiers. Resilience, High Availability & Disaster Recovery • Data Center Topology: Lead data center topology design for complex single-site and dual-site deployments, establishing site-to-site replication parameters and seamless operational handoffs. • Disaster Recovery (DR) Ownership: Own the end-to-end DR architecture for application environments, including NFS-based replication, cross-site DR testing, and strict RPO/RTO validation. • High-Availability (HA) Validation: Define high-availability architectures for virtual and physical database tiers to target and sustain 99.9% availability, backed by rigorous, lab-based validation. Joint Roadmap & Delivery Ownership • Strategic Roadmap Execution: Drive Lenovo’s capability status from Planned and Not Enabled to Generally Available (GA) across the joint strategic roadmap, collaborating directly with SAP engineering on all shared deliverables. • Technical Documentation & SOPs: Translate complex joint engineering decisions into production-ready reference architectures, validated configurations, comprehensive runbooks, and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). Required Experience • 10+ years of enterprise experience in infrastructure and platform architecture for hosted, managed, or multi-tenant cloud environments, with a proven track record of end-to-end delivery ownership. • Proven architectural leadership spanning core data center technologies across compute (virtualized and bare-metal environments), enterprise storage, and complex networking. • Advanced VMware Architecture: Deep technical expertise designing, scaling, and managing enterprise-grade VMware environments, including vSphere/ESXi , vCenter Server , and advanced software-defined data center (SDDC) components. • Advanced Disaster Recovery & High-Availability Design: Demonstrated expertise engineering resilient architectures utilizing synchronous/asynchronous replication, NFS replication, VMware high-availability configurations, and active-active stretched clusters (e.g., MetroCluster). Networking & Storage Expertise • Enterprise Networking: Hands-on experience designing and implementing network architectures utilizing Cisco or Arista switching, Palo Alto Networks firewalls, and modern overlay technologies like VXLAN. • Enterprise Storage: Deep technical expertise in storage architecture design, specifically focused on NetApp platforms and block/file storage protocols (NFS, SMB, iSCSI/FC). Core Professional Skills • Technical Documentation: Strong technical writing skills with a track record of producing clear, comprehensive architecture diagrams, design documents, and operational runbooks. What do we offer you? Compensation & Rewards · Performance-based salary reviews and annual bonuses · Meal tickets, service anniversary awards, and referral bonuses · Employee share purchase program · Scholarships for employees’ children Health & Well-being · Fully covered private medical insurance · Annual well-being allowance for sports, training, relaxation, and therapy and on-site chair massage · Life & accident insurance with extra medical coverage · Employee Assistance Program Training & Development · Technical Ladder & Sales Excellence programs · Individual Career Development Plans with mentoring · Bookster subscription for continuous learning Time Away · 21–28 vacation days depending on tenure · Sick days and extra paid leave for public holidays on weekends · Additional paid leave for employees with children We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: This is an exciting opportunity to join Lenovo as an intern and take on challenging real-life projects. We are looking for last year university students who are willing to take on an internship during their studies and would like to initiate an exciting career in a global company. As a Lenovo intern, you will join our Product Management Team in order to provide general support in a variety of tasks. The things you will do: Market analysis Price benchmark (PFV) Roadmap: products designation and materials, products pricing, forecasts Co-marketing: promotions and marketing materials Database management & Reports creation for management team The skills you bring to the table: Fluent Spanish (native or advanced) and fluent English. Comfortable with Office Suite/Google tools Excellent data analysis, Excel Proficiency in Power BI, C++ and/or Python (any previous experience, course ...) Problem solving skills & Critical thinking Ability to work on own and within a team Great communication skills, especially in client-facing situations Beyond the profile, the following is required: Eligible for an internship (still enrolled or have the possibility to obtain an internship agreement with a Spanish University) Ability to commute to our Madrid Office Availability for a min of 6 months to 1 year ideally A background in commercial education (min. 3 years) Curious mind and interest in AI What we offer: A multitude of professional and personal opportunities An open and stimulating environment within one of the most forwarding thinking IT companies Modern and flexible way of working to combine personal and professional life An international environment with a high focus on diversity Please send your CV in English. We are looking forward to discussing this position with you soon! We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

Responsibilities: Define and execute an Omni-Channel product vision for NA Consumer based on customer pain-points, consumer market intelligence, partner requirements, financial goals and route opportunity. Manage a Product level P&L including forecasting Units, Revenue and Profit and establishing List Pricing. Own go-to-market strategy, set and communicate expectations for execution across the Omni-channel. Work cross-functionally to create and implement a plan to sell Lenovo products with our Sales team, Account Planners, marketing, and other internal parties at Lenovo. Communicate and pitch our product plans and strategies to our Retail partners directly Focus on Customer Experience. Improve the consumer purchase experience by working with the BU, marketing, sales and retail partners. Help to redefine the internal process for forecasting and ordering to allow for on-time product launches as dictated by market dynamics. Work with Sales, S/D and Account Planning to map the product lifecycle to maximize sales and reduce liability. Requirements: 7+ years of product management and/or marketing experience, preferably in the Consumer IT industry MBA Encouraged Strong organizational and project management skills with the ability to think strategically but work tactically to get things done quickly. Ability to travel and meet directly with customers Strong financial literacy including P&L fluency Self-starter with strong work ethic. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to effectively influence and communicate with executive levels of management internally and across external partners. Knowledge of the retail requirements and needs for the North America market. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: Motorola Mobility, A Lenovo Company, is one of the world’s fastest growing smartphone providers, creating groundbreaking, affordable, high-quality products designed and built with the global customer in mind. And it’s our people who make this all happen. We are thinkers, risk-takers and problem solvers, working together to constantly challenge the status quo. If you share our commitment to ingenuity, creativity and innovation, we want you to help us define our world of tomorrow. Explore the opportunities and apply today. The Product Marketing organization plays a critical role in developing and evolving product stories from ideation through launch, communicating features in a compelling way and clearly articulating consumer
responsibilities: We are looking for a go-getter with a passion for consumer technology, premium smartphone experiences, and iconic product storytelling. As a Sr. Product Marketing Specialist for the razr franchise , you will play a pivotal role in shaping, evolving, and bringing to market one of Motorola’s most visible and strategically important product franchises. This role requires a strong consumer-first mindset, sharp business judgment, and the ability to translate product innovation into clear, differentiated, and compelling global marketing strategies. You will help define how razr shows up across audiences, markets, and touchpoints—ensuring the franchise story is consistent, relevant, and grounded in meaningful consumer

requirements: Job responsibilities ： A leadership role actively manages supporting functional groups, to ensure projects running success with a best-balanced delivery performance, in terms of customer value, cost, expense, and resources. The role will develop comprehensive plan from strategic view to deliver intelligent products and solutions to improve customer experience, initiate actions that align with the company’s goals and objectives. Conduct execution of lenovo AI PC strategic initiatives, lead process transformation, simplification, and optimization to adopt to AI PC development and landing. Define AI project scope, create master plans, manage timelines, allocate resources, and ensure that projects are delivered on time and within budget. Job Requirements： Proficient in AI project management methodologies and lifecycle management, and strong leadership and team management skills, able to guide cross teams. Familiar with software engineering principles, development process and best practices, including version control, testing, and debugging. Deep understanding of AI technologies, including machine learning, natural language processing, computer vision, and their applications in various domains. Software AI Project management experience is preferred. Familiar with software development lifecycle methodologies and best practices for building and releasing AI products. Strong analytical skills for evaluating project performance metrics, identifying trends, and making data-driven decisions. Additional certifications in project management and AI are desirable. Additional

requirements: Role Summary This role is responsible for ensuring service performance, governance, and compliance through robust reporting, audit, and control frameworks. The position plays a key role in driving: Risk mitigation Continuous improvement Operational excellence across end-to-end service delivery. Key Responsibilities Lead and execute quality assurance activities aligned with service standards, contractual requirements, and internal control frameworks Monitor and report service performance against SLAs, OLAs, KPIs, and quality metrics Design and maintain reporting dashboards and executive summaries to support performance governance and decision-making Conduct audits and control assessments across Service Desk and resolver teams Identify control gaps, compliance issues, and operational risks; drive corrective and preventive actions Analyze operational data, trends, and customer feedback to identify improvement opportunities Collaborate with Incident, Problem, Change, and Knowledge Management teams to embed quality controls Support Root Cause Analysis (RCA) processes and ensure improvement actions are tracked and implemented Maintain and update SOPs, control documentation, and audit evidence Prepare and present audit findings, compliance reports, and performance insights to stakeholders Act as a key point of contact for internal and external audits Promote a culture of accountability, quality, and compliance across teams Drive cross-functional alignment to strengthen governance and improve service delivery Key Skills & Competencies Strong written and verbal communication skills Solid understanding of ITIL/ITSM processes and service quality frameworks Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret data and identify trends Knowledge of Service Desk, End User Computing, and Digital Workplace environments Experience with continuous improvement methodologies (e.g., CSI, Lean, Six Sigma – desirable) Ability to influence cross-functional teams and drive change without direct authority Qualifications & Experience Experience 3–5 years of experience in IT service operations, quality management, or service delivery (internal or outsourced environments) Experience leading teams, squads, or cross-functional initiatives is highly desirable Education Diploma or Degree in an IT or business-related field Required Certification ITIL Foundation Preferred Certifications ISO 20000 Auditor Six Sigma Green Belt Product Management / Agile Methodology Data Analytics Customer Experience Additional
responsibilities: Lead and execute quality assurance activities aligned with service standards, contractual

requirements: 岗位职责： 1. 负责公司手环、智能眼镜等AI可穿戴产品线的全局技术规划，制定下一代RTOS平台方案。 2. 建立“快、稳、省”的量化评估标准与自动化监控体系，保障产品核心体验保持行业领先。 3. 主导ISP与端侧AI算力的协同架构设计，优化多传感器融合算法在RTOS端的运行效率。 4. 决策文件系统、网络协议栈、内存分配器等底层关键模块的技术路线，提升系统稳定性与抗风险能力。 5. 与BES、NXP等芯片厂商开展深度技术对标，协同进行芯片需求定制与联合优化。 岗位要求： 1. 在智能穿戴或嵌入式系统领域具备深厚的技术积累与行业影响力。 2. 精通RTOS架构设计，可针对手环、智能眼镜等不同产品形态灵活制定系统策略。 3. 熟练掌握多核处理器架构下的任务同步与内存共享机制，对低功耗设计有严苛要求。 4. 具备出色的架构前瞻性，能够精准预判技术趋势并完成前瞻性技术储备。 5. 拥有大型项目或复杂嵌入式系统全生命周期的设计与管理经验。 Additional
responsibilities: 1. 负责公司手环、智能眼镜等AI可穿戴产品线的全局技术规划，制定下一代RTOS平台方案。 2. 建立“快、稳、省”的量化评估标准与自动化监控体系，保障产品核心体验保持行业领先。 3. 主导ISP与端侧AI算力的协同架构设计，优化多传感器融合算法在RTOS端的运行效率。 4. 决策文件系统、网络协议栈、内存分配器等底层关键模块的技术路线，提升系统稳定性与抗风险能力。 5. 与BES、NXP等芯片厂商开展深度技术对标，协同进行芯片需求定制与联合优化。

requirements: Who Are We? We are a blend of innovators and entrepreneurs who relentlessly pursue new ideas and are committed to driving change. As a team, we nurture a culture of doing and performing by operating with a high level of integrity and trust. Why Lenovo? Lenovo is a US $50 billion Fortune Global 500 company, with 57,000 employees and operating in 180 markets around the world. We are #1 PC company on the planet, BCG's 50 most innovative companies, and one of Interbrand’s 100 BEST global brands. Focused on a bold vision to deliver smarter technology for all, we are developing world-changing technologies that create a more inclusive, trustworthy and sustainable digital society. By designing, engineering and building the world’s most complete portfolio of smart devices and infrastructure, we are also leading an Intelligent Transformation - to create better experiences and opportunities for millions of customers around the world. What You'll Do: This position will be responsible for leading a large enterprise team to develop and execute on an aggressive customer strategy for Lenovo's corporate accounts in the US. The ideal candidate will be a leader with a proven track record of driving technology sales across the entire stack of Lenovo’s solutions portfolio; mobility, infrastructure and professional services. S/he will be results oriented, and driven by excellence. S/he will have Entrepreneurship to build an organization and business formation for the go-to-market strategy. S/he will also work well in a matrix environment with peers in sales, product development, marketing, services and supply chain. This leader should have command and be able to influence at all levels throughout the organization, externally with customers and potential partners by virtue of his/her industry expertise. This leader will have the capacity to guide and develop a comprehensive understanding of the industry and distill this understanding into simple, straightforward solutions that make a clear difference to the customer’s business. The position will be home based in the Northeast area with ~50% travel. Position Responsibilities: Develop existing pipeline and new business opportunities in North America Engage senior decision makers (including C level executives), customer influencers, end-users and ecosystem partners to advance sales pursuits Review and analyze market opportunities and evaluate competitive and technology requirements needed to capitalize on opportunities. Understand current and future industry needs in the context of market dynamics to deliver multiyear business deals Manage relationships and communications with external customers, internal stakeholders and partners. Lead organization through the evolution from selling product to selling solutions. Shape and execute NA business development and sales strategy across senior level direct and matrixed cross-functional teams Align sales objectives to the corporate strategy and financial performance Identify relevant new business opportunities, ecosystems, partnerships and markets, gathering of market intelligence and design and drive "go-to-market" plans Uncover and qualify opportunities that will accelerate and drive growth. Basic Requirements : Exceptional leader and coach with a focus on strong sales operations skills and the ability to form and develop high level relationships with customers. 8+ years of industry experience in sales leadership or equivalent role Preferred Requirements: Strong track record and demonstrated expertise in developing and maintaining strong industry, professional relationships; prefer ‘C’ level engagement This position requires exemplary verbal and written communication skills; strong presentation skills preferred and ability to articulate value proposition internally, to the market and to customers Ability to hire, train/coach and develop sales teams Stakeholder management ability Relevant technical and industry acumen Ability to work independently and deliver measurable results Travel required Data center experience preferred “In CA, CO, CT, DC, HI IL, MD, MA, MN, NV, NJ, NY, OR, RI, WA the base salary range budgeted for this position is $175,000 – $200,000 (annually). Individuals may also be considered for bonuses 60/40 Sales Plan and/or commissions. Lenovo’s various

other operational metrics Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and data models to support operational and executive decision-making Implement data quality controls and governance practices requirements: Detailed Technical Responsibilities · Administer SAP HANA tenant databases, HANA services, backup/recovery, HSR monitoring, trace analysis and DB housekeeping. · Analyze expensive statements, memory usage, blocked transactions, savepoints, log backup failures and service instability. · Support system refresh, database copy, DB migration readiness, HANA client updates and DR validation activities. · Execute approved DB parameter changes, backup schedule changes and controlled restarts under change governance. · Prepare RCA inputs for DB incidents and recurring performance or availability issues. Day-to-Day Technical Skills · HANA SQL, HANA Cockpit performance views, HANA traces, backup catalog, HSR state checks and tenant DB operations. · Working knowledge of SLES/RHEL for SAP, filesystem utilization, storage latency, multipath and OS-level dependencies. · Understanding of HANA memory model, delta merge, log shipping, CPU waits and I/O performance indicators. · Ability to correlate DB issues with SAP Basis, OS, storage, cloud and network layers. · Scripting basics for evidence collection, housekeeping reports and monitoring automation. SAP RISE AMS Delivery Expectations · Mandatory working exposure to SAP RISE or SAP AMS managed services, including DEV, QAS, PRD and DR landscape support. · Work within SAP, customer, hyperscaler and AMS shared-responsibility boundaries with clear ownership and escalation discipline. · Support Incident, Service Request, Change, Problem, RCA, Knowledge and CDD activities using ITSM workflows. · Participate in 24x7 support, shift handover, controlled maintenance windows, CAB approvals and audit-ready documentation. · Coordinate across SAP Basis, HANA/DB, integration, infrastructure, security, network, customer business teams and SAP support. Upgrade / Patch / Deployment / Configuration Exposure · Support HANA revision upgrades, HANA client updates, HANA plugin/agent updates and post-revision performance checks. · Perform DB pre-checks, backup validation, HSR sync confirmation and rollback readiness review before changes. · Support SLES/RHEL OS patching and minor version upgrade validation for HANA workloads. · Validate post-patch backup, replication, DB connectivity, critical jobs and application smoke tests. · Participate in DR drills, failover/failback validation and post-DR DB consistency checks. Monitoring, Management and ITSM Responsibilities · Use ServiceNow or equivalent ITSM to maintain ticket categorization, priority, affected service/CI, business impact, evidence and closure notes. · Support monitoring dashboards, health reports, SLA/OLA tracking, ticket ageing, backlog control and recurring alert review. · Follow escalation matrix, major incident bridge protocol, change governance and problem management process. · Maintain SOPs, runbooks, KB articles, handover notes and known-error database for repeated issues. · Identify automation and shift-left opportunities for repeated operational tasks and standard requests. Tools / Platforms · HANA Cockpit, HANA Studio, SAP for Me, SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Focused Run, Linux CLI, SAP Notes, ServiceNow, cloud monitoring tools. Expected Deliverables and KPIs · DB availability, backup success, HSR health, DB incident MTTR, successful change execution, RCA quality and recurring issue reduction. Preferred Certification / Evaluation Focus · SAP HANA Technology Associate preferred. Interview focus: memory spike analysis, HSR lag, backup failure, HANA revision upgrade and SLES/RHEL patch validation. Additional

requirements: Detailed Technical Responsibilities · Administer SAP systems, profiles, transports, background jobs, RFCs, ICM/gateway, spool, dumps, locks and application services. · Resolve L2 Basis incidents involving job failures, dumps, RFC failures, transport errors, performance degradation and SAP service restarts. · Troubleshoot IDoc, qRFC/tRFC, SOAP/REST/API, CPI iFlow, PI/PO message and certificate/endpoint issues. · Execute approved changes such as transport imports, profile updates, job schedule changes, interface reprocessing and controlled restarts. · Prepare change plans, rollback steps, pre/post checks and RCA inputs. Day-to-Day Technical Skills · Strong SAP Basis transactions: SM51, SM50/66, SM37, ST22, SM21, ST02, ST03N, STMS, RZ10/RZ11, SM59, ICM, SPAM/SAINT and SUM basics. · Integration operations using WE02/WE05/BD87, SM58, SMQ1/SMQ2, SRT_MONI, CPI message monitoring and PI/PO monitoring. · Understanding of SAP kernel directories, profiles, transport directory, RFC trust, gateway/ICM and certificates. · Ability to correlate SAP issues with OS, DB, network, firewall, DNS and proxy layers. · Shell scripting basics for automation, evidence capture and operational reporting. SAP RISE AMS Delivery Expectations · Mandatory working exposure to SAP RISE or SAP AMS managed services, including DEV, QAS, PRD and DR landscape support. · Work within SAP, customer, hyperscaler and AMS shared-responsibility boundaries with clear ownership and escalation discipline. · Support Incident, Service Request, Change, Problem, RCA, Knowledge and CDD activities using ITSM workflows. · Participate in 24x7 support, shift handover, controlled maintenance windows, CAB approvals and audit-ready documentation. · Coordinate across SAP Basis, HANA/DB, integration, infrastructure, security, network, customer business teams and SAP support. Upgrade / Patch / Deployment / Configuration Exposure · Execute/support SAP kernel upgrades, SAP Host Agent updates, support package activities and SUM-based maintenance. · Support SLES/RHEL patching and OS upgrade validation for SAP workloads with Basis pre/post checks. · Support CPI/PI/iFlow deployments, certificate rotation, endpoint migration and post-change interface smoke testing. · Support system refresh, client copy and post-copy automation coordination. · Validate system availability, jobs, RFCs, interfaces, transports and performance after maintenance. Monitoring, Management and ITSM Responsibilities · Use ServiceNow or equivalent ITSM to maintain ticket categorization, priority, affected service/CI, business impact, evidence and closure notes. · Support monitoring dashboards, health reports, SLA/OLA tracking, ticket ageing, backlog control and recurring alert review. · Follow escalation matrix, major incident bridge protocol, change governance and problem management process. · Maintain SOPs, runbooks, KB articles, handover notes and known-error database for repeated issues. · Identify automation and shift-left opportunities for repeated operational tasks and standard requests. Tools / Platforms · SAP GUI, SUM, Maintenance Planner, SAP for Me, SAP Notes, SAP Cloud ALM, SolMan, Focused Run, SAP Integration Suite, PI/PO, ServiceNow. Expected Deliverables and KPIs · Basis incident MTTR, change success rate, transport reliability, job stability, interface availability and post-maintenance defect rate. Preferred Certification / Evaluation Focus · SAP Basis certification preferred. Interview focus: kernel upgrade, SUM, transport failure, RFC issue, IDoc/CPI failure and OS patch validation. Additional
responsibilities: · Administer SAP systems, profiles, transports, background jobs, RFCs, ICM/gateway, spool, dumps, locks and application services. · Resolve L2 Basis incidents involving job failures, dumps, RFC failures, transport errors, performance degradation and SAP service restarts. · Troubleshoot IDoc, qRFC/tRFC, SOAP/REST/API, CPI iFlow, PI/PO message and certificate/endpoint issues. · Execute approved changes such as transport imports, profile updates, job schedule changes, interface reprocessing and controlled restarts. · Prepare change plans, rollback steps, pre/post checks and RCA inputs. Day-to-Day Technical Skills · Strong SAP Basis transactions: SM51, SM50/66, SM37, ST22, SM21, ST02, ST03N, STMS, RZ10/RZ11, SM59, ICM, SPAM/SAINT and SUM basics. · Integration operations using WE02/WE05/BD87, SM58, SMQ1/SMQ2, SRT_MONI, CPI message monitoring and PI/PO monitoring. · Understanding of SAP kernel directories, profiles, transport directory, RFC trust, gateway/ICM and certificates. · Ability to correlate SAP issues with OS, DB, network, firewall, DNS and proxy layers. · Shell scripting basics for automation, evidence capture and operational reporting. SAP RISE AMS Delivery Expectations · Mandatory working exposure to SAP RISE or SAP AMS managed services, including DEV, QAS, PRD and DR landscape support. · Work within SAP, customer, hyperscaler and AMS shared-responsibility boundaries with clear ownership and escalation discipline. · Support Incident, Service Request, Change, Problem, RCA, Knowledge and CDD activities using ITSM workflows. · Participate in 24x7 support, shift handover, controlled maintenance windows, CAB approvals and audit-ready documentation. · Coordinate across SAP Basis, HANA/DB, integration, infrastructure, security, network, customer business teams and SAP support. Upgrade / Patch / Deployment / Configuration Exposure · Execute/support SAP kernel upgrades, SAP Host Agent updates, support package activities and SUM-based maintenance. · Support SLES/RHEL patching and OS upgrade validation for SAP workloads with Basis pre/post checks. · Support CPI/PI/iFlow deployments, certificate rotation, endpoint migration and post-change interface smoke testing. · Support system refresh, client copy and post-copy automation coordination. · Validate system availability, jobs, RFCs, interfaces, transports and performance after maintenance. Monitoring, Management and ITSM Responsibilities · Use ServiceNow or equivalent ITSM to maintain ticket categorization, priority, affected service/CI, business impact, evidence and closure notes. · Support monitoring dashboards, health reports, SLA/OLA tracking, ticket ageing, backlog control and recurring alert review. · Follow escalation matrix, major incident bridge protocol, change governance and problem management process. · Maintain SOPs, runbooks, KB articles, handover notes and known-error database for repeated issues. · Identify automation and shift-left opportunities for repeated operational tasks and standard requests. Tools / Platforms · SAP GUI, SUM, Maintenance Planner, SAP for Me, SAP Notes, SAP Cloud ALM, SolMan, Focused Run, SAP Integration Suite, PI/PO, ServiceNow. Expected Deliverables and KPIs · Basis incident MTTR, change success rate, transport reliability, job stability, interface availability and post-maintenance defect rate. requirements: Would you like to build and evolve a new, scalable CTO function aligned with our transformation? Can you shape the future of technology strategy and drive real business impact? As the UK & Ireland Commercial CTO , you will play a pivotal role at the heart of the One Lenovo transformation. You will connect business strategy with technical excellence , bringing together sales, solutioning, and innovation to deliver meaningful outcomes for customers and drive sustainable growth. This is a completely new highly visible leadership role where your ability to influence, simplify, and execute will define success across the UK&I market. This is a commercial, customer-facing CTO role focused on driving growth through technology strategy, solutioning, and innovation, rather than product engineering or internal IT. The core role is growing solution-led revenue, increasing win rates & enabling cross-portfolio selling. In this role, you will expand influence beyond the direct team . What are your main responsibilities in this role? Define and lead the end-to-end commercial technology strategy across the UK & Ireland market Partner with Sales Leadership to translate customer demand into scalable, solution-led growth opportunities Lead a team of approximately 10+ people in technical sales, solutioning and pre-sales across business groups. Lead capability development by identifying skill gaps and enabling hiring, reskilling, and growth Act as a trusted advisor to senior customers and partners, engaging at C-level to shape transformation agendas Drive cross-business integration to deliver a unified, customer-centric value proposition Establish a strong operating rhythm across account planning, forecasting, and pipeline management Champion adoption of AI tools and digital solutions to improve productivity and sales effectiveness What are the role requirements? 10+ years of experience in the technology sector, including senior technology leadership role within a large IT vendor or similar organization. Strong commercial acumen with experience aligning technology strategy to revenue growth Deep knowledge of enterprise technology solutions and go-to-market models Proven leadership experience managing technical sales or solutioning teams Confident engaging and influencing senior stakeholders, including C-level executives You thrive in complex, matrix environments and can drive alignment across diverse teams Track record of leading transformation initiatives and driving change You are highly analytical, with the ability to translate data into actionable insights What success looks like in 12 – 18 months Establish a market-leading commercial CTO function that accelerates One Lenovo by driving solution-led growth, improving win rates, and embedding AI-enabled selling across the UK&I business. Create seamless alignment between sales, technology, and partners, enabling a consistent, customer-centric value proposition that unlocks cross-portfolio growth. What do we offer you? Opportunity to shape market-wide technology strategy and drive business transformation High visibility role with direct exposure to senior leadership Collaborative, international environment within a globally recognized brand Career growth opportunities across regions, functions, and business units Competitive compensation and performance-based rewards Access to cutting-edge technology, innovation, and AI-driven tools Ongoing professional and leadership development programs Flexible working approach supporting work-life balance If you are energized by leading through influence, passionate about bridging technology and business, and ready to make a lasting impact, this is your opportunity. Join us and help us shape the future of commercial technology across the UK & Ireland. We are open to candidates from a range of backgrounds, including adjacent industries and non-linear career paths, and encourage applications from individuals who bring diverse perspectives. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Sr. Hardware Engineer I (Band 9) What you'll do: The position is for a Hardware Development Engineer in the Lenovo Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), based in Bangalore, Karnataka. This is an exciting role in the ThinkSystem Server Development organization that has a strong focus to be the #1 trusted IT Partner in the Datacenter market. The hardware development engineer will be an integral member of a team responsible for developing innovative and complex server designs to meet customer and business needs. These organic designs provide exposure to the latest technologies from Lenovo and its development partners to meet market demands. The role also entails solving unique client challenges through the custom development of solutions. Required skills : Bachelor’s Degree in Electronics/Computer Engineering. 10+ years of in-depth experience in x86 architecture and hardware design. Server design experience is strongly preferred. Knowledge and design experience of DDR, PCIe, USB, Ethernet, SPI, I2C along with High-speed PCB design and signal integrity will be an essential requirement. Experience in EDA schematic design tools for PCB schematic creation, supporting physical/ Layout design activity, hardware bring-up and validation will be required. Responsible for translating business/customer requirements into platform architecture encompassing hardware, software, SoCs, and other components designed to support a variety of systems, solutions, and applications. Design, develop, and analyze overall platform architecture and partitioning between hardware and software to meet use cases and workloads in terms of performance, scalability, manageability, reliability, and security requirements Leads architectural governance and decisions related to product technology and platform tradeoffs as part of a cross-functional architecture decision group. Influences the shaping of future products/solutions by significantly contributing to the architecture and technology employed throughout the compute continuum Ability to triage and resolve complex hardware issues, working seamlessly with other engineers on problem investigation. Strong problem-solving skills with an ability to triage and resolve complex hardware issues, working seamlessly with other engineers on problem investigation will be essential. Strong written and verbal communication skills for a variety of tasks, potentially including customer engagements, architecture design/feedback, and executive updates. Demonstrated ability to work effectively in both a team environment and self-directed; passionate about learning new technologies and skills. Preferred skills: Master’s Degree Previous experience in FPGA code development and validation, DC Power circuit design, and/or PCIe bus design, simulation, and validation. Proficiency in Linux/Windows Operating systems. Experience with OS virtualization. Strong proficiency in shell scripting (BASH/Perl/Python and Windows Power Shell) Demonstrated successful hardware design execution on a project with engineering team members located in multiple geographies. Business acumen to engage with various functional teams required to take projects from concept to production. Additional

requirements: SDM (Service Delivery Management) Sr Manager ■ ポジション概要 SDM (Service Delivery Management) Sr Manager とはカスタマーデリバリー/プロジェクトの責任者であるSDMを統括するLenovoのPeople Managerポジションです。主にPCから成るデバイスに関するProfessional Service/Managed Serviceの 運用サービス全体の統括責任者 として、デリバリーのプロジェクトガバナンス、オペレーションエクセレンスを追求する職責となります。 具体的にはカスタマープロジェクトの支援やメンバーへのメンタリングにおけるリーダーシップを発揮いただき、サービス品質・顧客満足・SLA等の部門KPIを管理をします 。 また、日本Directorへのレポーティングだけでなく、海外拠点との連携し、Lenovoの サービスビジネス成長の中核を担うポジション です。 ■ 主な職務内容 1. サービス運用管理 サービスデリバリーの管理 プロジェクトガバナンス インシデント／リクエスト／エスカレーション対応 SLA/KPI（応答時間、解決率、顧客満足など） ２．チームマネジメント サービスチームの組織マネジメント パフォーマンス管理、育成、エンゲージメント向上 3. SMO (SDM) 機能サポート サービス管理プロセスの設計、標準化、運用改善 品質管理（Quality Assurance）および継続的改善 顧客および社内のガバナンス遵守の推進 4. エスカレーション・顧客対応 インシデント・エスカレーション時の指揮・意思決定 顧客およびシニアステークホルダーとのコミュニケーション エグゼクティブレベルへの報告 5. サプライチェーン・デリバリー連携 ワークオーダー（WO）プロセスの最適化 デバイスライフサイクル管理の運用改善 6. ビジネス・ファイナンス観点 サービス運用における収益性・コスト構造の理解と改善 プロジェクトファイナンスの視点での意思決定 リソース最適化、利益最大化に向けた施策推進 ■ 必須要件（Must） 下記のいずれかのご経験が当てはまる方（すべて当てはまる必要はございません） People Managerのご経験 ITサービスデリバリーまたはサービスマネジメント経験（5年以上） Service Desk / Help Deskの運用管理経験 マルチベンダー・アウトソーシング環境のマネジメント経験 サプライチェーンマネジメント経験 （ITハードウェア企業、精密機器メーカーや外資系ロジスティクス企業などの経験だと尚良い） 高いコミュニケーション能力（顧客・経営層対応含む） ピープルマネジメントスキル 日本語（ネイティブレベル）＋ビジネス英語 ソフトスキル： 高いインターパーソナルスキル スピード感をもってアクションできる実行力 アジャイルな環境への適応力 ■ 歓迎要件（Preferred） ハードウェアベンダーでのサービスデリバリーのご経験 製造業における生産計画のご経験 ロジスティクス・サプライチェーン関連のご経験 精密機器・IT機器のサポート／サービスご経験 Project Management / PMOご経験 ■ 求める人物像 Agileに実行、成果を生み出すリーダーシップ 多様なメンバー（サブコン含む）にビジョンを示し目標に向かって巻き込むリーダーシップ “枠組みを作り、スピーディに回す”実行型リーダー 状況に応じて柔軟にキャッチアップし、最適解を導ける人 事実・データに基づき改善アクションを設計できる人 日本ラインと海外のVirtualラインのマトリックスをマネージできる方 外資企業のスピード・変化に抵抗がない人 ■ Key Success Factors SLA / KPIの安定達成 顧客満足度の向上 エスカレーション削減・品質改善 サービス運用の標準化・効率化 チームの定着率・パフォーマンス向上 Additional

requirements: Lenovo is seeking a highly skilled Senior Solutions & Services Sales Specialist in our Software business to support the growth of its Lenovo Security Services offering, delivered in partnership with Microsoft, Absolute & SentinelOne. Lenovo Security Services combines device rooted security, expert led services, and an AI enhanced global SOC into one unified, end-to-end offering. Underpinned by the Lenovo Cyber Resiliency Framework, it protects every layer from device to cloud and AI while remaining vendor agnostic and globally compliant. This simplified single contract model reduces complexity, closes skills gaps, and helps customers achieve total cyber resiliency from pocket to cloud, delivered through Lenovo’s differentiated end to end Cyber Resiliency as a Service (CRaaS) approach. This role is a critical bridge between sales, customers, and technical delivery focused on designing, positioning, and validating Lenovo Security Services underpinned by the Cyber Resiliency Framework. You will work closely with account executives, channel partners, and customers to translate complex security requirements into scalable, outcome-driven solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep cybersecurity expertise with strong customer-facing skills and experience supporting both direct and channel-driven sales motions. requirements: Senior Marketing Specialist - Commercial Portfolio Are you passionate about marketing that delivers real business impact? Strong attention to detail while maintaining a strategic overview Ability to effectively organize work and manage multiple projects simultaneously Advanced English skills, both written and spoken University degree is an advantage, but not a requirement What We Offer The opportunity to work on the marketing of one of the most respected brands in the business world A high level of autonomy and trust – we are looking for a partner in brand development, not just someone to execute tasks A diverse and dynamic role. requirements: About the Role Lenovo Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG) – Product Assurance is seeking a Server Firmware Test Engineer to drive quality and validation for server firmware solutions. This role focuses on deep firmware validation (BMC / BIOS / UEFI), with strong emphasis on technical depth, problem-solving capability, and customer-facing engagement . Key Responsibilities Lead server firmware validation activities , including BMC, BIOS/UEFI, RAS, and Rack Manager domains Design and review test plans, test cases, and validation strategies based on product specifications and design documentation Execute and manage firmware validation cycles , ensuring coverage, quality, and timely delivery Perform defect analysis, root cause investigation, and debugging , including failure reproduction and resolution tracking Develop strong understanding of firmware-to-hardware interactions , and validate system-level behavior Drive test automation initiatives to improve efficiency and coverage Ensure high-quality deliverables aligned with customer expectations and product requirements Engage directly with internal and external stakeholders, including customers, to communicate technical findings and resolutions Core Competencies 1. Technical Depth & Problem Solving (Critical) Strong understanding of server firmware architecture (BMC / BIOS / UEFI) Ability to explain testing methodology, system behavior, and debugging approach Hands-on experience with: Firmware feature validation (e.g. sensors, Redfish/API, Web UI) Error injection, failure handling, and recovery scenarios Debugging tools and root-cause analysis Demonstrates structured, analytical approach to complex technical problem-solving 2. Analytical Thinking & Debug Capability Ability to go beyond execution and articulate the “why” behind validation scenarios Strong troubleshooting mindset with proven experience in issue isolation and resolution Comfortable handling deep technical discussions and scenarios 3. Stakeholder Management & Communication (Key Differentiator) Ability to engage with technically strong customers and stakeholders Communicates complex technical concepts in a clear and structured manner Demonstrates strong collaboration skills across cross-functional teams Acts as a credible technical representative in customer-facing situations 4. Adaptability & Team Fit Strong learning agility and ability to adapt to new technologies and environments Team-oriented mindset with effective communication and collaboration skills Demonstrates professionalism, ownership, and accountability Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or related field Minimum 3+ years of experience in server firmware validation/testing (BMC / BIOS / UEFI / RAS / Rack Manager) Experience in: Firmware validation and test case design Debugging and troubleshooting complex issues Test automation frameworks and implementation Experience in firmware development (BIOS/BMC) is a strong advantage Exposure to SIT (System Integration Testing) environments with firmware-related scope is a plus Strong written and verbal communication skills in English Preferred Profile (What We Value) Candidates with deep firmware validation exposure , not just surface-level testing SIT engineers with significant firmware-related scope (≥50%) BIOS/BMC developers with strong system-level understanding interested in validation Candidates who can demonstrate both technical depth AND customer-facing communication capability Additional

requirements: Lenovo is a global Fortune 500 company and a leader in providing innovative consumer, commercial and enterprise technology. Our portfolio of high-quality, secure products and services covers PCs, workstations, servers, storage, networking, smart TVs and a family of mobile products such as smartphones, tablets and apps. We have over 52,000 employees worldwide and customers in more than 160 countries. Lenovo’s worldwide headquarters is located in Hong Kong, with key development centers in Taipei, Beijing and Morrisville. With the acquisition of IBM System x, Lenovo expanded its position in the Enterprise Server, Storage, and Solutions space, providing large and competitive enterprise portfolios to meet customer needs in all segments. Lenovo Infrastructure Solutions Group Product Assurance is currently seeking a Server Firmware Test Engineer (CSP). Job Requirements: Mainly focus on server firmware engineering, including BMC, UEFI, RAS or Rack Manager. Identify, review design and NUDD documents, and deliver test cases, plans regarding product specifications. Conduct, monitor and manage test execution. Active defect reporting, analysis and tracking. Own the quality of assigned programs and ensure the outputs fulfill customers’ satisfaction. Identify in-house/industry test requirement documents and provide automation initiatives. Background ： Bachelor’s degree in CS, MIS, CE, EE or equivalent work experience. Ability to study open standards and new technologies. Ability to debug and resolve difficult technical problems and strong troubleshooting skills. Ability to determine the feasibility of projects in a fast-paced deadline driven environment. Ability to anticipate problems proactively and take appropriate actions to mitigate negative impact. Ability to work within a team environment as well as ability to work independently. Have proven experience defining server firmware test cases and tailoring test plans. Minimum 3 years’ experience with server firmware (BMC, UEFI, RAS or Rack Manager) function testing; experiences involving project management will be a plus. Familiar with test automation and implementation. Experienced in FW development would be the best. Must be self-motivated, flexible, and willing to work in a fast paced and dynamic environment. Fluent English speaking and writing skills. Additional

requirements: We are expanding our Digital Workplace (DWS) portfolio through a worldwide ServiceNow Practice and are seeking a Senior Services Sales Domain Specialist for ServiceNow to drive strategic growth across our customers through services and solutions. At this role You will get an opportunity to make a substantial impact in advancing this strategy and also accelerate personal growth. This is a senior, high-impact sales leadership role. You will lead large, complex ServiceNow transformation engagements, shape strategic consulting and implementation propositions, and expand Lenovo’s Services and Solutions’ footprint across selected accounts. You will operate as a trusted advisor to senior client stakeholders — helping organisations modernise enterprise workflows, optimise digital operations, and embed platform-led innovation across IT, HR, Customer Operations, Domain specific solutions and beyond leveraging ServiceNow. Beyond platform sales, you will position Lenovo’s broader ServiceNow Consulting and Implementation Services capabilities to deliver end-to-end transformation — from advisory and design through implementation, integration, optimisation, and managed services. This role is open in Germany, Switzerland or Austria. Key Responsibilities: Lead and own the full sales lifecycle for complex, multi-tower ServiceNow opportunities, from early-stage shaping through to closure. Work in close collaboration with Sales Teams from Solutions & Services Group and International Sales Organization to identify growth opportunities in existing and new accounts. Shape outcome-led value propositions integrating ServiceNow with Lenovo’s Consulting and Implementation capabilities. Engage C-suite and senior executives as a strategic transformation partner, influencing long-term digital roadmaps. Collaborate with consulting leaders, solution architects, engineering teams, alliance partners, and delivery executives to structure differentiated, high-value deals. Identify and drive cross-sell opportunities across Lenovo’s DWS services portfolio. Represent Lenovo at executive briefings, industry events, and partner forums to enhance brand presence and strategic positioning for our ServiceNow practice. What We’re Looking For: Significant experience in senior-level ServiceNow sales within enterprise IT services, consulting, or digital transformation environments. Proven track record of closing large, complex and transformational deals. Strong understanding of consulting-led engagements and engineering-driven delivery models. Deep knowledge of ServiceNow platform capabilities and enterprise workflow automation strategies. Demonstrated ability to engage and influence C-suite stakeholders and senior decision-makers. Strong commercial acumen, strategic thinking capability, and disciplined pipeline management. Willingness to travel nationally and internationally as required. What we can offer You: Employee Share Purchase Plan Employee Assistance Program, e.g., for health, legal & financial consultancy Pension Plan Meal Allowance / Lunch Vouchers Internal E-learning Development Platform Available for Employees Specialized Development Trainings (based on nomination process) Employees Groups (LGBT+, WILL, etc.) Opportunity to Join/Create Employees Groups (inclusivity, well-being, sports, volunteering, charity, etc.) Job Rad (Bike Leasing) Mobile phone + 3 Sim Cards for Mobile Working Women who drive Lenovo? #Women@Lenovo – Women at Lenovo . requirements: Lenovo is currently seeking an experienced Solutions and Services Executive. As a Solutions and Services Executive (SSE), you will be entrusted with nurturing and owning the SSG relationship with the customer across the lifecycle of a deal. The SSE will orchestrate deals that resonate with customer needs and bring the very best of the SSGs offerings. This is a client-facing sales role; industry sales experience, the ability to identify, consultative engagement, close net new business, and expertise are required. We are looking for an acquisition seller with a proven track record to take their career and our solutions and services business to the next level. The candidate will also evaluate customer opportunities as presented in, RFPs/RFQs or engagements, from the Services and Hardware Sales teams and use this information to manage the sales engagement process to ensure we meet the customer’s requirements. Lenovo solutions can include Services, Hardware, Software, Security, ISVs, and additional 3rd Party Services from Lenovo Business partners. This role will provide dedicated support across our Midwest Corporate R&D efforts. While the position is fully remote, we are seeking candidates located within this geographic region (Texas, Kansas, Missouri, Oklahoma, Iowa, Nebraska, Dakotas, Arkansas, or Louisiana) to ensure strong alignment with regional hiring needs, time zones, and stakeholder collaboration. Candidates should be comfortable working remotely while partnering closely with cross-functional teams across the Central Region. Strong knowledge of Digital Workplace Solutions and Device as a Service. Exceptional communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. Self-motivated, results-oriented, and able to work independently. Posses a proactive and driven approach to identify and pursue new sales opportunities Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, competitive sales environment. requirements: Solutions & Services Executive – Higher Education Focus Lenovo is seeking a highly experienced Solutions & Services Executive (SSE) to drive strategic growth across the Higher Education segment, with a focus on North East markets. In this client-facing role, you will own and expand the end-to-end SSG relationship across the full lifecycle of complex engagements, delivering solutions aligned to the evolving needs of universities, research institutions, and academic organizations. As a trusted advisor, you will bring a deep understanding of Higher Education environments including decentralized decision-making structures, research-driven IT requirements, funding models (grants, endowments, and departmental budgets), and campus-wide digital transformation initiatives. You will position Lenovo’s full portfolio across Services, Hardware, Software, Security, ISVs, and partner-led solutions to address institutional goals such as student success, hybrid learning, research enablement, and operational efficiency. This role requires a proven acquisition mindset, strong consultative selling capabilities, and the ability to navigate complex academic ecosystems while building long-term, strategic relationships. This role will provide dedicated support across our higher education efforts, spanning from Virginia to Maine. While the position is fully remote, we are seeking candidates located within this geographic region to ensure strong alignment with regional hiring needs, time zones, and stakeholder collaboration. Candidates should be comfortable working remotely while partnering closely with cross-functional teams across the Northeast corridor. experience: 2年及以上智能座舱、车机系统、车载生态相关产品经理工作经验，熟悉前装整车或后装车载市场业务模式，有完整的车载产品从0到1落地及迭代经验者优先。
requirements: 岗位职责： 车载产品经理主要负责： 1.智能座舱、车机系统及车载生态类产品的全生命周期管理，贴合车规标准与出行场景需求，统筹产品规划、需求定义、研发落地与迭代优化。 2.岗位聚焦前装整车及后装车载市场，洞察行业趋势、用户痛点与竞品动态，输出产品方案与迭代策略。 3.需协同研发、设计、测试、供应链及市场团队，把控产品安全性、稳定性与量产可行性，平衡体验、成本与商业化价值，持续优化车载产品能力，提升用户出行体验与产品市场竞争力。 任职资格：任职资格： 1. 学历专业 ：本科及以上学历，计算机、软件工程、车辆工程、工业设计、产品设计等相关专业优先。 2. 工作经验 ：2年及以上智能座舱、车机系统、车载生态相关产品经理工作经验，熟悉前装整车或后装车载市场业务模式，有完整的车载产品从0到1落地及迭代经验者优先。 3. 专业能力 ：熟悉智能座舱核心模块（仪表、车机、HUD、车载互联等）、车载主流技术架构及车规级产品开发标准；具备优秀的需求挖掘、方案设计、产品规划能力，能够独立输出PRD、产品方案、迭代规划等文档；了解车载行业竞品生态及商业化玩法。 4. 综合能力 ：具备极强的跨部门沟通协同、项目推进及问题解决能力，能够高效协调研发、供应链、市场等团队推进项目落地；拥有良好的逻辑思维、数据敏感度和市场洞察力，可精准捕捉用户痛点与行业趋势；具备成本意识、商业化思维和量产思维，能平衡产品体验、成本与商业价值。 Additional

requirements: About the Team Lenovo Workplace Services serves as the single point of contact (SPOC) for end-user managed services, delivering comprehensive IT support. The team operates in close alignment with customer IT environments, providing end-to-end support both locally and remotely. Role Overview The Software Asset Management (SAM) Intermediate Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, governance, and lifecycle management of software assets across the organization. This role supports cost optimization, licensing compliance, and effective utilization of software through strong operational processes and technical expertise. Key Responsibilities Manage software asset data to ensure accuracy, consistency, and governance across systems Oversee asset lifecycle activities, including software certification, updates, and asset movements Perform compatibility testing across standard corporate platforms (Windows, Linux, macOS) and mobile environments (Android/iOS) Execute validation checks to ensure application security and compliance standards are met Package and deploy applications using enterprise tools (e.g., SCCM, Intune), including virtualization and distribution Maintain records in CMDB and SAM tools, ensuring full traceability and documentation Collect and maintain evidence of testing and validation activities (logs, reports, screenshots) Manage certification requests, including backlog tracking and proactive updates Participate in regular customer meetings Key Skills & Competencies Strong communication skills, both verbal and written Solid technical knowledge across hardware and software support environments Ability to diagnose and resolve intermediate-level technical issues Working knowledge of ITSM tools (ServiceNow preferred) Experience with Microsoft MDT, SCCM, and Intune Strong understanding of internal processes and cross-team collaboration Ability to produce detailed technical documentation and reports Performance Metrics Asset management accuracy Response and resolution times Customer satisfaction Qualifications & Experience 2–5 years of experience in software asset management or a related IT operations role Degree in an IT-related discipline, or technical certifications with relevant experience ITIL Foundation certification Preferred Certifications (at least two): MD-101: Managing Modern Desktops SC-900: Security, Compliance, and Identity Fundamentals Administering System Center Configuration Manager (20703-1 / 55313AC) LPI Linux Essentials macOS Support Essentials Additional

requirements: XClarity One is Lenovo’s cloud-native systems management platform for operating data center infrastructure, including servers and storage devices, at enterprise scale. It unifies hardware, firmware, OS, and virtualization management into a single SaaS experience built for hybrid environments. The platform integrates deeply with VMware and Windows to enable consistent lifecycle management, observability, and automation. Engineers working on XClarity One collaborate closely with real data center equipment and have hands-on access to the Lenovo lab in Bucharest, Romania. This role offers direct exposure to production-grade hardware and the systems that run modern data centers. Requirements Experience developing backend applications with Node.js (Express.js) and JavaScript Good understanding of APIs and backend development principles Familiarity with: MongoDB (or other document-oriented databases) RabbitMQ (or similar messaging technologies) DragonflyDB , Redis , or caching concepts Docker Kubernetes (basic understanding) Understanding of microservices and distributed system fundamentals Ability to write clean, maintainable, and tested code Strong debugging and problem-solving skills Willingness to learn and improve through code reviews and collaboration AI-Assisted Development Comfortable using GitHub Copilot or similar AI coding assistants Ability to use AI tools to improve productivity while understanding and validating generated code Interest in modern AI-assisted development practices Nice to Have Go (Golang) Python Java Bash scripting Exposure to cloud platforms Familiarity with Grafana , Keycloak , or CI/CD pipelines

Key Responsibilities: Automation Framework Development: Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable test automation frameworks. Write reusable, maintainable, and efficient automated test scripts. Testing and Quality Assurance: Perform functional, regression, performance, and integration testing using automation tools. Identify, record, document, and track bugs, collaborating with developers to resolve them. Ensure comprehensive test coverage of all system components. Collaboration and Reporting: Work closely with product managers, developers, and other QA engineers to understand requirements and define testing strategies. Prepare detailed test plans, test cases, and test reports. Provide regular updates on testing progress, issues, and risks. Continuous Improvement: Stay updated with the latest trends and best practices in QA automation tools and techniques. Optimize and enhance the existing automation processes and frameworks. Required Skills and Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field. 2+ years of experience in software quality assurance with a focus on test automation. Proficiency in one or more programming/scripting languages (e.g., Java, Python, Pytest, JavaScript). Hands-on experience with automation tools such as Playwright, Selenium, Appium, TestNG, JUnit, or similar. Strong knowledge of software testing methodologies, tools, and processes. Experience with CI/CD tools (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab, Azure DevOps). Familiarity with API testing tools like Postman or RestAssured. Knowledge of performance and load testing tools (e.g., JMeter, LoadRunner) is a plus. Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and debugging skills. Strong communication skills and the ability to work in a team-oriented environment. Preferred Qualifications: Experience in cloud-based environments (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud). Knowledge of containerization tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes). Exposure to Agile/Scrum development methodologies. ISTQB or equivalent certification Additional

requirements: The Role: This is an early career sales hunter role designed for individuals with 0–2 years of experience in technology sales. We are not looking for order-takers; we are looking for future sales leaders. You will be on the front lines, identifying new business opportunities, generating leads, and learning how to sell complex IT solutions to commercial and FSI clients. If you are eager to learn, hungry to earn, and want to build a career in a fast-paced environment, this is your starting point. You will: Hunt for New Business: Proactively identify, prospect, and generate new sales opportunities within the commercial and FSI sector through cold calling, networking, and social selling. Support the Sales Cycle: Assist senior Sales Managers in qualifying leads, responding to tenders (RFPs/RFQs), and preparing sales presentations and proposals. Learn the Tech: Develop a foundational understanding of LPS’s core offerings (AI, Cloud, Data Practice, System Integration) to articulate value propositions to potential clients. Build Relationships: Shadow senior team members in client meetings to learn how to build strategic relationships with technical and business leaders. Drive Revenue: Work towards individual sales targets and KPIs (meetings booked, pipeline generated, deals closed) to contribute to the team’s revenue goals. Coordinate Internally: Collaborate with the Technology Solution Group and bid teams to ensure accurate submission of proposals and seamless handover of won deals. You bring: Experience: 0–2 years of experience in a sales, business development, or client-facing role (internships included). Experience in technology, IT services, or consulting is a plus, but a strong sales aptitude is essential. The "Hunter" Mindset: You are self-motivated, resilient, and competitive. You are not afraid of rejection and view “No” as the first step toward “Yes.” Drive: You are eager to learn (absorbing technical and sales knowledge) and hungry to earn (motivated by upside potential and commission structures). Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills in both English and Chinese (Cantonese and/or Mandarin) to effectively engage local clients. Business Acumen: A natural ability to influence and negotiate. You are organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple leads simultaneously. Education: University degree or equivalent experience in Business, Marketing, Information Technology, or a related field. Why Join Us? Accelerated Growth: Mentorship from top-tier sales leaders. Career Path: A clear trajectory from Specialist to Manager to Senior Sales Manager as you hit your milestones. Cutting-Edge Solutions: Sell what’s next — AI, Cloud, and Digital Transformation — to real-world enterprises. #LPS Additional

岗位职责： 1、负责区域产品与解决方案业务落地，根据销售目标，制定销售计划，打造标杆项目，完成销售任务； 2、根据公司业务发展需要，落实区域产品与解决方案业务的策略制定、拓展赋能，提升公司在区域、行业内产品与解决方案业务竞争力； 3、负责销售过程管理，制定各销售阶段的差异化竞争策略并通过实施推动订单获取； 4、基于公司转型战略，持续加强产品与解决方案类新业务模式探索，管理并发展内外部合作伙伴的良好关系，搭建并维护区域业务生态资源，提高合作满意度； 5、保持对竞争对手和市场的高度敏感，持续增强行业产品与解决方案Know-how，密切关注市场增长机会； 6、定期复盘，持续提高销售效率和生产力。 岗位要求： 1、具有七年以上IT销售经验，精通企业级客户销售模式，有客户资源，有服务销售背景优先。 2、优秀沟通协调能力、资源整合能力，以及推动协作部门部门解决问题的能力。 3、洞悉市场竞争环境，理解IT行业（教育、金融、政府、制造、电信及互联网）业务场景与需求，对软件、硬件和服务产品有很深的理解，能够深入洞察客户需求，梳理并引导客户需求。 4、具备区域业务规划能力，具备很强的业务拓展能力。 5、优秀的市场商机敏锐度和洞察力。 6、出色销售业绩表现：在销售高价值方案、赢取战略客户、赢取大单等方面表现突出。 7、能适应短途出差。 Additional

requirements: Job Description – Solutions & Services Sales Executive (SSE), Thailand Role Summary We are seeking a highly experienced and result-driven Solutions & Services Sales Executive (SSE) to accelerate Lenovo’s Services & Solutions Group (SSG) growth. This strategic role focuses on expanding Lenovo’s presence across Hybrid Cloud Solutions, Infrastructure-as-a-Service, Managed Services, Software & Cybersecurity Services. Key Responsibilities Sales & Account Growth - Develop and execute strategies to increase SSG penetration in Thailand key accounts. - Identify customer needs and position tailored solutions across Hybrid Cloud Solutions, Infrastructure-as-a-Service/Truscale and Managed Services. - Build and maintain detailed account plans to strengthen customer relationships and deliver revenue growth. - Lead land‑and‑expand growth discussions to broaden services within existing customers and acquire new logos - Drive new funnel creation, monitor win rates, and achieve quarterly quota for services and solutions. Solution Leadership & Collaboration - Collaborate with hardware sales teams, solution consultants, and cross‑functional stakeholders to deliver integrated solutions. - Lead the end‑to‑end sales lifecycle, including opportunity identification, pursuit management, contracting, and closure. - Conduct enablement sessions to equip sales teams with updated knowledge of Lenovo’s service portfolio. - Present structured weekly, monthly, and quarterly business updates to leadership. Market Insights & Competitive Strategy - Conduct competitive analysis and share insights to refine go‑to‑market strategies. - Monitor trends across Hybrid Cloud Solutions, Infrastructure-as-a-Service/Truscale and Managed Services service models. Skills & Experience Must‑Have - 12+ years of experience in IT, Services, or solutions sales with proven success in selling Infrastructure Services. - Excellent analytical, financial modeling, communication, and executive‑level presentation capabilities - Strong network with C‑level executives and proven experience leading complex solution sales. - Deep understanding of Hybrid Cloud Solutions, Infrastructure-as-a-Service/Truscale, Managed Services, and vertical solutions. - Experience with as-a-Service, managed services, cloud transformation and cybersecurity solutions. - Strong cross‑functional influence within a matrix organization. - Excellent analytical, communication, and executive presentation skills. Additional

requirements: We are seeking a motivated and customer-focused Solutions and Services Sales Executive to drive sales of Professional Services across both Lenovo Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG) and Intelligent Devices Group (IDG). At this role You will be responsible for identifying, developing, and closing opportunities for consulting, deployment, integration, and lifecycle management services that support our infrastructure and end-user environments. You will report to our Sr. Solutions & Services Executive Manager France. To be successful in this role you will use both your technical understanding and commercial acumen to position value-added services that help our clients modernize, optimize, and manage their IT environments end to end. Your Responsibilities are to: Drive strategic growth of DaaS and IaaS through the identification, development, and closure of high-value Channel opportunities across local and international markets. Build and sustain executive-level relationships with key Channel Partners and customer stakeholders to influence long-term business outcomes. Champion Lenovo’s “as-a-Service” portfolio — cloud infrastructure, managed services, and endpoint solutions — ensuring strong market adoption and partner engagement. Oversee accurate pipeline governance and forecasting through Microsoft Dynamics, enabling data-driven business decisions. Lead the end-to-end services sales lifecycle, ensuring seamless handover to delivery teams and maintaining executive visibility throughout execution. Align closely with account teams to attach high-value professional services to hardware and software initiatives, increasing overall deal value and customer stickiness. Partner with solution architects to shape complex, customized service offerings covering data center, PC deployment, and IT transformation programs; Own proposal development, SOW definition, and commercial structuring, articulating clear technical and business value propositions. Consistently exceed revenue, margin, and customer satisfaction goals through disciplined execution and strong partner alignment. Maintain a forward-looking perspective on Lenovo’s product, service, and business model innovations and translate them into scalable, market-ready service solutions. What you bring: 5 years of experience in services or solutions sales within IT/data center/end-user computing. Proven success selling professional or managed services (consulting, deployment, integration); Experience with enterprise and public sector customers. Strong consultative selling, negotiation, and relationship-building skills. Ability to translate customer challenges into technical/business solutions; excellent communication. Bachelor’s degree in business, IT, Engineering (preferred); industry certifications advantageous. Familiarity with IT lifecycle services, hybrid cloud, virtualization, and ROI/TCO-based selling What We can offer You: An international team with a high focus on Gender Diversity.​ Employee Assistance Program, e.g., for psychological, legal & financial consultancy​ You are joining a company that prioritizes sustainable solutions like CO2 Offset, Asset Recovery Services, and the Lenovo Certified Refurbished portfolio.​ Access to training for personal development. Internal E-learning Development Platform Available for Employees ​ Mentorship program ...and many more! We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional
